---

### Phase KD Execution Order

```
KD-A (POW filter — frontend only, zero migration risk, immediate UX win)
  → KD-B (Guide cards + FAB — frontend only, zero risk)
    → KD-C (Overview hero — frontend only, high stakeholder value)
      → KD-G (canEditTab extension — fast composable change, no risk)
        → KD-D (Personnel categories — migration + backend + frontend)
          → KD-E (Project Diary — migration + backend + frontend)
            → KD-F (Analytics tab — depends on useCoiPowItems composable)
```

**KD-A, KD-B, KD-C, KD-G** — can be executed in a single session (all frontend-only).
**KD-D** — requires dedicated migration session.
**KD-E** — requires dedicated migration + full backend session.
**KD-F** — can be done after KD-A (depends on `useCoiPowItems` composable from KD-A refactor).


## Phase KE — COI Workflow Consolidation, View/Edit Separation, Personnel Governance Expansion, Analytics Restoration

> **Status:** PHASE 2 PLAN — pending operator authorization for Phase 3
> **Research Reference:** Section 2.144 (AA, A.1, A.2, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I)
> **Last Updated:** 2026-05-14 (Phase KT complete � public COI detail page enhanced with narrative fields, strategic profile, team section, and POW endpoint)
> **Continuity:** Extends Phases KB, KC, KD. No regression of any verified KC/KD sub-phase.

---

### Phase KE Governance Directives

| ID | Directive |
|---|---|
| KE-G1 | No implementation until Phase 3 explicitly authorized |
| KE-G2 | Sub-phases execute in priority order KE-F → KE-AA → KE-G → KE-D → KE-A.1 → KE-B → KE-A.2 → KE-C → KE-E |
| KE-G3 | All Create/Edit forms MUST mount the same shared `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` component |
| KE-G4 | View page MUST NOT mount any upload, file dialog, or mutation control after KE-F lands |
| KE-G5 | Tab `value` strings on Detail page MUST NOT change (bookmark stability) |
| KE-G6 | Edit page tab consolidation MUST emit anchor migration logic (old `?tab=contract` redirects to `?tab=basic`) |
| KE-G7 | NO new JWT roles in Phase KE — Option C3 governance via personnel_category modifier only |
| KE-G8 | All shared components live in `pmo-frontend/components/coi/`; shared composables in `pmo-frontend/composables/` |
| KE-G9 | Backend remains single source of truth for authorization; frontend `canEditTab()`/`canTabAction()` are presentational mirrors |
| KE-G10 | Analytics empty-state must distinguish "no data yet" from "data load failed"; both must include actionable next-step text |
| KE-G11 | The `Edit Project Details` button is the SOLE inline edit affordance on the Detail page after KE-F |
| KE-G12 | Migration files (if any) follow MikroORM TS class convention; logged in `MIGRATION_LOG.md` |
| KE-G13 | Phase KE is frontend-heavy; backend changes confined to KE-C (controller @Roles audit) and KE-E (analytics aggregation endpoints if needed) |

---

### Phase KE Sub-Phase Overview

| Sub-Phase | Area | Priority | Migration | Backend |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| KE-F | View/Edit separation on Detail page | 1 | no | no |
| KE-AA | Basic + Profile consolidation (shared form) | 2 | no | no (DTO already supports) |
| KE-G | Shared component extraction (sync infra) | 3 | no | no |
| KE-D | Edit page tab consolidation (contract→basic, progress→timeline) | 4 | no | no |
| KE-A.1 | Mirror diary to Edit page Timeline tab | 5 | no | no |
| KE-B | Overview tab executive enrichment | 6 | no | no |
| KE-A.2 | Personnel tab UX polish | 7 | no | no |
| KE-C | Authorization 6-role mapping via Option C3 | 8 | no | yes (logging endpoint scope) |
| KE-E | Analytics expansion (4→8 charts + Document/Personnel) | 9 | no | yes (aggregation reads) |

---

### KE-F — View vs Edit Separation (Detail Page)

**Goal:** Detail page becomes read-only preview. All inline mutations and uploads removed; single canonical "Edit Project Details" button is the only edit entry point.

**Steps:**

1. **Top-bar button rename:** Change "Edit Project" (line 560) → "Edit Project Details". Visibility unchanged (`v-if="canEditCurrentProject"`).
2. **Remove pencil-icon edit jumps:** Delete all 6+ `editSection()` `v-btn` elements on Overview/Timeline/Team/Documents/Gallery tabs. Keep `editSection()` function definition (used by remaining text-link affordances).
3. **Replace with text-link affordances:** At top of each editable section, add a text link:
   ```html
   <div class="text-caption text-grey mb-2">
     <a v-if="canEditCurrentProject"
        class="text-primary cursor-pointer"
        @click="editSection('overview')">
       Edit this section ↗
     </a>
   </div>
   ```
4. **Remove Gallery upload UI:** Delete `<v-dialog v-model="uploadDialog">` block + `Upload Photo` button on Detail page Gallery tab. Verify equivalent UI exists on Edit page Gallery tab; if missing, add to Edit before removing.
5. **Remove Documents upload UI:** Delete `<v-dialog v-model="docFileDialog">` and `<v-dialog v-model="driveLinkDialog">` blocks + their trigger buttons on Detail page Documents tab. Verify Edit page has the equivalent.
6. **Keep workflow buttons:** Submit for Review / Withdraw / Publish / Reject buttons remain on Detail page (state transitions, not data mutations).
7. **Add `useCoiAccess.canEditAnyTab()`:**
   ```typescript
   const canEditAnyTab = computed(() =>
     EDITABLE_TABS.some(t => canEditTab(t).value),
   )
   ```
   Return from composable. Hide top-bar Edit button when `!canEditAnyTab.value`.

**Files:**
- `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` (template + minor script edits)
- `composables/useCoiAccess.ts` (add `canEditAnyTab`)

**Verification:**
- KE-V1: Detail page contains zero `<v-file-input>`, zero `uploadFile`, zero `docFileDialog v-model`
- KE-V2: Detail page Gallery tab has no "Upload Photo" button
- KE-V3: Detail page Documents tab has no upload or external-link buttons
- KE-V4: Pencil-icon edit jumps replaced with "Edit this section ↗" text links
- KE-V5: Top-bar button reads "Edit Project Details" and hides for users with no editable tab

---

### KE-AA — Basic Info + Project Profile Consolidation (Shared Form)

**Goal:** Single `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` component owns ALL basic + profile fields. Mounted by both `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` and `pages/coi/new.vue`.

**Steps:**

1. **Create `pmo-frontend/types/coiFormState.ts`:**
   ```typescript
   export interface BasicInfoFormState {
     // Identity
     project_code: string
     title: string
     description: string
     summary: string
     // Location
     campus: string
     latitude: number | null
     longitude: number | null
     // Contract & Funding (merged from `contract` tab)
     contract_number: string
     contract_amount: number | null
     contractor_id: string | null
     funding_source_id: string
     original_contract_duration: string
     // Implementation
     building_type: string
     floor_area: number | null
     number_of_floors: number | null
     beneficiaries: number | null
     facilities: string
     scope: string
     objectives: string[]
     key_features: string[]
     // Strategic Alignment (KC-C)
     strategic_alignment: string
     output_indicators: string[]
     outcome_indicators: string[]
     implementing_agency: string
     // Status
     status: string
     project_status_category: string
     subcategory_id: string | null
     ideal_infrastructure_image: string
     // Status updates / readiness / signatories (JSONB arrays)
     status_updates: Array<{ date: string; text: string }>
     readiness_documents: Array<{ type: string; status: string; remarks: string }>
     signatories: Array<{ name: string; position: string; date: string }>
   }
   ```

2. **Create `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiBasicInfoForm.vue`:**
   - `defineModel<BasicInfoFormState>()` for two-way binding
   - Internal layout: `v-expansion-panels multiple` with sections:
     - Identity (project_code, title, description, summary) — default open
     - Location (campus, latitude, longitude)
     - Contract & Funding (contract_number, contract_amount, contractor_id, funding_source_id, original_contract_duration)
     - Implementation Details (building_type, floor_area, number_of_floors, beneficiaries, facilities, scope, objectives[], key_features[])
     - Strategic Alignment & Indicators (strategic_alignment, output_indicators[], outcome_indicators[], implementing_agency)
     - Status & Lifecycle (status, project_status_category, subcategory_id, ideal_infrastructure_image)
     - Status Updates (status_updates[] array editor)
     - Readiness Documents (readiness_documents[] array editor)
     - Signatories (signatories[] array editor)
   - Each array section uses Add/Remove row pattern from existing edit-[id].vue
   - Image upload field for `ideal_infrastructure_image` (existing UploadsService)

3. **Mount in Edit page:**
   - Replace `<v-window-item value="basic">` content with `<CiBasicInfoForm v-model="basicForm" />`
   - Replace `<v-window-item value="profile">` content with empty placeholder OR delete tab entirely (after KE-D)
   - On save, spread `basicForm` into the existing PATCH payload

4. **Mount in Create page:**
   - Replace `<v-window-item value="basic">` content with `<CiBasicInfoForm v-model="basicForm" />`
   - On save, spread `basicForm` into the POST payload

5. **Hydrate on Edit page load:**
   - Map `adaptProjectDetail` output → `BasicInfoFormState` shape
   - Initialize `basicForm.value` from project data

**Files:**
- `types/coiFormState.ts` (new)
- `components/coi/CiBasicInfoForm.vue` (new ~600 lines)
- `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` (mount component, remove inline basic+profile fields)
- `pages/coi/new.vue` (mount component, remove inline basic fields)

**Verification:**
- KE-V6: Create page Basic Info tab renders all 9 expansion-panel sections
- KE-V7: Create page can submit project with strategic_alignment populated; backend persists
- KE-V8: Edit page Basic Info tab pre-populates strategic_alignment from existing project
- KE-V9: Edit page no longer has separate Profile tab (or Profile tab is empty placeholder pending KE-D)
- KE-V10: Both pages show identical field layout, validation, and labels

---

### KE-G — Shared Component Extraction (Cross-Flow Sync Infrastructure)

**Goal:** Extract reusable form/editor components and composables to enforce Create+Edit+Detail parity.

**Steps:**

1. **Extract `CiPersonnelEditor.vue`:**
   - Encapsulates the per-member metadata repeater (KD-D personnel_category select + role/department/phone)
   - Mounted by Edit page Personnel tab (replaces inline UI at line 2029-2080) AND Create page Personnel tab (currently lacks the per-member editor)
   - `defineModel<{ assigned_user_ids: string[]; assignmentMetadata: Record<string, AssignmentMeta> }>()`

2. **Extract `CiTimelineSection.vue`:**
   - Encapsulates milestones list (existing `existingMilestones` rendering)
   - Composes `<CiProjectDiary />` in 2-column layout
   - Mounted by Detail page (replaces current inline rendering) AND Edit page Timeline tab (KE-A.1 mounts it)

3. **Create `useCoiBasicForm.ts` composable:**
   - Encapsulates form-state shape, validation rules, and adapter→form hydration
   - Returns `{ form, validation, hydrate, toPayload }`

4. **Create `tests/manual/ke_parity_checklist.md`:**
   - One-page checklist: for each shared component, list the verification steps for Create/Edit/Detail mounts
   - Operator runs after each KE sub-phase merge

**Files:**
- `components/coi/CiPersonnelEditor.vue` (new)
- `components/coi/CiTimelineSection.vue` (new)

### KE-D — Edit Page Tab Consolidation

**Goal:** Reduce Edit page tab count from 10 to 7 by absorbing redundant tabs into related parents.

**Steps:**

1. **Absorb `contract` into `basic`:**
   - All 4 `contract` tab fields (contract_number, contract_amount, contractor_id, original_contract_duration) already in `BasicInfoFormState` (KE-AA Step 1)
   - Delete `<v-tab value="contract">` and `<v-window-item value="contract">` from edit-[id].vue
   - Add anchor redirect: if `route.query.tab === 'contract'`, set `activeTab = 'basic'`

2. **Absorb `progress` into `timeline`:**
   - Move 4 progress fields (physical_progress, target_physical_progress, financial_progress, target_financial_progress) into a small header card above milestones list in Timeline tab
   - Delete `<v-tab value="progress">` and `<v-window-item value="progress">`
   - Anchor redirect for `?tab=progress` → `?tab=timeline`

3. **Absorb `profile` into `basic` (if not already done by KE-AA):**
   - Delete `<v-tab value="profile">` and `<v-window-item value="profile">`
   - Anchor redirect for `?tab=profile` → `?tab=basic`

4. **Rename `schedule` → `Dates & Duration`:**
   - Tab label only; `value="schedule"` unchanged for URL stability

5. **Final tab inventory (7 tabs):** `basic / schedule / timeline / pow / financial / personnel / documents`

**Files:**
- `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` (template edits + anchor redirect logic in mounted)

### KE-A.2 — Personnel Tab UX Polish

**Goal:** Improve visual hierarchy and operational clarity per Section 2.144-A.2.

**Steps:**

1. **Group header tonal styling:**
   - In `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` Team tab, change `<v-card variant="outlined" class="mb-3 pa-2">` to:
     ```html
     <v-card variant="tonal" :color="group.color" class="mb-3">
       <v-card-title class="text-subtitle-2 d-flex align-center ga-2 py-2">
         <v-icon :icon="group.icon" size="small" />
         <span>{{ group.label }}</span>
         <v-chip size="x-small" variant="elevated">{{ group.users.length }}</v-chip>
       </v-card-title>
       <v-card-text class="bg-surface pa-2">
         …
       </v-card-text>
     </v-card>
     ```

2. **Row enhancement with avatar and chips:**
   - Add `<v-avatar size="32" color="primary" variant="tonal"><span class="text-caption">{{ initials(user.name) }}</span></v-avatar>` to `#prepend`
   - Replace existing list-item-title with structured layout: name (font-weight-bold) + role chip prominent
   - Add contact action chips when phone/email present

3. **Empty-state per group:**
   - Render every defined PERSONNEL_GROUPS card even when `users.length === 0`
   - Empty card body: "No personnel assigned to this category." + `Add via Personnel tab` text link (gated by `canEditTab('team')`)

4. **`initials()` helper in `<script setup>`:**
   ```typescript
   function initials(name: string): string {
     const parts = name.trim().split(/\s+/)
     return ((parts[0]?.[0] || '') + (parts[parts.length - 1]?.[0] || '')).toUpperCase()
   }
   ```

**Files:**
- `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` (Team tab template + initials helper)

### KE-C — Authorization 6-Role Mapping (Option C3 — No New JWT Roles)

**Goal:** Surface the 6 governance roles using existing JWT roles + personnel_category modifier.

**Steps:**

1. **Extend `useCoiAccess.ts` with `canTabAction(tab, action)`:**
   ```typescript
   type TabAction = 'read' | 'edit' | 'submit' | 'approve' | 'audit'
   const TAB_ACTION_MATRIX: Record<string, Record<TabAction, string[]>> = {
     basic:      { read: ['Admin','Staff','Viewer','Auditor'], edit: ['Admin','Staff'], submit: ['Admin','Staff'], approve: ['Admin'], audit: ['Auditor','Admin'] },
     pow:        { read: ['Admin','Staff','Viewer','Auditor'], edit: ['Admin','Staff'], submit: ['Admin','Staff'], approve: ['Admin'], audit: ['Auditor','Admin'] },
     // … per tab
   }

   function canTabAction(tab: string, action: TabAction): ComputedRef<boolean> {
     return computed(() => {
       const allowed = TAB_ACTION_MATRIX[tab]?.[action] || []
       const userRole = authStore.user?.role
       if (!userRole) return false
       if (!allowed.includes(userRole)) return false
       // personnel_category modifier: Staff requires assignment with appropriate category
       if (userRole === 'Staff' && action === 'edit') {
         return isOwnerOrAssigned.value
       }
       return true
     })
   }
   ```

2. **Audit `construction-projects.controller.ts` `@Roles()` lists:**
   - For each of 45 endpoints, verify `@Roles()` matches the matrix
   - Add OVERSIGHT-relevant Auditor reads where missing
   - Document the canonical matrix in MIGRATION_LOG.md

3. **New activity log endpoint scope:**
   - Add `@Get('/:id/activity-log') @Roles('Admin','Auditor')` to expose project-level audit trail to Auditor role explicitly
   - Service method already exists from KD-D (logs are already written); only the read endpoint is new

4. **Frontend role-tag UI:**
   - In Personnel tab card, when a user has personnel_category=OVERSIGHT, display "Oversight Role" badge (chip color=warning)
   - Same for MONITORING / APPROVAL (if approval category added — defer)

**Files:**
- `composables/useCoiAccess.ts` (extend)
- `construction-projects.controller.ts` (audit + new endpoint)
- `construction-projects.service.ts` (activity-log read method if not present)
- `pmo-backend/src/database/MIGRATION_LOG.md` (document matrix)

**Verification:**
- KE-V30: `useCoiAccess.canTabAction('basic', 'edit')` returns true for Admin, Staff (if assigned), false for Viewer/Auditor
- KE-V31: GET `/construction-projects/:id/activity-log` returns 200 for Auditor; 403 for Viewer
- KE-V32: Personnel card shows "Oversight" badge for OVERSIGHT-category users

### KE-E — Analytics Restoration & Expansion (4 → 8 Charts)

**Goal:** Add 4 new analytics surfaces to `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` per Section 2.144-E.

**Steps:**

1. **KE-E-1 — Empty-state diagnostic banner:**
   - At top of analytics tab, render a `v-alert` when ALL 4 base charts are empty:
     "Charts are empty because this project has no POW items, financial entries, or milestones yet. Add data via the Edit page."
   - When SOME data is present, no banner

2. **KE-E-2 — Financial utilization gauge per FY:**
   - Add `vue3-apexcharts type="radialBar"` per fiscal year
   - Series: utilization rate (0-100)
   - Loops through `project.financials`; renders one gauge per FY in a v-row

3. **KE-E-3 — Milestone slippage histogram:**
   - For each milestone with both `targetCompletionDate` and `actualCompletionDate` (or `updatedAt`), compute days delta
   - Bin into buckets: "On time", "1-7 days late", "8-30 days late", "30+ days late"
   - Render as bar chart

4. **KE-E-4 — Document compliance donut:**
   - Source: `GET /:id/document-checklist` (KB-E)
   - Bucket by `submission_status`: NOT_SUBMITTED / SUBMITTED / UNDER_REVIEW / APPROVED / REJECTED
   - Render as donut with KPI summary above (X of Y required documents approved)

5. **KE-E-5 — Personnel activity chart:**
   - New backend endpoint: `GET /:id/personnel-analytics` returns `[{ user_id, name, category, last_action_at, total_actions }]`
   - Source: JOIN `record_assignments` with aggregated `activity_logs` filtered by `entity_id`
   - Render as bar chart (Y = user name, X = action count) + last-active timestamp in tooltip

**Files:**
- `components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` (extend with 4 new charts + diagnostic banner)
- `construction-projects.service.ts` (new `findPersonnelAnalytics(projectId)` method)
- `construction-projects.controller.ts` (new `@Get('/:id/personnel-analytics')` route)

**Verification:**
- KE-V33: Empty-state banner shows when project has zero POW/financial/milestone
- KE-V34: Utilization gauge per FY renders correctly
- KE-V35: Slippage histogram bins milestones by delay
- KE-V36: Document compliance donut sums to 100%
- KE-V37: Personnel activity chart shows action count per assigned user

### Phase KE Execution Order

```
KE-F (View/Edit separation — frontend, zero risk)
  → KE-AA (Basic+Profile shared form — frontend)
    → KE-G (Shared component extraction — frontend infra)
      → KE-D (Edit page tab consolidation — frontend; depends on KE-AA)
        → KE-A.1 (Edit Timeline diary parity — frontend; depends on KE-G)
          → KE-B (Overview enrichment — frontend; depends on KE-AA hydration)
            → KE-A.2 (Personnel UX polish — frontend, isolated)
              → KE-C (Authorization 6-role via Option C3 — backend audit + composable extension)
                → KE-E (Analytics expansion — frontend + 1 new backend endpoint)
```

**Single session feasibility:** KE-F + KE-AA + KE-G + KE-D + KE-A.1 + KE-A.2 — frontend-only, no migration. Estimated ~3-4 hours focused work.

**Separate session:** KE-B + KE-C + KE-E — KE-B is high-detail polish, KE-C is backend audit, KE-E adds backend endpoint. Better isolated.

### Phase KE Governance Directives Summary

- All cross-page sync via shared components (`CiBasicInfoForm`, `CiPersonnelEditor`, `CiTimelineSection`)
- All shared form-state via `useCoiBasicForm` composable + `BasicInfoFormState` interface
- Detail page is read-only after KE-F; sole edit affordance is the top-bar button
- No new JWT roles — Option C3 (personnel_category modifier) only
- Tab `value` strings on Detail page never change; Edit page consolidations include anchor redirects
- Analytics expansion uses ApexCharts (no new dependencies); aggregation via raw SQL when crossing tables
- Verification matrix (KE-V1 through KE-V37) must be executed by operator before declaring phase complete

### KF-AA — CiBasicInfoForm 4-Section Refactor

**Objective:** Reduce cognitive overload by restructuring 9 expansion panels into 1 always-visible Core card + 3 expansion panels.

**Target structure:**

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  v-card: "Core Information"  (always visible)        │
│  Fields: project_code, title, campus, status,        │
│    project_status_category, funding_source_id,       │
│    contract_amount, contractor_id                    │
│  (8 fields — required and semi-required)             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

v-expansion-panels (multiple, 3 panels):

  [PANEL 0 — open by default] Project Details
    Fields: description, summary, building_type,
    floor_area, number_of_floors, beneficiaries,
    latitude, longitude, project_engineer,
    project_manager, implementing_agency,
    original_contract_duration, ideal_infrastructure_image
    (13 fields — secondary identity and operational info)

  [PANEL 1 — collapsed] Strategic Context
    Fields: strategic_alignment, output_indicators,
    outcome_indicators, objectives, key_features,
    scope, facilities
    (7 fields — for strategy/planning users)

  [PANEL 2 — collapsed] Administrative Records
    Sub-sections: Status Updates (repeater),
    Readiness Documents (repeater),
    Signatories (repeater)
    Chip badges on panel title: count of each repeater
```

**Steps:**

1. **Rewrite `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` template section**
   - Remove all 9 `<v-expansion-panel>` blocks.
   - Add a `<v-card class="mb-4">` as the Core Information section containing the 8 required/semi-required fields from former panels 0 and 3.
   - Add `<v-expansion-panels multiple v-model="openPanels">` with 3 panels as described above.
   - `openPanels = ref([0])` — only Project Details is open by default.
   - Move Leadership & Agency fields (project_engineer, project_manager, implementing_agency) from the former Panel 4 into the Project Details expansion panel.
   - Move narrative fields (objectives, key_features, scope, facilities) from former Panel 1 into Strategic Context.
   - Keep JSONB repeater UI (add/remove buttons, row inputs) as-is in Panel 2 "Administrative Records".
   - Panel title for each expansion: show icon, label, and (for Panel 2) chip badges for counts.

2. **Add chip badges to Administrative Records panel title**
   - Show `statusUpdateRows.length + readinessDocRows.length + signatoryRows.length` as a single "{{ totalAdminRecords }} records" chip (or 3 separate chips per sub-type).

3. **Add section sub-headers within Project Details panel**
   - Use `<div class="text-overline text-medium-emphasis mt-2 mb-1">Building & Location</div>` and `<v-divider class="mb-3" />` to break the 13-field panel into logical groups without adding more expansion levels.

4. **No changes to `<script setup>` section** — all models, props, helpers remain identical.

**Verification:**
- KF-AA-V1: Basic Info tab renders 1 visible Core card + 3 expansion panels (not 9).
- KF-AA-V2: Core card shows project_code, title, campus, status, project_status_category, funding_source_id, contract_amount, contractor_id.
- KF-AA-V3: Project Details panel is open by default; Strategic Context and Administrative Records are closed.
- KF-AA-V4: All 9 former panels' fields still exist in the new structure (none omitted).
- KF-AA-V5: Create page and Edit page both render the new 4-section layout.
- KF-AA-V6: Form submit in new.vue sends all fields (no regression in handleSubmit payload).

---

### KF-C — Personnel Tab Visual Redesign

**Objective:** Redesign `edit-[id].vue` Personnel tab to use category-colored tonal cards, prominent category selection, and a category summary.

**Steps:**

1. **Extract shared PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP to utils/coiFormState.ts**
   - Add exported constant:
     ```typescript
     export const PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP: Record<string, { label: string; icon: string; color: string }> = {
       IMPLEMENTING_TEAM:    { label: 'Implementing Team',     icon: 'mdi-hard-hat',      color: 'blue'   },
       MONITORING_OVERSIGHT: { label: 'Monitoring / Oversight', icon: 'mdi-eye-check',     color: 'orange' },
       RESTRICTED_EDITOR:    { label: 'Restricted Editor',     icon: 'mdi-pencil-lock',   color: 'purple' },
       OVERSIGHT:            { label: 'Oversight',             icon: 'mdi-shield-check',  color: 'green'  },
       READ_ONLY:            { label: 'Read-Only',             icon: 'mdi-eye-outline',   color: 'grey'   },
     }
     ```
   - Update `detail-[id].vue` to import and use `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP` from `coiFormState.ts` (replacing its inline `PERSONNEL_GROUPS` constant or aligning its values).

2. **Refactor Personnel tab in `edit-[id].vue` (lines 1863–1984)**

   a. **Rename and restructure Card 1 → "Project Leadership" section:**
      - Change card title from "Personnel" to "Project Leadership".
      - Add `implementing_agency` text field to this card (currently in CiBasicInfoForm Panel 4, but the Personnel tab in edit also needs this for clarity; or remove from Personnel tab if Basic Info is the single source).
      - Keep `project_engineer` and `project_manager` fields.

   b. **Refactor per-assignment repeater (Card 2 "Assigned Staff"):**
      - Change card title from "Assigned Staff" to "Assignment Roster".
      - Add a category summary row above the repeater: computed chips `{ category: X, count: N }` for each unique category in `assignmentMetadata`.
      - In each assignment card (`v-for="uid in form.assigned_user_ids"`):
        - Remove `style="background-color: rgb(var(--v-theme-surface-variant))"`.
        - Replace with `<v-card variant="tonal" :color="categoryColor(uid)" class="mb-3 pa-3 rounded">` where `categoryColor(uid)` returns `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP[assignmentMetadata[uid]?.personnelCategory]?.color || 'grey'`.
        - Move `Personnel Category` select to **first** position (before Role/Position).
        - Add a colored icon badge next to the user's name showing the category icon.
      - Add `personnelCategoryOptions` color fields aligned with `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP`.

   c. **Helper function `categoryColor(uid: string): string`**:
      ```typescript
      function categoryColor(uid: string): string {
        const cat = assignmentMetadata[uid]?.personnelCategory
        return PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP[cat]?.color || 'grey'
      }
      ```

3. **Mirror changes in `new.vue` Personnel tab**
   - `new.vue` has a personnel tab at some line with the same pattern. Apply the same tonal-card and category-first changes.
   - Note: Per KE-G plan, the eventual goal is a shared `CiPersonnelEditor.vue` component. KF-C can prepare the logic but does not require extracting to a component (that is KE-G scope). The two pages will have parallel (not shared) Personnel tab markup in KF-C.

**Verification:**
- KF-C-V1: Each assigned-user card uses a tonal color matching the selected Personnel Category.
- KF-C-V2: Personnel Category is the first field in each assignment row.
- KF-C-V3: Assignment cards with no category assigned show grey tonal color.
- KF-C-V4: Category summary chips show correct counts per category.
- KF-C-V5: PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP exported from `coiFormState.ts`.
- KF-C-V6: `detail-[id].vue` imports and uses `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP` from `coiFormState.ts`.

---

### KF-A — Project Diary Visibility + Timeline Panel Fix

**Objective:** Fix the `CiProjectDiary` silent-empty issue and surface both diary systems in the Detail page Timeline.

**Steps:**

1. **Operator: Apply `Migration20260514010000_AddConstructionDiaryEntries`**
   - This is a prerequisite. Without the migration, no code change fixes the diary visibility.
   - Instruction to operator: Run all 4 pending migrations (see KF-D).

2. **Fix KE-F residual pencil button** (already covered in KF-D step 1, shared fix).

3. **Add work log read-only section to Detail Timeline tab (Option C from research)**
   - File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
   - Current Timeline tab layout (lines 905–1020): Left column = Schedule + Milestones; Right column = CiProjectDiary.
   - Add a "Work Log" read-only section below Milestones in the LEFT column showing `construction_timeline_entries` data (fetched via existing endpoint `/api/construction-projects/:id/timeline-entries`).
   - The Work Log is read-only in detail view; users are directed to Edit page for modifications.
   - Implementation:
     a. Add `workLogEntries = ref([])` and `loadingWorkLog = ref(false)` refs.
     b. Add `fetchWorkLog()` function (same pattern as `CiProjectDiary.fetchEntries()` but calls `timeline-entries`).
     c. Call `fetchWorkLog()` in `onMounted()` (alongside other fetch calls).
     d. Add `<v-card class="mb-4">` in the left column below Milestones: title "Work Log", list of entries showing entry_date, entry_type chip, title, and (truncated) description.
     e. Include "Manage in Edit Project Details ↗" text link `v-if="canEditAnyTab"`.

4. **Add error handling to CiProjectDiary (improvement)**
   - File: `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectDiary.vue`
   - Current: `fetchEntries()` catches error and logs silently; user sees empty state.
   - Add an `error = ref<string | null>(null)` ref; in catch block set `error.value = 'Failed to load diary entries. The database table may not be ready.'`
   - Show `<v-alert v-if="error" type="warning" variant="tonal">{{ error }}</v-alert>` in the template above the empty-state.
   - This prevents silent failures from being confused with "no diary entries yet".

**Verification:**
- KF-A-V1: After migration applied, Detail page Timeline right column shows `CiProjectDiary` with entries (not empty).
- KF-A-V2: Detail page Timeline left column shows "Work Log" section below Milestones with timeline-entries data.
- KF-A-V3: Work Log section shows "Manage in Edit Project Details ↗" link.
- KF-A-V4: `CiProjectDiary` shows a warning alert if the API call fails (not silent empty state).
- KF-A-V5: KE-F pencil icon removed from Schedule card header (see KF-D-V1).

---

### KF-AB — Project Image Governance (PROFILE Gallery Category)

**Objective:** Enable upload of up to 3 architectural/profile project images; display in Overview.

**Prerequisites:**
- Migration for PROFILE category must be applied before upload works.
- Gallery upload infrastructure (Multer, controller) already exists.

**Steps:**

1. **Backend: New migration to add PROFILE category**
   - File: `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260???_AddProfileGalleryCategory.ts`
   - Add `'PROFILE'` to the CHECK constraint on `construction_gallery.category`:
     ```sql
     ALTER TABLE construction_gallery
       DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS construction_gallery_category_check;
     ALTER TABLE construction_gallery
       ADD CONSTRAINT construction_gallery_category_check
         CHECK (category IN ('BEFORE', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'DOCUMENTATION', 'PROFILE'));
     ```
   - Rollback: restore original 4-value CHECK.

2. **Backend: Update GalleryCategory enum**
   - File: `pmo-backend/src/common/enums/gallery-category.enum.ts`
   - Add: `PROFILE = 'PROFILE'`

3. **Backend: Enforce max-3 PROFILE images per project in service**
   - File: `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`
   - In `addGalleryItem()` method: before creating the entity, check if `dto.category === GalleryCategory.PROFILE`:
     ```typescript
     if (dto.category === GalleryCategory.PROFILE) {
       const profileCount = await this.galleryRepo.count({ projectId, category: GalleryCategory.PROFILE })
       if (profileCount >= 3) {
         throw new BadRequestException('A project can have at most 3 profile images.')
       }
     }
     ```
   - Add migration log entry in `MIGRATION_LOG.md`.

4. **Frontend (Edit page): Add Project Images upload section to Gallery tab**
   - File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
   - In the `documents` (or gallery) tab, add a new card above the existing gallery:
     ```
     ┌────────────────────────────────┐
     │  Project Profile Images (0/3)  │
     │  [Thumbnail] [Thumbnail] [Add] │
     │  Max 3 architectural images    │
     └────────────────────────────────┘
     ```
   - Fetch existing PROFILE images via `GET /:id/gallery?category=PROFILE`.
   - Show existing images as thumbnails (max 3) with delete buttons.
   - Show upload input (`v-file-input`, accept="image/*") and "Upload" button, disabled when count >= 3.
   - On upload: call `POST /:id/gallery` with `category=PROFILE` (multipart/form-data).

5. **Frontend (Detail page): Display PROFILE images in Overview tab**
   - File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
   - In the Overview tab: add a "Project Images" section showing PROFILE gallery items as an image strip (up to 3).
   - Fetch: add `fetchProfileImages()` that calls `GET /:id/gallery?category=PROFILE`.
   - Fallback: if no PROFILE images, display `project.idealInfrastructureImage` (URL field) as a single image; if neither, show "No architectural renderings uploaded" placeholder.

**Verification:**
- KF-AB-V1: Backend migration applied — `'PROFILE'` accepted by DB CHECK constraint.
- KF-AB-V2: Uploading a 4th PROFILE image returns 400 Bad Request.
- KF-AB-V3: Edit page Gallery tab shows "Project Profile Images (N/3)" section.
- KF-AB-V4: Upload button disabled when 3 PROFILE images exist.
- KF-AB-V5: Detail page Overview tab shows up to 3 PROFILE images; falls back to `idealInfrastructureImage` URL if none.
- KF-AB-V6: Placeholder renders if neither PROFILE images nor URL exist.

---

### Phase KF Execution Order

```
KF-D  (Cleanup: pencil button + stale options — zero regression risk)
  → KF-AA (CiBasicInfoForm refactor — shared component, affects Create + Edit)
    → KF-C  (Personnel tab redesign — affects Edit + Create Personnel tabs)
      → KF-A  (Diary visibility: migration prereq + Work Log panel + error handling)
        → KF-AB (Image governance: backend migration + upload UI + Overview display)
```

**Session feasibility:** KF-D + KF-AA + KF-C + KF-A (frontend parts) — all frontend. Can be done in one session after migrations are applied. KF-AB requires new backend migration (separate small step).

---

## Phase KG — Create-Page Alignment, Analytics Fix, and Continuity Resolution

**Governance:** ACE v2.4 Phase 2 (Plan Locked)
**Date:** 2026-05-14
**Research Reference:** Section 2.146 in `docs/research.md`
**Scope:** (A) new.vue tab consolidation to match KE-D standard; (B) Analytics milestone chart bug fix

**Critical Finding:** `new.vue` was NOT updated during KE-D (edit-[id].vue tab consolidation). It retains a stale 6-tab structure with duplicate contract fields (also present in CiBasicInfoForm) and duplicate building/location fields (also in CiBasicInfoForm Panel 0). This creates data confusion and violates the rule that Create and Edit must stay synchronized.

---

### Governance Directives (Phase KG)

| # | Directive |
|---|---|
| KG-D1 | Any field in `CiBasicInfoForm` must NOT be duplicated in `new.vue` window-items |
| KG-D2 | After removing the Contract tab, `funding_source_id` Required validation must move to `tabRequired.basic` |
| KG-D3 | `target_physical_progress` and `target_financial_progress` must have a home in the Schedule tab after Progress tab removal |
| KG-D4 | Guide card must accurately reflect the post-refactor tab count |
| KG-D5 | `handleSubmit` payload must NOT be changed — only the visual tab structure changes |

---

### KG-A — new.vue Tab Alignment (Critical)

**Objective:** Remove the stale Contract and Progress window-items from `new.vue`, move orphaned fields to appropriate tabs, and update all navigation/validation logic to the new 4-tab structure.

**Steps:**

1. **Update `tabOrder`**
   - File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue`
   - Change:
     ```typescript
     const tabOrder = ['basic', 'contract', 'schedule', 'progress', 'personnel', 'documents'] as const
     ```
     to:
     ```typescript
     const tabOrder = ['basic', 'schedule', 'personnel', 'documents'] as const
     ```

2. **Update `tabCompletion` computed**
   - Remove `contract` and `progress` keys
   - Add `funding_source_id` to the `basic` check:
     ```typescript
     basic: !!(form.value.title && form.value.project_category_id && form.value.funding_source_id),
     schedule: !!(form.value.start_date && form.value.end_date),
     personnel: true,
     documents: true,
     ```

3. **Update `tabRequired` computed**
   - Remove `contract` and `progress` keys
   - Add `funding_source_id` to `basic` required fields:
     ```typescript
     basic: ['title', 'project_category_id', 'funding_source_id'],
     schedule: ['start_date', 'end_date'],
     personnel: [],
     documents: [],
     ```

4. **Remove Contract tab button and window-item**
   - Remove `<v-tab value="contract">` block
   - Remove `<v-window-item value="contract">` block entirely (all 4 fields are already in CiBasicInfoForm Core card)

5. **Remove Progress tab button and window-item**
   - Remove `<v-tab value="progress">` block
   - Remove `<v-window-item value="progress">` block
   - Before removing, extract the two unique fields (`target_physical_progress`, `target_financial_progress`) for placement in Step 6

6. **Add progress fields to Schedule window-item**
   - File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue`
   - In the Schedule window-item (`<v-window-item value="schedule">`), after the existing start/end date fields, add:
     ```html
     <v-row class="mt-2">
       <v-col cols="12">
         <div class="text-subtitle-2 mb-3">Progress Targets</div>
       </v-col>
       <v-col cols="12" md="6">
         <v-text-field
           v-model.number="form.target_physical_progress"
           label="Target Physical Progress (%)"
           type="number"
           min="0"
           max="100"
           suffix="%"
           variant="outlined"
           density="compact"
         />
       </v-col>
       <v-col cols="12" md="6">
         <v-text-field
           v-model.number="form.target_financial_progress"
           label="Target Financial Progress (%)"
           type="number"
           min="0"
           max="100"
           suffix="%"
           variant="outlined"
           density="compact"
         />
       </v-col>
     </v-row>
     ```

7. **Update guide card**
   - Find the guide card `:content` array and change `'5 tabs to complete'` → `'4 tabs to complete'`
   - Update guidance text if needed to remove references to "Contract" or "Progress" tabs

8. **Verify `handleSubmit` payload is unchanged**
   - Confirm `form.value.funding_source_id`, `form.value.contractor_id`, `form.value.contract_number`, `form.value.contract_amount` are still in the payload (they are — through `form` ref which CiBasicInfoForm mutates via v-model)
   - Confirm `form.value.target_physical_progress` and `form.value.target_financial_progress` are in the payload (they are — same `form` ref)
   - **Do NOT modify `handleSubmit`.** Only the visual/tab structure changes.

**Verification:**
- KG-A-V1: `tabOrder` has exactly 4 entries: `['basic', 'schedule', 'personnel', 'documents']`
- KG-A-V2: Create form renders 4 tabs, no "Contract" or "Progress" tab visible
- KG-A-V3: Funding Source select appears in Basic Info tab (CiBasicInfoForm Core card) — not duplicated
- KG-A-V4: Building/location fields appear in Basic Info (CiBasicInfoForm Panel 0) — not in Schedule
- KG-A-V5: Schedule tab shows start/end date fields PLUS Target Physical Progress % and Target Financial Progress % fields
- KG-A-V6: Guide card shows "4 tabs to complete"
- KG-A-V7: Create project submission payload is unchanged (same fields as before)
- KG-A-V8: Tab navigation Next/Prev works correctly across all 4 tabs
- KG-A-V9: `tabCompletion.basic` is false when `funding_source_id` is empty

---

### KG-B — Analytics Milestone Chart Fix

**Objective:** Fix the ApexCharts horizontal bar milestone chart so category labels display correctly.

**Steps:**

1. **Fix `yaxis.categories` → `xaxis.categories`**
   - File: `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`
   - Find the `milestoneChart` computed (approximately line 107–127):
     ```typescript
     const milestoneChart = computed(() => {
       const ms = props.project?.milestones ?? []
       if (!ms.length) return null
       return {
         series: [{ name: 'Progress', data: ms.map(m => m.completionPercentage ?? 0) }],
         options: {
           chart: { type: 'bar' },
           plotOptions: { bar: { horizontal: true } },
           xaxis: { max: 100, title: { text: 'Progress %' } },
           yaxis: { categories: ms.map(m => m.name || m.title || '—') },   // ← BUG
         }
       }
     })
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```typescript
     xaxis: {
       categories: ms.map(m => m.title || m.name || '—'),
       max: 100,
       title: { text: 'Progress %' },
     },
     yaxis: {},
     ```
   - The `categories` property must be on `xaxis`, not `yaxis`, for ApexCharts horizontal bar charts.

**Verification:**
- KG-B-V1: Milestone chart renders with milestone names displayed as labels on the left axis
- KG-B-V2: Progress bars show correct width proportional to `completionPercentage`
- KG-B-V3: Chart renders correctly when 1 milestone exists; "No milestones yet" text shown when no milestones

---

### Phase KG Execution Order

```
KG-A  (new.vue alignment — removes duplicate fields, fixes tab count)
  → KG-B  (analytics milestone chart bug fix — one-line change)
```

Both steps are frontend-only. No backend changes or migrations required.

---

### Phase KG Verification Matrix

| ID | Verification | Area |
|---|---|---|
| KG-A-V1 | `tabOrder` = `['basic', 'schedule', 'personnel', 'documents']` | new.vue |
| KG-A-V2 | 4 tabs rendered in Create form | new.vue |
| KG-A-V3 | Funding Source only in Basic Info (CiBasicInfoForm) | new.vue |
| KG-A-V4 | Building/location only in Basic Info (CiBasicInfoForm) | new.vue |
| KG-A-V5 | Schedule tab has Target Physical + Financial Progress fields | new.vue |
| KG-A-V6 | Guide card shows "4 tabs" | new.vue |
| KG-A-V7 | Create submission payload unchanged | new.vue |
| KG-A-V8 | Tab navigation correct (Prev/Next) | new.vue |
| KG-A-V9 | `tabCompletion.basic` requires `funding_source_id` | new.vue |
| KG-B-V1 | Milestone labels visible on chart Y-axis | CiProjectAnalyticsTab |
| KG-B-V2 | Progress bars correct width | CiProjectAnalyticsTab |
| KG-B-V3 | Empty state text when no milestones | CiProjectAnalyticsTab |

---

### Phase KG Governance Directives Summary

- `new.vue` must use `CiBasicInfoForm` as the single source of truth for Basic Info fields — no duplicate rendering in other tabs
- `target_physical_progress` and `target_financial_progress` are unique to `new.vue`; they move to Schedule tab (not removed)
- `handleSubmit` payload must not change — only visual/navigation structure changes
- ApexCharts horizontal bar uses `xaxis.categories` for the category labels — not `yaxis.categories`
- Do NOT commit or push until operator explicitly instructs

## Phase KH — Attachment Governance Refactor, View/Edit Governance Fix, Analytics Bug, and Overview Optimization

**Governance:** ACE v2.4 Phase 2 (Plan Locked)
**Date:** 2026-05-11
**Research Reference:** Section 2.147 in `docs/research.md`
**Scope:** (F) View/Edit governance violations; (D) Analytics obligation typo; (A) Attachment tab restructure; (C) Overview optimization; (B) Personnel layout; (E) Timeline TAB_MAP fix; (G) Gallery category enum

---

### Governance Directives (Phase KH)

| # | Directive |
|---|---|
| KH-D1 | `detail-[id].vue` is the read-only surface — NO component on this page may receive `:can-edit="true"` or a reactive authorized value; all mutation gates must be hard-coded `:can-edit="false"` |
| KH-D2 | The `obligation` field is singular throughout the codebase (`f.obligation`); `f.obligations` is a typo and MUST NOT be used |
| KH-D3 | `profileImageFile` and `galleryImageFile` must be separate `ref<File | null>` variables — they MUST NOT share a single ref |
| KH-D4 | The Attachment Documents tab in edit-[id].vue must be split into three visually distinct `v-card` sections: (1) Documents, (2) Gallery, (3) Links — each with its own upload control |
| KH-D5 | Gallery category enum values MUST match `GalleryCategory` entity enum; new.vue and edit-[id].vue must use the same set |
| KH-D6 | `overviewPanels` initial value must NOT include all panels open; only high-priority panels (Financial Summary, Key Documents) are open by default |
| KH-D7 | `editSection('attachments')` in detail page Gallery tab MUST be changed to `editSection('gallery')` — TAB_MAP has `'gallery' → 'documents'` |
| KH-D8 | No backend changes or migrations in Phase KH — all changes are frontend only |
| KH-D9 | Do NOT commit or push until operator explicitly instructs |

---

### KH-F — View/Edit Governance: Fix `:can-edit` Leakage on Detail Page

**Objective:** Three components on `detail-[id].vue` receive `:can-edit="canEditCurrentProject"`, which allows users to perform write operations from the read-only view page. Pass `:can-edit="false"` instead.

**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**Steps:**

1. **Fix `CiProjectDiary` on Detail page**
   - Find (line ~1254):
     ```html
     <CiProjectDiary :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```html
     <CiProjectDiary :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="false" />
     ```

2. **Fix `CiPowItemTable` on Detail page**
   - Find (line ~1368):
     ```html
     <CiPowItemTable :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```html
     <CiPowItemTable :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="false" />
     ```

3. **Fix `CiDocumentChecklist` on Detail page**
   - Find (line ~1414):
     ```html
     <CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```html
     <CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="false" />
     ```

4. **Fix `editSection('attachments')` in Gallery tab**
   - Find (line ~1387):
     ```html
     @click="editSection('attachments')"
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```html
     @click="editSection('gallery')"
     ```
   - TAB_MAP entry `gallery → 'documents'` already exists at line 108.

**Verification:**
- KH-F-V1: On Detail page, diary entries cannot be added/edited/deleted by any user
- KH-F-V2: On Detail page, POW items cannot be added/edited/deleted by any user
- KH-F-V3: On Detail page, document checklist items cannot be toggled/modified by any user
- KH-F-V4: "Manage" link in Gallery tab navigates to Edit page Documents tab (not Basic Info tab)

---

### KH-D — Analytics: Fix `obligation` Typo

**Objective:** The financial bar chart Obligations series always renders as zero because `f.obligations` (plural) is always `undefined`. Fix to `f.obligation` (singular).

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`

**Steps:**

1. **Fix `obligations` → `obligation` in `financialChart`**
   - Find (line ~97):
     ```typescript
     { name: 'Obligations',   data: sorted.map(f => Number(f.obligations ?? 0)) },
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```typescript
     { name: 'Obligations',   data: sorted.map(f => Number(f.obligation ?? 0)) },
     ```

2. **Fix `financialUtilization` to aggregate across all records**
   - Find (lines ~41–46):
     ```typescript
     const financialUtilization = computed(() => {
       const f = props.project.financials?.[0] as any
       if (!f) return null
       const r = f.utilizationRate ?? f.utilization_rate
       return r != null ? Number(r) : null
     })
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```typescript
     const financialUtilization = computed(() => {
       const fins = (props.project.financials || []) as any[]
       if (!fins.length) return null
       const totalApp = fins.reduce((s, f) => s + Number(f.appropriation ?? 0), 0)
       const totalObl = fins.reduce((s, f) => s + Number(f.obligation ?? 0), 0)
       return totalApp > 0 ? (totalObl / totalApp) * 100 : null
     })
     ```

**Verification:**
- KH-D-V1: Financial bar chart Obligations series shows non-zero values when obligation data exists
- KH-D-V2: Financial Utilization KPI card shows aggregate utilization (total obligation / total appropriation × 100)
- KH-D-V3: Chart renders correctly for projects with multiple fiscal year records

---

### KH-A — Attachment Tab Restructure (Edit Page)

**Objective:** Break the monolithic `v-card` Documents tab in `edit-[id].vue` into three clearly separated sections (Documents, Gallery, Links), fix the shared `imageFile` ref bug, and improve the existing-files list with category grouping.

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

**Steps:**

1. **Declare separate file refs for profile vs gallery image uploads**
   - Find in script setup:
     ```typescript
     const imageFile = ref<File | null>(null)
     const imageCategory = ref('PROGRESS')
     const imageCaption = ref('')
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```typescript
     const profileImageFile = ref<File | null>(null)
     const profileImageCaption = ref('')
     const galleryImageFile = ref<File | null>(null)
     const galleryImageCategory = ref<string>('IN_PROGRESS')
     const galleryImageCaption = ref('')
     ```
   - Verify `imageFile` is not referenced elsewhere in the script before removing it.

2. **Update `uploadEditImage` function signature**
   - The existing `uploadEditImage()` likely reads `imageFile` and `imageCategory` — split into two targeted functions:
     ```typescript
     async function uploadProfileImage() {
       if (!profileImageFile.value) return
       // ... upload with category='PROFILE', caption=profileImageCaption
       // on success: profileImageFile.value = null; profileImageCaption.value = ''
     }

     async function uploadGalleryImage() {
       if (!galleryImageFile.value) return
       // ... upload with category=galleryImageCategory.value, caption=galleryImageCaption
       // on success: galleryImageFile.value = null; galleryImageCaption.value = ''
     }
     ```
   - Keep separate loading flags: `profileImageUploading` and `galleryImageUploading`.

3. **Restructure Documents tab template into three cards**
   - Remove the single `v-card` wrapping the entire tab content
   - Replace with three sibling `v-card` elements:

   **Card 1 — Documents:**
   ```html
   <v-card class="mb-4">
     <v-card-title>
       <v-icon start>mdi-paperclip</v-icon>
       Documents
       <v-chip size="x-small" variant="tonal" class="ml-2">{{ existingDocs.length }}</v-chip>
     </v-card-title>
     <v-divider />
     <v-card-text>
       <!-- Existing docs list grouped by documentType -->
       <!-- File upload row -->
     </v-card-text>
   </v-card>
   ```

   **Card 2 — Gallery:**
   ```html
   <v-card class="mb-4">
     <v-card-title>
       <v-icon start>mdi-image-multiple</v-icon>
       Gallery
       <v-chip size="x-small" variant="tonal" class="ml-2">{{ existingGallery.length }}</v-chip>
     </v-card-title>
     <v-divider />
     <v-card-text>
       <!-- Profile images sub-section (max 3, shown in Overview) -->
       <!-- General gallery sub-section -->
       <!-- Gallery upload row (galleryImageFile) -->
     </v-card-text>
   </v-card>
   ```

   **Card 3 — External Links:**
   ```html
   <v-card class="mb-4">
     <v-card-title>
       <v-icon start>mdi-link</v-icon>
       External Links
     </v-card-title>
     <v-divider />
     <v-card-text>
       <!-- Existing links list -->
       <!-- Link add row -->
     </v-card-text>
   </v-card>
   ```

4. **Add category grouping to existing documents list**
   - Create a computed:
     ```typescript
     const docsByCategory = computed(() => {
       const groups: Record<string, typeof existingDocs.value> = {}
       for (const d of existingDocs.value) {
         const k = d.documentType || 'attachment'
         ;(groups[k] ||= []).push(d)
       }
       return groups
     })
     ```
   - Render grouped by category with a `text-overline` heading per group

5. **Fix gallery category enum to match `GalleryCategory`**
   - Check `pmo-backend/src/common/enums/gallery-category.enum.ts` for canonical values
   - Update `galleryImageCategory` select items in edit-[id].vue to match enum
   - Update `newImageCategory` in new.vue to use the same enum values

**Verification:**
- KH-A-V1: Documents, Gallery, and External Links each render in their own `v-card` with distinct titles
- KH-A-V2: Profile image upload uses `profileImageFile` ref; general gallery upload uses `galleryImageFile` ref — selecting an image for one does not affect the other
- KH-A-V3: Existing documents are grouped by document type with overline headings
- KH-A-V4: Gallery category options in edit-[id].vue match the backend enum
- KH-A-V5: Upload operations execute independently with separate loading flags

---

### KH-C — Overview Tab Optimization

**Objective:** Reduce default panel expansion to prevent overwhelming scroll, add a compact KPI stat row above the expansion panels, and implement a 2-column panel layout for content-light panels.

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**Steps:**

1. **Reduce default open panels**
   - Find:
     ```typescript
     const overviewPanels = ref<number[]>([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8])
     ```
   - Change to:
     ```typescript
     const overviewPanels = ref<number[]>([3, 5])
     ```
   - Index 3 = Financial Summary (operationally critical); Index 5 = Key Documents (compliance awareness)
   - All other panels start collapsed — user expands on demand

2. **Add compact KPI stat row above expansion panels**
   - After the Status/Progress card (line ~689), before `v-expansion-panels`, insert:
     ```html
     <v-row dense class="mb-3">
       <v-col cols="6" sm="3">
         <v-card variant="tonal" color="primary" class="pa-3 text-center">
           <div class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis">Physical Progress</div>
           <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">{{ project.physicalProgress }}%</div>
           <div class="text-caption" :class="project.physicalProgress >= project.targetPhysicalProgress ? 'text-success' : 'text-warning'">
             Target {{ project.targetPhysicalProgress }}%
           </div>
         </v-card>
       </v-col>
       <v-col cols="6" sm="3">
         <v-card variant="tonal" color="info" class="pa-3 text-center">
           <div class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis">Financial Progress</div>
           <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">{{ project.financialProgress }}%</div>
           <div class="text-caption" :class="project.financialProgress >= project.targetFinancialProgress ? 'text-success' : 'text-warning'">
             Target {{ project.targetFinancialProgress }}%
           </div>
         </v-card>
       </v-col>
       <v-col cols="6" sm="3">
         <v-card variant="tonal" color="success" class="pa-3 text-center">
           <div class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis">Utilization Rate</div>
           <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">{{ formatRate(financialSummary.utilization) }}</div>
           <div class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis">Obligation / Appropriation</div>
         </v-card>
       </v-col>
       <v-col cols="6" sm="3">
         <v-card variant="tonal" color="warning" class="pa-3 text-center">
           <div class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis">Milestones</div>
           <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">
             {{ project.milestones?.filter((m: any) => m.status === 'COMPLETED').length ?? 0 }} / {{ project.milestones?.length ?? 0 }}
           </div>
           <div class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis">Completed</div>
         </v-card>
       </v-col>
     </v-row>
     ```

3. **Remove duplicate progress information from Status card**
   - The Status card (lines 577–689) shows progress bars that duplicate the new KPI row
   - Keep the Status chip + Publication chip + Approval metadata in the status card
   - Remove the Physical Progress and Financial Progress progress-bar section from the status card (these are now the KPI row above)
   - Keep Contract Amount — it is not in the KPI row

**Verification:**
- KH-C-V1: On page load, only Financial Summary and Key Documents panels are open; all others are collapsed
- KH-C-V2: 4 KPI stat cards (Physical %, Financial %, Utilization %, Milestones) are visible above panels without scrolling
- KH-C-V3: Progress bars removed from the Status card row (no duplicate information)
- KH-C-V4: Status card still shows Status chip, Publication status, Contract Amount, and approval metadata

---

### KH-B — Personnel Tab Layout Improvement (Edit Page)

**Objective:** Add group-context headers and 2-column grid to the assignment metadata repeater in `edit-[id].vue`, so operators can see category groupings while editing.

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

**Steps:**

1. **Create a computed `groupedAssignments` for edit page**
   - In script setup, add:
     ```typescript
     const groupedAssignments = computed(() => {
       return PERSONNEL_GROUPS.map(g => ({
         ...g,
         userIds: form.value.assigned_user_ids.filter(uid =>
           (assignmentMetadata.value[uid]?.personnelCategory || '__UNCATEGORIZED__') === g.code
         ),
       }))
     })
     ```

2. **Replace flat assignment card stack with grouped layout**
   - In the Personnel window-item, the existing `v-card v-for="uid in form.value.assigned_user_ids"` block (lines 1747–1827)
   - Replace with:
     ```html
     <div v-for="group in groupedAssignments" :key="group.code">
       <div v-if="group.userIds.length > 0" class="mb-4">
         <div class="d-flex align-center ga-2 mb-2">
           <v-icon :icon="group.icon" size="small" :color="group.color" />
           <span class="text-subtitle-2 font-weight-medium" :class="`text-${group.color}`">{{ group.label }}</span>
           <v-chip size="x-small" variant="tonal">{{ group.userIds.length }}</v-chip>
         </div>
         <v-row dense>
           <v-col
             v-for="uid in group.userIds"
             :key="uid"
             cols="12"
             md="6"
           >
             <v-card variant="tonal" :color="group.color" class="h-100">
               <v-card-text class="pa-3">
                 <!-- existing per-user metadata fields -->
               </v-card-text>
             </v-card>
           </v-col>
         </v-row>
       </div>
     </div>
     <!-- Uncategorized at bottom -->
     ```
   - Users not yet assigned a category fall into `__UNCATEGORIZED__` group and render at the bottom

3. **Add personnel metadata fields to new.vue**
   - In new.vue personnel section, after the `v-autocomplete` for `assigned_user_ids`, add the per-user metadata repeater (same pattern as edit-[id].vue)
   - Requires `assignmentMetadata` ref and `ensureAssignmentMetadata` helper to be added to new.vue
   - The metadata is included in the `handleSubmit` payload as `assignments` array (mirroring edit-[id].vue line 385–396)

**Verification:**
- KH-B-V1: In edit page Personnel tab, assigned users are visually grouped by personnel category
- KH-B-V2: Each category group shows a color-coded header with icon, label, and user count
- KH-B-V3: Uncategorized users appear in a distinct group at the bottom
- KH-B-V4: 2-column layout used for assignment metadata cards at `md` breakpoint
- KH-B-V5: new.vue personnel section allows per-user category/role/department/phone assignment

   - Expected state: `{ overview: 'basic', timeline: 'timeline', pow: 'pow', gallery: 'documents', documents: 'documents', team: 'personnel', analytics: 'basic' }`
   - If `analytics` is missing, add it (falls back to `basic` which is acceptable)

### KH-G — Gallery Category Enum Synchronization

**Objective:** Unify gallery category enum values between `new.vue` (uses `PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `AFTER`, `OTHER`) and `edit-[id].vue` (uses `IN_PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `COMPLETED`, `DOCUMENTATION`).

**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue`, `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`, `pmo-backend/src/common/enums/gallery-category.enum.ts`

**Steps:**

1. **Read canonical enum**
   - Open `pmo-backend/src/common/enums/gallery-category.enum.ts`
   - The values defined there are the source of truth

2. **Update new.vue gallery category options to match canonical enum**
   - Replace the current `{ title: 'Progress', value: 'PROGRESS' }` etc. with the canonical set

3. **Ensure edit-[id].vue gallery category options include all canonical values**
   - Verify edit-[id].vue uses the correct canonical values (it currently uses `IN_PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `COMPLETED`, `DOCUMENTATION`)

**Verification:**
- KH-G-V1: Gallery category options in new.vue and edit-[id].vue use identical value sets
- KH-G-V2: Images uploaded from Create appear in Edit gallery with correct category displayed

---

### Phase KH Execution Order

```
KH-F  (View/Edit governance: 3x can-edit + TAB_MAP gallery fix — 4 targeted line changes)
  → KH-D  (Analytics obligation typo + utilization aggregate — 2 targeted changes)
    → KH-A  (Attachment tab restructure — largest change; split ref + 3 cards)
      → KH-C  (Overview panels default + KPI row + remove duplicate progress bars)
        → KH-B  (Personnel grouped layout + new.vue metadata fields)
          → KH-E  (TAB_MAP analytics entry verification)
            → KH-G  (Gallery enum sync after reading canonical values)
```

KH-F and KH-D are each ~4 line changes and should be done first as standalone fixes.
KH-A is the largest change (~250 lines restructured in Documents tab).
KH-C and KH-B are medium-scope UI changes.
KH-E and KH-G are verification-and-patch steps.

## Phase KI — Overview Consolidation and Timeline Balance

**Research Reference:** Section 2.148 (docs/research.md)
**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` only
**Backend changes:** None
**New migrations:** None
**New API endpoints:** None

---

### Governance Directives (KI)

| # | Directive |
|---|---|
| KI-D1 | Phase 3 may only execute KI-A and KI-B as defined below — no additional scope |
| KI-D2 | No changes to CiProjectHeroCard.vue — hero card is stable and complete |
| KI-D3 | No changes to CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue — KE-E is complete |
| KI-D4 | No backend changes, no migration files, no new composables |
| KI-D5 | Expansion panels structure (9 panels) must be preserved — only default open set changes |
| KI-D6 | Schedule card must be relocated (full-width above row), not removed — it contains 3 unique fields |

---

### KI-A — Overview Tab: Remove Redundant Status Card + Slim KPI Row + Change Default Panels

**Objective:** Eliminate triple-layer KPI redundancy on the Overview tab. After KI-A, each data point appears exactly once in the Overview header area.

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**Steps:**

1. **Remove Status/Progress header card**
   - Delete the full `<v-card class="mb-4">` block covering the Status chip, Publication chip, Contract Amount, Implementing Agency, and Project Status Category (lines 578–640)
   - Relocate unique content: place a compact `<div>` chip row directly below CiProjectHeroCard (after the profile images strip at ~line 575)
   - The compact chip row must contain: publication status chip only (the `publicationStatusChip` computed object already has label + color)
   - Contract Amount and Status chip are already in CiProjectHeroCard — do NOT duplicate them
   - Implementing Agency and Project Status Category are already in the Strategic Context expansion panel — no relocation needed

2. **Slim KPI stat row from 4 cards to 2 cards**
   - Locate the 4-card KPI stat row (lines 643–687)
   - Remove: Physical Progress % card (already in hero card KPI strip)
   - Remove: Utilization Rate card (already covered by "Financial Utilization %" in hero card KPI strip)
   - Keep: Financial Progress % card (`project.financialProgress`)
   - Keep: Milestones count card (`milestonesSummary.completed / milestonesSummary.total`)
   - Result: 2-card row using `<v-col cols="6">` each for even spacing at full width

3. **Change default expansion panels**
   - Locate `overviewPanels = ref<number[]>([3, 5])` (line ~27)
   - Change to `overviewPanels = ref<number[]>([5])` (Key Documents panel only)
   - Rationale: Financial Summary (index 3) contains a `v-data-table` — eager render on mount is unnecessary; Key Documents (index 5) remains open as the most operationally relevant section

**Verification:**
- KI-A-V1: Status/Progress header card is gone from Overview tab
- KI-A-V2: Publication status chip visible as compact chip strip below hero card (between hero card and KPI row)
- KI-A-V3: KPI row shows exactly 2 cards: Financial Progress % + Milestones count
- KI-A-V4: Physical Progress % appears only in hero card KPI strip (not duplicated in KPI row)
- KI-A-V5: Utilization Rate appears only in hero card KPI strip
- KI-A-V6: On Overview load, only Key Documents panel is expanded
- KI-A-V7: Financial Summary panel is collapsed by default (no eager v-data-table render)

---

### KI-B — Timeline Tab: Move Schedule Card to Full-Width

**Objective:** Fix left-column height imbalance in Timeline tab by moving Schedule card above the 2-column row, matching the edit page pattern.

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**Steps:**

1. **Identify the 2-column row in the Timeline tab**
   - The timeline tab structure (lines 1096–1254):
     - `<v-row>` containing two `<v-col cols="12" md="6">`
     - Left col: Schedule card + Milestones card + Work Log card
     - Right col: `<CiProjectDiary :can-edit="false" />`

2. **Move Schedule card above the row**
   - Cut the Schedule `<v-card class="mb-4">` block (fields: Start Date, Target Completion, Actual Completion, Duration, Original Contract Duration) from left `<v-col>`
   - Paste the card as a full-width block directly inside the `<v-window-item value="timeline">`, immediately ABOVE the `<v-row>` — mirroring the edit page's "Progress Summary full-width → 2-col Milestones+Diary" pattern
   - Left col now contains: Milestones card + Work Log card only
   - Right col still contains: `<CiProjectDiary :can-edit="false" />`

3. **Verify no duplicate structure introduced**
   - Schedule card fields (Start Date, Target Completion, Actual Completion, Duration, Original Contract Duration) are non-redundant in the timeline tab context — the hero card shows only Start + Target Completion; this full schedule card adds Actual Completion + Duration + Original Contract Duration which are timeline-specific

**Verification:**
- KI-B-V1: Schedule card appears as full-width card above the 2-column row in Timeline tab
- KI-B-V2: Left column contains only Milestones card + Work Log card
- KI-B-V3: Right column contains only CiProjectDiary (`:can-edit="false"` preserved)
- KI-B-V4: No empty right-side gap visible when Milestones list is short
- KI-B-V5: `:can-edit="false"` on CiProjectDiary is unchanged

---

### Phase KI Execution Order

```
KI-A  (Overview: remove status card, slim KPI row, change default panels — ~60 line change)
  → KI-B  (Timeline: move Schedule card to full-width above row — ~30 line change)
```

KI-A first — higher UX impact, independent of timeline tab.
KI-B after — isolated to timeline window-item; no dependency on KI-A.

---

| KI-A-V2 | Publication status chip visible below hero card | detail-[id].vue |
| KI-A-V3 | KPI row: exactly 2 cards (Financial Progress % + Milestones) | detail-[id].vue |

### Step KJ-D — Documents Tab: File Library Before Checklist

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Risk:** ZERO — pure template block reorder

Current order:
1. `<CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="false" />`
2. `<v-card>` File Library (files + links)

Required order:
1. `<v-card>` File Library (files + links) — move to top
2. `<CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="false" />` — move below

|---|---|---|
| KJ-D-V1 | File Library card appears above CiDocumentChecklist in Documents tab | detail-[id].vue |

### Step KJ-A — Overview Tab: 2-Column Expansion Panel Layout + Gallery Preview

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Risk:** LOW — ref rename + template restructure

**A.1 — Reactive ref changes (script setup)**

Remove:
```typescript
const overviewPanels = ref<number[]>([5])
```

Replace with:
```typescript
const overviewPanelsLeft = ref<number[]>([3])   // Key Documents at left-array index 3
const overviewPanelsRight = ref<number[]>([])
```

Add gallery preview computed:
```typescript
const recentGalleryImages = computed(() =>
  gallery.value.filter(g => g.category !== 'PROFILE').slice(0, 6)
)
```

**A.2 — Template: Replace single v-expansion-panels with 2-col <v-row>**

Wrap panels in `<v-row class="mt-0">` with two `<v-col cols="12" md="6" class="pt-0">`.

LEFT column panels (in order, `v-model="overviewPanelsLeft"`):
1. Project Information (original index 0)
2. Contract Information (original index 2)
3. Financial Summary (original index 3)
4. Key Documents (original index 5) ← index 3 in left-col array → `overviewPanelsLeft = [3]`

RIGHT column panels (in order, `v-model="overviewPanelsRight"`):
1. Project Details & Location (original index 1)
2. Approval History (original index 4)
3. Strategic Context (original index 6)
4. Project Indicators (original index 7)
5. Signatories (original index 8)

**A.3 — Gallery preview strip**

### Step KJ-C — Timeline Tab: Equal-Height Columns + View All Dialogs

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Risk:** LOW — CSS class additions + new dialog blocks

**C.1 — Equal-height columns**

Change both `<v-col cols="12" md="6">` in the timeline `<v-row>` to include `class="d-flex flex-column"`.

On the last card in each column, add `class="flex-grow-1"` (or `style="flex: 1"`):
- LEFT: Milestones card → `flex-grow-1`
- RIGHT: CiProjectDiary wrapper div or the diary card itself → `flex-grow-1`

**C.2 — Script setup additions (Milestones dialog)**

```typescript
const milestonesDialogOpen = ref(false)
const milestoneSearch = ref('')
const milestoneStatusFilter = ref<string>('ALL')

const filteredMilestones = computed(() => {
  let items = project.value?.milestones ?? []
  if (milestoneStatusFilter.value !== 'ALL') {
    items = items.filter(m => m.status === milestoneStatusFilter.value)
  }
  if (milestoneSearch.value.trim()) {
    const q = milestoneSearch.value.toLowerCase()
    items = items.filter(m => m.title?.toLowerCase().includes(q))
  }
  return items
})
```

**C.3 — Script setup additions (Work Log dialog)**

```typescript
const workLogDialogOpen = ref(false)
const workLogSearch = ref('')
const workLogTypeFilter = ref<string>('ALL')

const filteredWorkLog = computed(() => {
  let items = workLogEntries.value ?? []
  if (workLogTypeFilter.value !== 'ALL') {
    items = items.filter(e => e.entryType === workLogTypeFilter.value)
  }
  if (workLogSearch.value.trim()) {
    const q = workLogSearch.value.toLowerCase()
    items = items.filter(e => e.description?.toLowerCase().includes(q) || e.workDone?.toLowerCase().includes(q))
  }
  return items
})
```

**C.4 — Template: "View All" buttons**

In the Milestones card title area, add:
```vue
<v-btn v-if="(project?.milestones?.length ?? 0) > 5" variant="text" size="small"
  @click="milestonesDialogOpen = true">View All</v-btn>
```

In the Work Log card title area, add:
```vue
<v-btn v-if="workLogEntries.length > 5" variant="text" size="small"
  @click="workLogDialogOpen = true">View All</v-btn>
```

**C.5 — Dialog templates**

Milestones dialog: `<v-dialog v-model="milestonesDialogOpen" max-width="860" scrollable>` with status filter chips + search field + filteredMilestones v-list.

Work Log dialog: `<v-dialog v-model="workLogDialogOpen" max-width="860" scrollable>` with type filter chips + search field + filteredWorkLog v-list.

Both dialogs reset their filter refs on close via `@update:modelValue="if (!$event) { milestoneSearch = ''; milestoneStatusFilter = 'ALL' }"`.

**Verification:**

| KJ-C-V2 | "View All" button appears in Milestones card title when > 5 milestones exist | detail-[id].vue |
| KJ-C-V3 | "View All" button appears in Work Log card title when > 5 entries exist | detail-[id].vue |
| KJ-C-V4 | Milestones dialog opens and shows all milestones | detail-[id].vue |
| KJ-C-V5 | Work Log dialog opens and shows all work log entries | detail-[id].vue |
| KJ-C-V6 | Status filter in milestones dialog correctly filters list | detail-[id].vue |
| KJ-C-V7 | Type filter in work log dialog correctly filters list | detail-[id].vue |
| KJ-C-V8 | Search in both dialogs filters by text content | detail-[id].vue |
| KJ-C-V9 | Filter state resets when dialogs are closed | detail-[id].vue |

### Step KJ-B — Analytics Tab: Empty-State UX + Personnel Analytics

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`
**Risk:** LOW — template conditional + new block

**B.1 — Improved empty state**

When `allEmpty = true`, current behavior shows all KPI cards at 0 + 6 placeholder chart cards = cluttered.

Wrap all KPI cards + chart cards in `<template v-if="!allEmpty">`.

Separately, wrap the existing info alert in `<template v-if="allEmpty">` and add a clean empty-state card:

```vue
<template v-if="allEmpty">
  <v-card variant="outlined" class="pa-8 text-center">
    <v-icon size="64" color="grey-lighten-1">mdi-chart-box-outline</v-icon>
    <div class="text-h6 mt-4 text-grey-darken-1">No Analytics Data Available</div>
    <div class="text-body-2 text-grey mt-2 mb-6">
      Analytics will appear once project data has been entered.
    </div>
    <v-list dense class="text-left mx-auto" style="max-width: 340px">
      <v-list-item prepend-icon="mdi-checkbox-blank-circle-outline" title="Add POW items in the POW tab" />
      <v-list-item prepend-icon="mdi-checkbox-blank-circle-outline" title="Enter financial data for this project" />
      <v-list-item prepend-icon="mdi-checkbox-blank-circle-outline" title="Add project milestones in the Timeline tab" />
      <v-list-item prepend-icon="mdi-checkbox-blank-circle-outline" title="Upload compliance documents" />
    </v-list>
  </v-card>
</template>
```

**B.2 — Personnel analytics block**

Add a new chart card below the existing 6 cards when `!allEmpty && hasPersonnelData`:

```typescript
const hasPersonnelData = computed(() => (props.project?.assignedUsers?.length ?? 0) > 0)
const personnelSummary = computed(() => {
  const users = props.project?.assignedUsers ?? []
  const byRole: Record<string, number> = {}
  users.forEach(u => { byRole[u.role ?? 'Unknown'] = (byRole[u.role ?? 'Unknown'] ?? 0) + 1 })
  return Object.entries(byRole).map(([role, count]) => ({ role, count }))
})
```

Card: horizontal bar chart or v-list summary showing personnel count by role — uses `personnelSummary` computed.

**Verification:**
| ID | Check | File |
|---|---|---|
| KJ-B-V1 | When project has no data, only the clean empty-state card renders (no 0-value KPI cards) | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |
| KJ-B-V2 | When project has data, all KPI cards and chart cards render as before | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |
| KJ-B-V3 | Personnel summary card renders when assignedUsers.length > 0 | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |
| KJ-B-V4 | Personnel card is hidden when no users are assigned | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |
| KJ-B-V5 | No regression in existing chart rendering (KE-E behavior preserved) | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |

## Phase KK — Overview Panel Governance, Analytics Stabilization, Timeline Scalability, Document Dual-Column

**Research basis:** research.md Section 2.150

**Backend changes:** NONE

**Status:** KK-B ✅ Analytics refactored 2026-05-13 (ClientOnly fallback skeleton, series guards on all 4 main charts, normalized chart heights to 320, ApexOptions `as const` literals). KK-A / KK-C / KK-D still pending.

### Governance Directives (Phase KK)

| ID | Directive |
|---|---|
| KK-GOV-1 | Do NOT commit or push until operator explicitly authorizes |
| KK-GOV-2 | Overview `overviewPanelsLeft` ref change is the ONLY script modification in KK-A |
| KK-GOV-3 | Analytics `<ClientOnly>` wrapper must use `#fallback` skeleton; no empty fallback |
| KK-GOV-4 | Series guards must use `some(v => v > 0)` — not `every(v => v > 0)` — to avoid suppressing partially-populated charts |
| KK-GOV-5 | Timeline inline v-for MUST be sliced to 5; "View All" button threshold remains > 5 |
| KK-GOV-6 | Documents dual-column wraps existing blocks only; no content inside blocks changes |
| KK-GOV-7 | All KJ-C dialog functionality (search, filter, reset) must be verified before KK-C is executed |
