### Step KK-B — Analytics: ClientOnly Wrapper + Eager Mount + Series Guards

**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`, `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`
**Risk:** LOW — wrapper addition + v-if guards; no logic changes

**B.1 — detail-[id].vue: Add `eager` and `<ClientOnly>` wrapper**

Locate the analytics window-item (currently: `<v-window-item value="analytics">`).

Change to:
```vue
<v-window-item value="analytics" eager>
  <ClientOnly>
    <CiProjectAnalyticsTab
      v-if="project"
      :project="project"
      :project-id="project.id"
    />
    <template #fallback>
      <v-skeleton-loader type="card, card" class="ma-4" />
    </template>
  </ClientOnly>
</v-window-item>
```

`eager` forces pre-mount before tab transition begins, so ApexCharts initializes when the DOM container already has stable dimensions.

`<ClientOnly>` ensures `apexcharts` never attempts SSR rendering, preventing hydration mismatches.

**B.2 — CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue: Series guard computeds**

For each chart that may have all-zero series, add a guard computed:

```typescript
// Financial chart guard
const hasNonZeroFinancials = computed(() =>
  (financialChart.value?.series ?? []).some((s: number) => s > 0)
)

// POW status chart guard
const hasNonZeroPow = computed(() =>
  (powStatusChart.value?.series ?? []).some((s: number) => s > 0)
)

// Milestone chart guard
const hasNonZeroMilestones = computed(() =>
  (milestoneChart.value?.series?.[0]?.data ?? []).some((v: number) => v > 0)
)
```

**B.3 — CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue: Apply guards to VueApexCharts elements**

Add `v-if` guard to each `<VueApexCharts>` component block:

```vue
<!-- Financial Summary chart -->
<VueApexCharts v-if="hasNonZeroFinancials" ... />
<div v-else class="text-center text-body-2 text-grey pa-4">No financial data available</div>

<!-- POW Status chart -->
<VueApexCharts v-if="hasNonZeroPow" ... />
<div v-else class="text-center text-body-2 text-grey pa-4">No POW data available</div>

<!-- Milestone chart -->
<VueApexCharts v-if="hasNonZeroMilestones" ... />
<div v-else class="text-center text-body-2 text-grey pa-4">No milestone data available</div>
```

Apply same pattern to remaining charts (compliance donut, slippage histogram, category bar, utilization gauges).

**Verification:**

| KK-B-V3 | Charts render correctly when project has financial + POW data | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |
| KK-B-V4 | "No [type] data available" text shown inside chart cards when series is all-zero | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |
| KK-B-V5 | Skeleton loader appears on ClientOnly fallback before hydration completes | detail-[id].vue |
| KK-B-V6 | `allEmpty` empty-state card still renders when all data sources are empty | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue |

### Step KK-C — Timeline: Inline List Slicing + Stat Cards

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Risk:** LOW — template v-for modification + new stat-card row

**C.1 — Script setup: stat card computeds**

Add after existing timeline refs:
```typescript
const totalMilestones   = computed(() => project.value?.milestones?.length ?? 0)
const completedMilestones = computed(() => project.value?.milestones?.filter(m => m.status === 'COMPLETED').length ?? 0)
const inProgressMilestones = computed(() => project.value?.milestones?.filter(m => m.status === 'IN_PROGRESS').length ?? 0)
const completionPct = computed(() =>
  totalMilestones.value > 0
    ? Math.round((completedMilestones.value / totalMilestones.value) * 100)
    : 0
)
```

**C.2 — Template: stat-card row**

Add immediately before the 2-col timeline `<v-row>`:

```vue
<v-row dense class="mb-3">
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="outlined" class="pa-3 text-center" rounded="lg">
      <div class="text-h5 font-weight-bold">{{ totalMilestones }}</div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey">Total Milestones</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="outlined" class="pa-3 text-center" rounded="lg">
      <div class="text-h5 font-weight-bold text-success">{{ completedMilestones }}</div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey">Completed ({{ completionPct }}%)</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="outlined" class="pa-3 text-center" rounded="lg">
      <div class="text-h5 font-weight-bold text-primary">{{ inProgressMilestones }}</div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey">In Progress</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="outlined" class="pa-3 text-center" rounded="lg">
      <div class="text-h5 font-weight-bold">{{ workLogEntries.length }}</div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey">Work Log Entries</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

**C.3 — Template: slice inline Milestones list**

Locate:
```vue
v-for="milestone in project.milestones"
```
(or `project?.milestones`)

Change to:
```vue
v-for="milestone in (project?.milestones ?? []).slice(0, 5)"
```

Add count label below the card title when count > 5:
```vue
<span v-if="(project?.milestones?.length ?? 0) > 5" class="text-caption text-grey ms-2">
  (showing 5 of {{ project.milestones.length }})
</span>
```

**C.4 — Template: slice inline Work Log list**

Locate:
```vue
v-for="e in workLogEntries"
```

Change to:
```vue
v-for="e in workLogEntries.slice(0, 5)"
```

Add count label:
```vue
<span v-if="workLogEntries.length > 5" class="text-caption text-grey ms-2">
  (showing 5 of {{ workLogEntries.length }})
</span>
```

### Step KK-D — Documents: Dual-Column Governance Layout

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Risk:** LOW — template structural wrap; no content changes

**D.1 — Template: wrap documents blocks in v-row**

Current documents window-item structure (after KJ-D):
```
<v-window-item value="documents">
  <!-- File Library card (full width) -->
  <!-- <CiDocumentChecklist /> (full width) -->
```

Change to:
```vue
<v-window-item value="documents">
  <v-row>
    <v-col cols="12" md="7">
      <CiDocumentChecklist
        :project-id="project.id"
        :can-edit="canEditAnyTab"
      />
    </v-col>
    <v-col cols="12" md="5">
      <!-- existing File Library v-card block — NO content changes -->
    </v-col>
  </v-row>
```

The CiDocumentChecklist component receives the same props as before. The File Library card block is identical — only its container changes from full-width to a `md="5"` column.

**D.2 — Column widths rationale**

- LEFT `md="7"`: CiDocumentChecklist has complex accordion sections with status chips, progress indicators, and nested v-lists → needs more horizontal space
- RIGHT `md="5"`: File Library is a simple v-list of file entries with download buttons → narrower column is acceptable

| KK-D-V4 | CiDocumentChecklist behavior (accordion open/close, edit interactions) unchanged | detail-[id].vue |
| KK-D-V5 | File Library file download and link opening still functional | detail-[id].vue |

### Governance Directives (Phase KM)

| KM-D2 | `<ClientOnly>` on CiProjectAnalyticsTab MUST wrap the component element. The parent `<v-window-item>` still gets `eager`. Both are required. |
| KM-D3 | Inline milestone/worklog v-for MUST use `.slice(0, 5)`. "View All" button condition: `> 5` items. Dialogs remain unsliced. |
| KM-D4 | `documentsByType` computed is PURELY display grouping. No new API endpoints. The existing `documents` / `documentFiles` arrays are reused. |
| KM-D5 | `editSection('documents')` is the correct semantic target for the Documents tab navigation link. `TAB_MAP` resolves both `gallery` and `documents` to `'documents'` edit tab — functionally equivalent, but `editSection('documents')` is the correct label. |
| KM-D6 | `canEditCurrentProject` is the correct guard for ALL navigation links in detail-[id].vue. Replace `canEditAnyTab` on Gallery and Team navigation links. |
| KM-D7 | KM does NOT touch `edit-[id].vue`. All changes are confined to `detail-[id].vue` and `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`. |

### Step KM-B — Analytics Whitescreen Fix

**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` + `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`
**Risk:** LOW — additive wrapping only; no logic changes

#### KM-B-1: Add `eager` and `<ClientOnly>` to analytics window-item + fix emit

File: `detail-[id].vue` — replace analytics window-item block:

```diff
- <v-window-item value="analytics">
-   <CiProjectAnalyticsTab v-if="project" :project-id="projectId" :project="project" />
- </v-window-item>
+ <v-window-item value="analytics" eager>
+   <ClientOnly>
+     <CiProjectAnalyticsTab
+       v-if="project"
+       :project-id="projectId"
+       :project="project"
+       @go-to-edit="editSection('pow')"
+     />
+   </ClientOnly>
+ </v-window-item>
```

This simultaneously:
- Adds `eager` → forces mount before CSS transition → correct chart width on first render
- Wraps in `<ClientOnly>` → defers ApexCharts to client hydration (no SSR `window`/`document` errors)
- Adds `@go-to-edit` listener → "Add Data via Edit Project" button becomes functional

#### KM-B-2: Fix allEmpty false-positive during POW loading

File: `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` — update allEmpty computed:

```diff
- const allEmpty = computed(() =>
-   !hasPowData.value && !hasFinancialData.value && !hasMilestoneData.value && !hasChecklistData.value,
- )
+ const allEmpty = computed(() =>
+   !powLoading.value &&
+   !hasPowData.value && !hasFinancialData.value && !hasMilestoneData.value && !hasChecklistData.value,
+ )
```

#### KM-B Verification

| ID | Check | Status |
|----|-------|--------|
| KM-B-V1 | Clicking Analytics tab renders charts (no blank/white page) for a project with POW/financial/milestone data | ⬜ |
| KM-B-V2 | Empty-state banner shows only for projects with NO data and NOT during loading | ⬜ |
| KM-B-V3 | "Add Data via Edit Project" button in empty state navigates to edit POW tab | ⬜ |
| KM-B-V4 | No console errors from ApexCharts on analytics tab mount | ⬜ |
| KM-B-V5 | No regression on existing chart types (radialBar, bar, donut) | ⬜ |

Inject the following `<v-row>` immediately above the existing 2-column milestone/worklog row in the timeline window-item:

```vue
<!-- KM-C: Timeline KPI stat cards -->
<v-row dense class="mb-3">
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="tonal" color="primary" class="pa-3 text-center">
      <div class="text-caption">Total Milestones</div>
```

### Step KM-D — Documents Dual-Column + File Type Grouping + Semantic Fix

**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Risk:** LOW — layout restructure + additive computed; no API changes

#### KM-D-1: Add documentsByType computed

In `<script setup>`, near the existing `documentFiles` / `documentLinks` computed refs:

```ts
// KM-D: Group files by documentType for accordion display
const documentsByType = computed(() => {
  const grouped = new Map<string, typeof documentFiles.value>()
  for (const doc of documentFiles.value) {
    const type = doc.documentType || 'Other'
    if (!grouped.has(type)) grouped.set(type, [])
    grouped.get(type)!.push(doc)
  }
  return grouped
})
```

#### KM-D-2: Restructure documents window-item to dual-column

Replace the entire `<v-window-item value="documents">` block with:

```vue
<!-- KM-D: Dual-column documents governance layout -->
<v-window-item value="documents">
  <v-row>
    <!-- LEFT: Governance checklist (7 cols) -->
    <v-col cols="12" md="7">
      <CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="false" />
    </v-col>

    <!-- RIGHT: File attachments grouped by type (5 cols) -->
    <v-col cols="12" md="5">
      <v-card class="mb-4">
        <v-card-title class="d-flex align-center ga-2">
          <v-icon icon="mdi-file-multiple" size="small" />
          Attachments
          <v-spacer />
          <a
            v-if="canEditCurrentProject"
            class="text-caption text-primary text-decoration-none cursor-pointer"
            @click="editSection('documents')"
          >Manage in Edit Project Details ↗</a>
        </v-card-title>
        <v-divider />
        <v-card-text>
          <div v-if="loadingDocuments" class="text-center py-4">
            <v-progress-circular indeterminate color="primary" />
          </div>
          <div v-else-if="documents.length === 0" class="text-center py-6 text-grey">
            <v-icon icon="mdi-file-outline" size="48" color="grey-lighten-1" />
            <p class="mt-2">No attachments yet.</p>
            <p v-if="canEditCurrentProject" class="text-caption">Use "Edit Project Details" to add attachments.</p>
            <p v-else class="text-caption mt-1">Contact the project owner to add attachments.</p>
          </div>
          <template v-else>
            <!-- FILES grouped by documentType in accordion -->
            <v-expansion-panels v-if="documentFiles.length > 0" variant="accordion" class="mb-3">
              <v-expansion-panel
                v-for="[type, docs] in documentsByType"
                :key="type"
                :title="type"
              >
                <v-expansion-panel-text class="pa-0">
                  <v-list density="compact">
                    <v-list-item v-for="doc in docs" :key="doc.id">
                      <template #prepend>
                        <v-icon icon="mdi-file-document" color="primary" />
                      </template>
                      <v-list-item-title>
                        <a :href="doc.filePath" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="text-decoration-none text-primary">
                          {{ doc.fileName }}
                        </a>
                      </v-list-item-title>
                      <v-list-item-subtitle>
                        {{ formatFileSize(doc.fileSize) }} · {{ doc.description || 'No description' }}
                      </v-list-item-subtitle>
                    </v-list-item>
                  </v-list>
                </v-expansion-panel-text>
              </v-expansion-panel>
            </v-expansion-panels>

            <!-- LINKS section preserved -->
            <div v-if="documentLinks.length > 0" :class="{ 'mt-3': documentFiles.length > 0 }">
              <div class="text-overline text-medium-emphasis mb-1">LINKS</div>
              <v-list density="compact">
                <v-list-item v-for="doc in documentLinks" :key="doc.id">
                  <template #prepend>
                    <v-icon icon="mdi-link" color="info" />
                  </template>
                  <v-list-item-title>
                    <a :href="doc.filePath" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="text-decoration-none text-info">
                      {{ doc.fileName }}
                    </a>
                  </v-list-item-title>
                  <v-list-item-subtitle>
                    <v-chip size="x-small" variant="tonal" color="info" class="mr-2">External link</v-chip>
                    {{ doc.description || 'No description' }}
                  </v-list-item-subtitle>
                </v-list-item>
              </v-list>
            </div>
          </template>
        </v-card-text>
      </v-card>
    </v-col>
  </v-row>
</v-window-item>
```

| KM-D-V3 | Files with no documentType appear under "Other" group | ⬜ |

## Phase KN — Timeline Modal Governance, Documents Window Architecture, Attachment Synchronization

**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE (2026-05-13) — All 6 sub-phases implemented in `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Research:** Section 2.152
**Files affected:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` only (all 6 steps)
**Scope:** 2 field-bug fixes (CRITICAL), 4 additive UX features — zero new API endpoints, zero migrations, zero new components

---

### KN-E — Fix Overview Key Documents Field Bugs *(execute FIRST)*

**Priority:** 🔴 CRITICAL — two active bugs silently break the Overview Key Documents panel

**Step 1 — Fix `doc.originalName` → `doc.fileName` in `findDocumentPreview`**

Location: `detail-[id].vue` line ~297

```diff
- const haystack = `${doc.documentType || ''} ${doc.originalName || ''}`.toLowerCase()
+ const haystack = `${doc.documentType || ''} ${doc.fileName || ''}`.toLowerCase()
```

`DocumentItem` interface declares `fileName`, not `originalName`. This fixes the match logic so document names are included in keyword matching.

**Step 2 — Fix `doc.fileUrl` / `doc.linkUrl` → `doc.filePath` in Overview Key Documents download chip**

Location: `detail-[id].vue` lines ~1028–1033 (two occurrences in the Overview Key Documents panel)

```diff
- v-if="findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.fileUrl || findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.linkUrl"
+ v-if="findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.filePath"
```

```diff
- :href="findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.fileUrl || findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.linkUrl || undefined"
+ :href="findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.filePath || undefined"
```

`DocumentItem` declares `filePath`, not `fileUrl`/`linkUrl`. This restores download functionality in the Overview panel.

**Verification:**
| ID | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| KN-E-V1 | Overview Key Documents panel shows document name (not '—') for uploaded docs | ⬜ |
| KN-E-V2 | Download chip renders and opens correct file URL | ⬜ |

### KN-A — Milestone Individual Entry Detail Modal + `dueDate` Field Fix

**Priority:** 🔴 CRITICAL (dueDate bug) + 🟠 HIGH (detail modal)

**Step 1 — Fix `m.dueDate` → `m.targetDate` in milestone collection dialog**

Location: `detail-[id].vue` line ~1659 (inside KJ-C milestones dialog `v-list-item-subtitle`)

```diff
- <span v-if="m.dueDate" class="text-caption text-grey">Due: {{ formatDate(m.dueDate) }}</span>
+ <span v-if="m.targetDate" class="text-caption text-grey">Due: {{ formatDate(m.targetDate) }}</span>
```

`dueDate` is not a declared milestone field; `targetDate` is used throughout the inline list. This prevents blank date display.

**Step 2 — Add milestone detail modal state**

In `<script setup>`, after `milestonesDialogOpen`:

```ts
const selectedMilestone = ref<any>(null)

  selectedMilestone.value = m
  milestoneDetailDialogOpen.value = true
}
```

**Step 3 — Wire click handler on each milestone list item**

In the KJ-C milestones dialog `v-list-item`, add:
```html
@click="openMilestoneDetail(m)" style="cursor:pointer"
```

A new `v-dialog` with `v-model="milestoneDetailDialogOpen"` `max-width="600"` `scrollable` displaying:
- title (headline), status chip, category chip
- Progress bar with percentage
- Date fields: Start Date, Target Date, Actual Date (3-col row)
- Description (full text)
- Remarks (if present)
- Close button

Dialog placed adjacent to the milestones collection dialog in the template (not nested inside it).

**Verification:**

### KN-F — Documents Overflow Window for >10 Files

**Priority:** 🟢 LOW

**Step 1 — Add overflow window state**

```ts
const docsWindowOpen = ref(false)
const docsWindowSearch = ref('')
const docsWindowCategoryFilter = ref('ALL')
const docsWindowSort = ref('newest')
```

**Step 2 — Add "View All Documents" trigger**

In the KM-D Documents tab right column header, add a button rendered only when `documentFiles.length > 10`:
```html
<v-btn v-if="documentFiles.length > 10" variant="text" size="small" @click="docsWindowOpen = true">
  View All ({{ documentFiles.length }})
</v-btn>
```

**Step 3 — Overflow window computed**

```ts
const filteredAllDocs = computed(() => {
  let result = documentFiles.value
  if (docsWindowSearch.value)
    result = result.filter(d => d.fileName.toLowerCase().includes(docsWindowSearch.value.toLowerCase()) || (d.description || '').toLowerCase().includes(docsWindowSearch.value.toLowerCase()))
  if (docsWindowCategoryFilter.value !== 'ALL')
    result = result.filter(d => (d.documentType || 'Other') === docsWindowCategoryFilter.value)
  return docsWindowSort.value === 'newest'
    ? [...result].sort((a, b) => b.createdAt.localeCompare(a.createdAt))
    : [...result].sort((a, b) => a.createdAt.localeCompare(b.createdAt))
})
```

**Step 4 — Overflow dialog**

A `v-dialog` `v-model="docsWindowOpen"` `max-width="1100"` `scrollable` showing:
- Filter bar: search (md=5) + category select (md=4) + sort select (md=3)
- `v-list` iterating `filteredAllDocs` — each item clickable → `openDocDetail(doc)` (reuses KN-D modal)
- Close button in title bar

**Verification:**
| ID | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| KN-F-V1 | "View All" button only appears when documentFiles.length > 10 | ⬜ |
| KN-F-V2 | Overflow window shows all documents with search/filter/sort | ⬜ |
| KN-F-V3 | Clicking entry in overflow window opens KN-D detail modal | ⬜ |
| KN-F-V4 | Projects with ≤10 docs show no "View All" button (no regression) | ⬜ |

## Phase KO — Attachment Synchronization, Document Governance Alignment, Timeline MOV Architecture

**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE (2026-05-13)
**Research Reference:** Section 2.153 (research.md)
**Scope:** Attachment tab governance refactor, KB-E document taxonomy integration, MOV evidence link architecture for milestones and work logs, `WorkLogEntry` interface completion

---

### Step KO-A — New Migration: `construction_mov_entries` Table

**File:** `database/migrations/048_create_construction_mov_entries.sql`

Create the `construction_mov_entries` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE construction_mov_entries (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES construction_projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  related_entity_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (related_entity_type IN ('MILESTONE', 'TIMELINE_ENTRY')),
  related_entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
  mov_link TEXT NOT NULL,
  mov_title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  mov_description TEXT,
  evidence_category VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'other',
  entry_date DATE,
  uploaded_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  verification_status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING' CHECK (verification_status IN ('PENDING', 'VERIFIED', 'REJECTED')),
  remarks TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_mov_entries_project_id ON construction_mov_entries(project_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_mov_entries_entity ON construction_mov_entries(related_entity_type, related_entity_id);
```

**Constraints:**
- No file storage — `mov_link` is URL-only
- `related_entity_id` references either `construction_project_milestones.id` or `construction_timeline_entries.id` (polymorphic — no FK constraint enforced at DB level; validation at service layer)

**Verification:** Migration runs without error; table + indices exist in DB

---

### Step KO-B — Backend Entity + CRUD Endpoints

**New files:**
- `pmo-backend/src/database/entities/construction-mov-entry.entity.ts`
- `pmo-backend/src/coi/dto/create-mov-entry.dto.ts`
- `pmo-backend/src/coi/dto/update-mov-entry.dto.ts`

**Entity fields:** `id, projectId, relatedEntityType, relatedEntityId, movLink, movTitle, movDescription, evidenceCategory, entryDate, uploadedBy, verificationStatus, remarks, createdAt, updatedAt`

**Service methods in `construction-projects.service.ts`:**
```
createMovEntry(projectId, relatedEntityType, relatedEntityId, dto, userId)
listMovEntries(projectId, relatedEntityType, relatedEntityId)
deleteMovEntry(projectId, movEntryId, userId)
```

**Controller routes in `construction-projects.controller.ts`:**
```
POST   /api/construction-projects/:projectId/mov-entries
GET    /api/construction-projects/:projectId/mov-entries?relatedEntityType=MILESTONE&relatedEntityId=:id
DELETE /api/construction-projects/:projectId/mov-entries/:movEntryId
```

**`CreateMovEntryDto` fields:**
```ts
@IsNotEmpty() movLink: string;        // https:// URL
@IsNotEmpty() movTitle: string;
@IsOptional() movDescription?: string;
@IsOptional() evidenceCategory?: string;
@IsOptional() entryDate?: string;     // ISO date
@IsIn(['MILESTONE', 'TIMELINE_ENTRY']) relatedEntityType: string;
@IsUUID() relatedEntityId: string;
@IsOptional() remarks?: string;
```

**Verification:**
- `POST /api/construction-projects/:id/mov-entries` → 201 with created entry
- `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/mov-entries?relatedEntityType=MILESTONE&relatedEntityId=...` → array of entries
- `DELETE /api/construction-projects/:id/mov-entries/:movEntryId` → 204

### Step KO-F — Upgrade Upload Form to Use KB-E Document Types

   ```ts
   )
   ```

## Phase KP — COI UI Propagation Fix: Click Handlers, Thresholds, Accordion Removal

**Research Reference:** `docs/research.md` Section 2.154
**Trigger:** KN/KO features declared complete but not observable in rendered UI
**Scope:** Template-only changes in `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Backend changes required:** None
**New components required:** None

---

### Root Causes Addressed

| Root Cause | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| A | Inline milestone and work-log `v-list-item` elements have no `@click` handler → KN-A/KN-B modals unreachable |
| B | `>5`/`>10` threshold gates hide "View All" buttons for small datasets → modals inaccessible even via dialog path |
| C | Documents tab right column retains `v-expansion-panels` accordion → visual structure unchanged from pre-KN |

---

### Implementation Steps

#### KP-A: Add `@click` handlers to inline Timeline list items

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**Step 1 — Milestone inline list (lines 1328–1331):**
Add `style="cursor: pointer"` and `@click="openMilestoneDetail(milestone)"` to the `<v-list-item>` for each milestone in the main Timeline tab.

```html
<!-- BEFORE -->
<v-list-item
  v-for="(milestone, idx) in (project.milestones || []).slice(0, 5)"
  :key="milestone.id || idx"
  class="border-b"
>

<!-- AFTER -->
<v-list-item
  v-for="(milestone, idx) in (project.milestones || []).slice(0, 5)"
  :key="milestone.id || idx"
  class="border-b"
  style="cursor: pointer"
  @click="openMilestoneDetail(milestone)"
>
```

**Step 2 — Work log inline list (lines 1408–1412):**
Add `style="cursor: pointer"` and `@click="openWorkLogDetail(e)"` to the `<v-list-item>` for each work log entry in the main Timeline tab.

```html
<!-- BEFORE -->
<v-list-item
  v-for="e in workLogEntries.slice(0, 5)"
  :key="e.id"
  lines="three"
  class="border-b"
>

<!-- AFTER -->
<v-list-item
  v-for="e in workLogEntries.slice(0, 5)"
  :key="e.id"
  lines="three"
  class="border-b"
  style="cursor: pointer"
  @click="openWorkLogDetail(e)"
>
```

**Verification:** Click any milestone or work log row in the Timeline tab → KN-A or KN-B detail modal opens → MOV evidence section (KO-D/KO-E) is visible inside the modal.

---

#### KP-B: Lower "View All" thresholds

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

Change all three threshold gates from `> N` to `>= 1` (i.e., always visible when data exists):

```html
<!-- BEFORE -->
<v-btn v-if="(project.milestones?.length ?? 0) > 5" ...>View All</v-btn>
<v-btn v-if="workLogEntries.length > 5" ...>View All</v-btn>
<v-btn v-if="documentFiles.length > 10" ...>View All ({{ documentFiles.length }})</v-btn>

<!-- AFTER -->
<v-btn v-if="(project.milestones?.length ?? 0) > 0" ...>View All</v-btn>
<v-btn v-if="workLogEntries.length > 0" ...>View All</v-btn>
<v-btn v-if="documentFiles.length > 0" ...>View All ({{ documentFiles.length }})</v-btn>
```

**Verification:** With 1+ milestone, "View All" appears → KN-A stat cards + search/filter visible. With 1+ work log entry, "View All" appears. With 1+ document, "View All Attachments" overflow window accessible.

#### KP-C: Replace accordion with flat grouped list in Documents tab

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` (lines 1665–1696)

Replace `v-expansion-panels` accordion with a flat grouped list using inline section headers.

```html
<!-- BEFORE -->
<v-expansion-panels v-if="documentFiles.length > 0" variant="accordion" class="mb-3">
  <v-expansion-panel v-for="[type, docs] in documentsByType" :key="type" :title="type">
    <v-expansion-panel-text class="pa-0">
      <v-list density="compact">
        <v-list-item v-for="doc in docs" :key="doc.id" style="cursor: pointer" @click="openDocDetail(doc)">
          ...
        </v-list-item>
      </v-list>

  </v-expansion-panel>
</v-expansion-panels>

<!-- AFTER -->
<template v-if="documentFiles.length > 0">
  <div v-for="[type, docs] in documentsByType" :key="type" class="mb-3">
    <div class="text-overline text-medium-emphasis px-2 mb-1">{{ type }}</div>
    <v-list density="compact" class="pa-0">
      <v-list-item v-for="doc in docs" :key="doc.id" style="cursor: pointer" @click="openDocDetail(doc)">
        ...same inner content...
      </v-list-item>
    </v-list>
  </div>
</template>
```

**Verification:** Documents tab shows flat grouped sections (no accordion expand/collapse). Clicking any file row opens KN-D document detail modal.

## Phase KQ — Attachment Governance Normalization and Basic Info Cleanup

**Research Reference:** `docs/research.md` Section 2.155
**Trigger:** Operator identified `ideal_infrastructure_image` URL field redundancy (gallery PROFILE images already authoritative) + attachment upload does NOT auto-sync with Document Compliance Checklist + Attachment Tab UX fragmentation
**Scope:** `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`, `edit-[id].vue`, `new.vue`, `construction-projects.service.ts`, `adapters.ts`
**Backend changes required:** Yes — `uploadDocument()` auto-link hook
**New migrations required:** None
**New components required:** None (enhancements to existing structures)

---

### VERIFY_STEP KP

| Step | Expected Outcome | Status |
|---|---|---|
| KP-A | Inline milestone rows clickable → opens KN-A detail modal | ✅ Implemented |
| KP-B | "View All" buttons visible with ≥1 item | ✅ Implemented |
| KP-C | Documents tab shows flat grouped list (no accordion) | ✅ Implemented |
| KP-D | Edit Documents tab shows version + lifecycleStatus + createdAt | Pending operator smoke test |
| KP-E | Upload type dropdown uses KB-E taxonomy | Pending operator smoke test |

**KP declared complete. Proceeding to KQ.**

---

### Governance Directives (KQ)

| # | Directive |
|---|---|
| KQ-GD1 | Gallery PROFILE images are the authoritative source for project architectural imagery. The `ideal_infrastructure_image` URL field is deprecated from the frontend. |
| KQ-GD2 | Every document upload with a KB-E `typeCode` must automatically update the corresponding `ConstructionDocumentChecklist` item. Manual UUID paste is eliminated. |
| KQ-GD3 | Auto-link must NOT overwrite an existing `SUBMITTED`, `APPROVED`, or `REJECTED` checklist status. Only `NOT_SUBMITTED` transitions to `SUBMITTED` automatically. |
| KQ-GD4 | The freeform `docCategory` field is removed from the upload form. The `category` value is auto-derived from the KB-E `groupCode` of the selected `documentType`. |
| KQ-GD5 | The Upload button in the document upload row must be visible and usable at its column width. No `sm="1"` buttons. |
| KQ-GD6 | No regression to KN/KO/KP verified implementations. |

---

### Implementation Steps

#### KQ-A: Remove `ideal_infrastructure_image` URL Field from All Frontend Forms

**Files affected:**
- `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiBasicInfoForm.vue` — remove the URL text field
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` — remove from form state init, hydration, and payload
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue` — remove from form state and payload
- `pmo-frontend/utils/adapters.ts` — remove `idealInfrastructureImage` from adapter mapping (or retain as optional silent passthrough)

**Detailed changes:**

`CiBasicInfoForm.vue`:
- Remove the `<v-text-field>` block for `ideal_infrastructure_image` (line 218 area). This is the only change needed in this component — the field is not used elsewhere in the component logic.

`edit-[id].vue`:
- Remove `ideal_infrastructure_image: ''` from the `form` reactive object
- Remove the hydration line `form.ideal_infrastructure_image = project.idealInfrastructureImage`
- Remove `ideal_infrastructure_image: form.ideal_infrastructure_image` from the save payload object

`new.vue`:
- Remove `ideal_infrastructure_image: ''` from the form state
- Remove `ideal_infrastructure_image: form.ideal_infrastructure_image` from the save payload

`adapters.ts`:
- Remove `idealInfrastructureImage: backend.ideal_infrastructure_image || ''` (or mark `?: string` and leave silently, since backend still returns the field)

**Backend:** No changes. The column remains nullable. Existing projects retain stored values silently.

**Verification:**
- KQ-A-V1: Basic Info tab in Edit Project no longer shows the "Proposed Design / Architectural Rendering URL" text field
- KQ-A-V2: Create new project → Basic Info tab has no URL field
- KQ-A-V3: Save/edit project without the field → no validation error, save succeeds normally

---

#### KQ-B: Backend Auto-Link — Uploaded Documents Sync to Compliance Checklist

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`

**Method:** `uploadDocument()` (lines ~1852–1912)

**Logic to add** (immediately after `await this.em.persistAndFlush(doc)`):

```typescript
// Auto-link to compliance checklist if documentType matches a KB-E typeCode
if (dto.documentType) {
  const docType = await this.constructionDocTypeRepo.findOne({ typeCode: dto.documentType });
  if (docType) {
    const checklistItem = await this.constructionDocChecklistRepo.findOne({
      project: { id: projectId },
      documentType: { id: docType.id },
      submissionStatus: 'NOT_SUBMITTED',
    });
    if (checklistItem) {
      checklistItem.linkedDocumentId = doc.id;
      checklistItem.submissionStatus = 'SUBMITTED';
      await this.em.persistAndFlush(checklistItem);
    }
  }
}
```

**Required repository injections:** Verify `constructionDocTypeRepo` and `constructionDocChecklistRepo` are already injected in the service constructor. If not, add to constructor injection from the module providers.

**Behavior:**
- Upload with `documentType: 'BOQ'` → finds `ConstructionDocumentType` where `typeCode = 'BOQ'` → finds checklist item where `projectId = X AND documentTypeId = found.id AND submissionStatus = 'NOT_SUBMITTED'` → sets `linkedDocumentId = doc.id` + `submissionStatus = 'SUBMITTED'`
- Upload with `documentType: 'attachment'` (legacy) → `findOne({ typeCode: 'attachment' })` returns null → silently skips auto-link
- Checklist item already `SUBMITTED` or `APPROVED` → `findOne(..., submissionStatus: 'NOT_SUBMITTED')` returns null → silently skips → existing governance state preserved

**No new migration needed.** The `linked_document_id` column already exists in `construction_document_checklist`.

**Verification:**
- KQ-B-V1: Upload a document with type `NTP` (or any KB-E typeCode) → navigate to detail page Documents tab → checklist item for NTP shows `SUBMITTED` status with auto-linked document
- KQ-B-V2: Upload another document for same type → checklist item that is already SUBMITTED is NOT overwritten
- KQ-B-V3: Upload with legacy type `attachment` → no checklist update, no error

---

#### KQ-C: Replace Freeform `docCategory` with Auto-Derived Group from Taxonomy

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

**Problem:** `docCategory` is a freeform `<v-text-field>` that sends an unvalidated string to `documents.category`. After KO-F, `documentTypeOptions` loads KB-E taxonomy objects that include `groupCode` and `groupLabel`.

**Fix:**

1. Remove the `<v-text-field>` for `docCategory` from the upload form template.
2. Add a computed or watch that sets `docCategory` automatically when `docType` changes:

```javascript
watch(docType, (newType) => {
  const match = documentTypeOptions.value.find(opt => opt.value === newType);
  docCategory.value = match?.groupCode ?? '';
});
```

This means when the user selects a document type from the KB-E dropdown, `docCategory` is auto-populated with the matching `groupCode` (e.g., `CONTRACT_FOUNDATION`) and submitted silently with the form data. No manual input needed.

3. Remove the `<v-col sm="3">` block containing the `docCategory` text field from the upload row template.
4. The upload row columns change from `6 + 6 / 5 + 3 + 3 + 1` to `6 + 6 / 6 + 4 + 2` or a cleaner distribution:
   - Row 1: File input (`sm="7"`) + Document type dropdown (`sm="5"`)
   - Row 2: Title (`sm="8"`) + Description (`sm="4"`)
   - Row 3: Upload button (`sm="12"` with `block` prop, or right-aligned `sm="3"`)

**Note on section headers:** With `docCategory` now storing `groupCode`, the existing `docsByCategory` computed (which groups by `documentType`) should remain unchanged. The groupCode-based category will be stored correctly and the detail page can now use it if needed.

**Verification:**
- KQ-C-V1: Upload form no longer shows a freeform Category text field
- KQ-C-V2: Selecting document type `BOQ` from dropdown → hidden `docCategory` auto-set to `CONTRACT_FOUNDATION` → backend receives `category: 'CONTRACT_FOUNDATION'`
- KQ-C-V3: Upload button is visible and full-width on the upload row

---

#### KQ-D: Attachment Tab UX — Fix Layout and Add Compliance Summary

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

**Part 1 — Upload row layout fix (critical):**

Change the document upload button from `sm="1"` to a standalone full-row button below the form fields:

```html
<!-- BEFORE: Upload button crammed into sm="1" -->
<v-col sm="5"><!-- title --></v-col>
<v-col sm="3"><!-- category (removed in KQ-C) --></v-col>
<v-col sm="3"><!-- description --></v-col>
<v-col sm="1"><v-btn @click="uploadEditDoc()">Upload</v-btn></v-col>

<!-- AFTER: Reorganized into 2-row upload form -->
<!-- Row 1: File + Type -->
<v-col cols="12" sm="7"><v-file-input .../></v-col>
<v-col cols="12" sm="5"><v-select v-model="docType" .../></v-col>
<!-- Row 2: Title + Description -->
<v-col cols="12" sm="8"><v-text-field v-model="docTitle" label="Title" /></v-col>
<v-col cols="12" sm="4"><v-text-field v-model="docDesc" label="Description" /></v-col>
<!-- Row 3: Upload button (full row, right-aligned) -->
<v-col cols="12" class="d-flex justify-end pt-0">
  <v-btn color="primary" :loading="uploading" @click="uploadEditDoc()">
    <v-icon start>mdi-upload</v-icon> Upload Document
  </v-btn>
</v-col>
```

**Part 2 — Add compliance mini-summary banner in Documents card:**

At the top of the Documents card (before the upload form), add a compact compliance status strip using data from `documentChecklist` (if already fetched for this page) or a dedicated computed:

```html
<v-alert
  v-if="checklistSummary"
  :type="checklistSummary.allSubmitted ? 'success' : 'warning'"
  variant="tonal"
  density="compact"
  class="mb-3"
>
  <span>Document Compliance: {{ checklistSummary.submitted }}/{{ checklistSummary.total }} submitted</span>
  <v-btn size="small" variant="text" :to="`/coi/detail-${projectId}?tab=documents`" class="ml-2">
    View Checklist
  </v-btn>
</v-alert>
```

If `checklistSummary` computation is too complex for the edit page, a simpler alternative: a static hint text noting "Upload documents here — compliance status visible in the Project Details → Documents tab." Either approach resolves the UX gap identified in Section C2 of research.

**Part 3 — Improve section header typography:**

In the Documents list section, replace raw `documentType` string as section header with human-readable `typeLabel` from taxonomy:

```javascript
// Add computed map after documentTypeOptions loads
const typeCodeToLabel = computed(() => {
  const map: Record<string, string> = {};
  documentTypeOptions.value.forEach(opt => {
    map[opt.value] = opt.label || opt.value;
  });
  return map;
});
```

Then in the template, replace `{{ category }}` header with `{{ typeCodeToLabel[category] || category }}`.

**Verification:**
- KQ-D-V1: Upload button is clearly visible and spans multiple columns (not 1-column)
- KQ-D-V2: Document list section headers show human-readable type labels (not raw typeCode strings)
- KQ-D-V3: Compliance summary banner or hint appears at top of Documents card
- KQ-D-V4: Gallery and External Links cards are unmodified and continue to function

---

### Execution Order

```
KQ-A (Basic Info field removal — frontend only, low risk)
  → KQ-B (backend auto-link hook — service layer only, no migration)
  → KQ-C (remove docCategory field + auto-derive groupCode — frontend, low risk)
  → KQ-D (layout fixes + compliance banner — frontend UX, no logic changes)
```

**Rationale:**
- KQ-A is safe to do first (pure removal, no logic dependencies)
- KQ-B is backend-only and does not depend on frontend changes
- KQ-C depends on understanding the upload form to safely remove the field without breaking payload structure
- KQ-D is purely presentational and can be done last

---

### Risk and Regression Matrix

| Risk | Step | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Remove `ideal_infrastructure_image` causes backend DTO validation error | KQ-A | Column is nullable; backend does not require the field; safe to omit from payload |
| Auto-link overwrites APPROVED status | KQ-B | Gate on `submissionStatus: 'NOT_SUBMITTED'` — APPROVED/REJECTED/SUBMITTED are excluded |
| `constructionDocChecklistRepo` not injected in service | KQ-B | Verify injections; add if missing; follows existing MikroORM injection pattern |
| Removing `docCategory` field breaks form submission | KQ-C | `category` becomes auto-derived and submitted silently; `fd.append('category', docCategory.value)` still works with the auto-populated value |
| Upload row restructure breaks `uploadEditDoc()` function | KQ-D | Function reads from refs (`selectedFile`, `docType`, `docTitle`, `docDesc`); layout change doesn't affect ref bindings |
| KN/KO/KP regression | All | Files modified are `edit-[id].vue` and `service.ts` only; `detail-[id].vue` not touched |

## Phase KR — Timeline Modal Architecture, Document Governance Refactor, Attachment Normalization, Backend Constraint Fixes

**Trigger:** Operator post-KQ audit — Timeline modal governance gaps, Documents tab refactor, Attachment UX normalization, backend FK/validation/activity-log errors.
**Research:** `docs/research.md` Section 2.156
**Date:** 2026-05-13

---

### Steps

#### KR-D1 — Fix `removeDocument()` Pre-Delete Checklist Cleanup (BACKEND)

**Problem:** `removeDocument()` calls `em.removeAndFlush(doc)` without clearing `construction_document_checklist.linkedDocumentId`. After KQ-B added auto-linking, more checklist items will have `linkedDocumentId` set — deletion leaves stale references and may cause FK constraint violations.

**Implementation:**
In `construction-projects.service.ts`, `removeDocument()` — before `em.removeAndFlush(doc)`:
1. Clear any checklist items referencing this document:
   ```typescript
   const staleItems = await this.docChecklistRepo.find({ linkedDocumentId: docId });
   for (const item of staleItems) {
     item.linkedDocumentId = undefined;
     item.submissionStatus = 'NOT_SUBMITTED';
     item.submittedAt = undefined;
     item.submittedBy = undefined;
   }
   if (staleItems.length > 0) {
     await this.em.persistAndFlush(staleItems);
   }
   ```
2. Then call `em.removeAndFlush(doc)` as before.

**Files:** `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`
**Migration:** None required.

**Verification:**
- KR-D1-V1: Upload a BOQ document → checklist auto-links (SUBMITTED) → delete the document → checklist reverts to NOT_SUBMITTED
- KR-D1-V2: No FK constraint error on document delete

---

#### KR-D2 — Fix UpdateDto Project Code Validation (BACKEND)

**Problem:** `UpdateConstructionProjectDto extends PartialType(CreateConstructionProjectDto)` inherits `@Matches(/^CP-\d{4}-\d{3}$/)` on `project_code`. When frontend PATCH includes `project_code`, the regex validator fires even for unchanged codes.

**Implementation:**
In `update-construction-project.dto.ts`, override `project_code`:
```typescript
@IsOptional()
@IsString()
project_code?: string;
```

This override shadows the `@Matches()` constraint from the parent. Format validation governs at CREATE only; PATCH accepts any string.

**Files:** `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/dto/update-construction-project.dto.ts`
**Migration:** None required.

**Verification:**
- KR-D2-V1: PATCH project with `project_code: "CP-2024-001"` succeeds
- KR-D2-V2: PATCH project with `project_code: "CP-2023-015"` (unchanged, sent by form) succeeds

---

#### KR-D3 — Apply Migration 044 (OPERATOR ACTION, NOT CODE)

**Problem:** `activity_logs` table does not exist on the running database. Migration `044_create_activity_logs.sql` is in `database/migrations/` but has not been applied.

**Action:** Operator must run the project migration runner:
```bash
npm run migrate
```
This applies all pending migrations including 044 (`CREATE TABLE activity_logs ...`).

**No code changes.** Migration file already exists and is correct.

**Verification:**
- KR-D3-V1: After migration — activity log writes no longer produce `relation "activity_logs" does not exist` warnings in backend logs
- KR-D3-V2: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activity_logs` returns a result (not an error)

---

#### KR-B — Documents Tab: Rename + Key Documents Section (FRONTEND)

**Problem:** (1) Right column header reads "Attachments" — must be "Attachments or Files". (2) No "Key Documents" section for executive documents.

**Implementation in `detail-[id].vue` Documents tab (lines ~1627–1710):**

1. Rename card title from `Attachments` to `Attachments or Files`

2. Add `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` constant and `keyDocuments` computed above the grouped list:
   ```typescript
   const KEY_DOC_TYPECODES = ['PROJECT_PROFILE', 'FEASIBILITY_STUDY', 'HGDG_FORM', 'FLOOR_PLAN', 'POW']
   const keyDocuments = computed(() => documentFiles.value.filter(d => KEY_DOC_TYPECODES.includes(d.documentType || '')))
   ```

3. Insert Key Documents section above the grouped-by-type list:
   - Header: "Key Documents" with `mdi-star` icon
   - List of `keyDocuments` with same click-to-detail pattern as existing file rows
   - Empty state: "No key documents uploaded yet. Upload via Edit Project → Documents tab."
   - Uses same `openDocDetail()` handler

**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Migration:** None required.

**Verification:**
- KR-B-V1: Right column header reads "Attachments or Files"
- KR-B-V2: Key Documents section appears above grouped file list
- KR-B-V3: Uploading a document with typeCode `PROJECT_PROFILE` causes it to appear in Key Documents section
- KR-B-V4: Non-key documents still appear in grouped section below

---

#### KR-C — External Links Button Layout Fix (FRONTEND)

**Problem:** External Links card in edit-[id].vue uses `sm="5"/3/3/1` column layout — the `sm="1"` Add button is too narrow (≈83px). Same bug that was fixed in the Documents upload row (KQ-D) but missed here.

**Implementation in `edit-[id].vue` External Links card (~lines 2323–2363):**

Restructure from single dense row to 2-row layout:
- Row 1: URL field (sm=12 on mobile, md=6 on desktop) + Title (sm=12, md=6)
- Row 2: Description (cols=12 sm=8) + right-aligned "Add Link" button (`justify-end`)

```html
<v-row dense>
  <v-col cols="12" sm="6">
    <v-text-field v-model="linkUrl" label="External URL" ... />
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="12" sm="6">
    <v-text-field v-model="linkTitle" label="Title" ... />
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="12" sm="8">
    <v-text-field v-model="linkDescription" label="Description" ... />
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="12" sm="4" class="d-flex justify-end align-center pt-1">
    <v-btn color="info" :disabled="!linkUrl" :loading="linkSubmitting" prepend-icon="mdi-link-plus" @click="submitEditLink">
      Add Link
    </v-btn>
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Migration:** None required.

**Verification:**
- KR-C-V1: "Add Link" button is fully visible with readable label
- KR-C-V2: External link submission still works correctly

---

#### KR-A — Timeline Work Log Stat Cards (FRONTEND)

**Problem:** KM-C stat cards cover milestones only. Work logs have no equivalent stat summary.

**Implementation in `detail-[id].vue` Timeline tab, after existing KM-C milestone stat cards row (~line 1293):**

Add a second stat-card row for work logs:
- Total Work Log Entries (count of `workLogEntries`)
- Daily Entries
- Weekly Entries
- Monthly + Quarterly Entries (combined or separate)

```html
<!-- KR-A: Work log stat cards -->
<v-row dense class="mb-3">
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="tonal" color="info" class="pa-3 text-center">
      <div class="text-caption">Work Log Entries</div>
      <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">{{ workLogEntries.length }}</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="tonal" color="blue-darken-1" class="pa-3 text-center">
      <div class="text-caption">Daily</div>
      <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">{{ workLogEntries.filter(e => e.entryType === 'DAILY').length }}</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="tonal" color="cyan" class="pa-3 text-center">
      <div class="text-caption">Weekly</div>
      <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">{{ workLogEntries.filter(e => e.entryType === 'WEEKLY').length }}</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="6" md="3">
    <v-card variant="tonal" color="teal" class="pa-3 text-center">
      <div class="text-caption">Monthly / Quarterly</div>
      <div class="text-h6 font-weight-bold">{{ workLogEntries.filter(e => ['MONTHLY','QUARTERLY'].includes(e.entryType)).length }}</div>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Migration:** None required.

**Verification:**
- KR-A-V1: Work log stat cards visible in Timeline tab
- KR-A-V2: Counts are accurate relative to `workLogEntries` data

---

### Execution Order

```
KR-D1 (backend service fix — document delete checklist cleanup)
  → KR-D2 (backend DTO fix — project code validation override)
  → KR-D3 (operator action — run npm run migrate for activity_logs)
  → KR-B (frontend — Documents tab rename + Key Documents section)
  → KR-C (frontend — External Links layout fix)
  → KR-A (frontend — Timeline work log stat cards)
```

**Rationale:**
- Backend fixes first (D1, D2) — resolve active production errors before any UI work
- Migration (D3) is operator action, can run independently but noted here for governance
- B, C, A are frontend-only, low-risk, ordered by governance priority

---

### Files Affected

| File | Steps | Nature |
|------|-------|--------|
| `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts` | KR-D1 | Add pre-delete checklist cleanup before `removeAndFlush` |
| `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/dto/update-construction-project.dto.ts` | KR-D2 | Override `project_code` without `@Matches()` |
| `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` | KR-B, KR-A | Key Documents section + label rename + work log stat cards |
| `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` | KR-C | External Links layout restructure |

No new migration files needed for code steps. No new components. No controller changes.

---

### Risk and Regression Matrix

| Risk | Step | Mitigation |
|------|------|------------|
| Checklist cleanup in `removeDocument()` sets `NOT_SUBMITTED` on an APPROVED checklist item | KR-D1 | Document delete should also clear APPROVED items — APPROVED means the file was accepted; if the file is deleted, re-review is required. Resetting to NOT_SUBMITTED is correct governance behavior. |
| DTO project_code override allows invalid format on PATCH | KR-D2 | Acceptable — PATCH is idempotent; the code was already stored in valid format; a PATCH changing to invalid format would persist but DB column has no DB-level constraint (consistent with current behavior). Frontend validation can be added in a later phase. |
| Key Documents section shows same doc in both Key Documents and grouped section | KR-B | Acceptable — key docs appear in Key section first for prominence; appearing in grouped section below provides categorization context. No de-duplication needed. |
| Work log stat cards add height to Timeline tab | KR-A | Acceptable trade-off for governance completeness; cards are compact density |

---

### Verification Matrix

| Check | Step | Expected |
|-------|------|----------|
| KR-D1-V1 | KR-D1 | Upload document → auto-link → delete document → checklist reverts to NOT_SUBMITTED |
| KR-D1-V2 | KR-D1 | No FK violation error on document delete |
| KR-D2-V1 | KR-D2 | Edit Project form PATCH succeeds with unchanged project_code in payload |
| KR-D3-V1 | KR-D3 | Activity log writes no longer fail after migration applied |
| KR-B-V1 | KR-B | Documents tab right column header: "Attachments or Files" |
| KR-B-V2 | KR-B | Key Documents section visible above grouped list |
| KR-B-V3 | KR-B | PROJECT_PROFILE upload appears in Key Documents |
| KR-C-V1 | KR-C | "Add Link" button fully visible, not truncated |
| KR-A-V1 | KR-A | Work log stat cards visible in Timeline tab |

---

*Phase KR — COMPLETE. Implemented 2026-05-13.*

---

## Phase KS — MikroORM Migration Backfill (SQL 044, 045, 048)

**Trigger:** Operator confirmed `npm run migration:up` did not apply `activity_logs`. Root cause: SQL migrations 044, 045, 048 were never ported to the MikroORM TypeScript migration system (baseline anchored at migration-043 only).
**Research:** Section 2.157 (inline — findings are clear and contained)
**Date:** 2026-05-13

---

### Context

| SQL File | Content | MikroORM Coverage |
|----------|---------|-------------------|
| 044 | CREATE TABLE `activity_logs` + indexes | ❌ Missing |
| 045 | INSERT INTO `roles` — Auditor role seed | ❌ Missing |
| 046 | ALTER TABLE `construction_milestones` — start_date, progress, category | ✅ Covered by `Migration20260507180000` |
| 047 | ALTER TABLE `construction_pow_items` — progress, sort_order, etc. | ✅ Covered by `Migration20260507190000` |
| 048 | CREATE TABLE `construction_mov_entries` + indexes | ❌ Missing |

---

### Steps

#### KS-1 — Create `Migration20260514030000_CreateActivityLogs.ts`

Port SQL 044 into a proper MikroORM TypeScript migration.

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260514030000_CreateActivityLogs.ts`

**Verification:** `npm run migration:up` applies cleanly; `activity_logs` table exists in DB; `ActivityLogService` writes succeed.

---

#### KS-2 — Create `Migration20260514040000_SeedAuditorRole.ts`

Port SQL 045 (`INSERT INTO roles … Auditor`) into a MikroORM migration.

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260514040000_SeedAuditorRole.ts`

**Verification:** Auditor row exists in `roles` table after migration.

---

#### KS-3 — Create `Migration20260514050000_CreateConstructionMovEntries.ts`

Port SQL 048 into a proper MikroORM TypeScript migration.

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260514050000_CreateConstructionMovEntries.ts`

**Verification:** `construction_mov_entries` table exists; MOV CRUD endpoints respond without "relation does not exist" error.

---

### Execution Order

```
KS-1 (activity_logs table + indexes)
  → KS-2 (auditor role seed — depends on activity_logs for conceptual completeness)
  → KS-3 (construction_mov_entries table + indexes)
  → Operator runs: npm run migration:up (inside pmo-backend/)
```

### Files Affected

| File | Step |
|------|------|
| `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260514030000_CreateActivityLogs.ts` | KS-1 (new) |
| `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260514040000_SeedAuditorRole.ts` | KS-2 (new) |
| `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260514050000_CreateConstructionMovEntries.ts` | KS-3 (new) |

---

*Phase KS plan complete. All 3 migration files created. Operator must run: `npm run migration:up` inside `pmo-backend/`.*

---

## Phase KT — Public COI Detail Page Enhancement

**Phase:** KT
**Status:** ⬜ PENDING OPERATOR AUTHORIZATION
**Research:** Section 2.158 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** Enhance `pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue` to display all available narrative/profile/team data from `findPublicOne()`; add public POW endpoint and table.

### Context

The public COI detail page was scaffolded during KA–KS but never filled with available data. `findPublicOne()` returns `summary`, `scope`, `facilities`, `objectives`, `keyFeatures`, `strategicAlignment`, `outputIndicators`, `outcomeIndicators`, and `assignedUsers` — none of which are rendered. KT closes this gap. POW items require a new `@Public()` controller route (KT-D).

### Sub-phases

---

#### KT-A — Narrative Fields (Summary, Scope, Facilities)

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue`

Add a "Project Narrative" card to the left column (after the Project Information card) with three labelled sections:
- **Summary** — `project.summary` (show only if non-empty)
- **Scope** — `project.scope` (show only if non-empty)
- **Facilities** — `project.facilities` (show only if non-empty)

Each uses a `<p class="text-caption text-grey">` label + `<p>` body. The card itself renders only when at least one of the three is non-empty. No layout restructuring of existing cards.

**Verification:** Project with populated narrative fields shows the "Project Narrative" card; project with all-empty narrative fields shows nothing.

---

#### KT-B — Strategic Profile (Objectives, Key Features, Strategic Alignment)

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue`

Add a "Strategic Profile" card to the right column (above Building Details) with:
- **Strategic Alignment** — `project.strategicAlignment` (show if non-empty)
- **Objectives** — `project.objectives` as `<v-chip>` list (show if array is non-empty)
- **Key Features** — `project.keyFeatures` as `<v-chip>` list (show if array is non-empty)
- **Output Indicators** — `project.outputIndicators` as `<v-chip>` list (show if non-empty)
- **Outcome Indicators** — `project.outcomeIndicators` as `<v-chip>` list (show if non-empty)

Card renders only when at least one field is populated. Use `v-if` guards per group.

**Verification:** Projects with strategic profile data show the card; empty-data projects show nothing.

---

#### KT-C — Team Section (Assigned Users)

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue`

Add a "Project Team" card to the right column (below Contractor & Funding) with:
- `v-list` of `project.assignedUsers` entries
- Each row: name (`v-list-item-title`) + role/department as subtitle
- Render only when `project.assignedUsers.length > 0`

**Verification:** Projects with assigned personnel show the team card; projects without personnel show nothing.

---

#### KT-D — Public POW Endpoint + Table

**Type:** Backend + Frontend

**Backend step (KT-D1):**
- File: `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/public-construction.controller.ts`
- Add route: `@Get(':id/pow-items')` decorated with `@Public()`
- Handler:
  ```ts
  async findPowItems(@Param('id') id: string) {
    await this.constructionProjectsService.findPublicOne(id); // publication check
    return this.constructionProjectsService.findPowItems(id);
  }
  ```
- Import `@Public()` is already in the file

**Frontend step (KT-D2):**
- File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue`
- Add `powItems` ref and `fetchPowItems()` function calling `GET /api/public/construction-projects/${projectId}/pow-items`
- Call `fetchPowItems()` in `onMounted()`
- Add "Program of Works" `<v-data-table>` card to the left column (after Milestones) with columns: Item, Unit, Quantity, Unit Cost, Total Cost, Status
- POW items use `ConstructionPowItem` shape: `{ id, description, unit, quantity, unitCost, totalCost, status }`

**Backend adapter note:** `findPowItems` returns ORM entity array. Frontend maps: `description`, `unit`, `quantity`, `unit_cost`/`unitCost`, `total_cost`/`totalCost`, `status`. Check column naming from entity.

**Verification:** Public detail page shows POW table with items for PUBLISHED projects; returns empty state for projects with no POW.

---

### Execution Order

```
KT-A (narrative card — frontend, left column)
  → KT-B (strategic profile card — frontend, right column)
  → KT-C (team card — frontend, right column)
  → KT-D1 (public POW controller route — backend)
  → KT-D2 (POW table — frontend)
```

### Files Affected

| File | Step |
|------|------|
| `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue` | KT-A, KT-B, KT-C, KT-D2 (modify) |
| `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/public-construction.controller.ts` | KT-D1 (modify) |

---

*Phase KT plan complete.*



---

## Phase KU � Activity Log Frontend

**Phase:** KU
**Status:** ? COMPLETE
**Research:** Section 2.160 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** Surface the existing activity log backend (KS-1: table, KS-2: Auditor role) in the frontend. Three frontend-only deliverables; zero backend changes.

### Context

`ActivityLogController` at `GET /api/activity-logs` and `GET /api/activity-logs/:entityType/:entityId` is fully functional (SuperAdmin/Admin/Auditor access). `ActivityLogService.logAction()` is called ~25� in the COI service via `fireLog()`. The `activity_logs` table and Auditor role exist (KS-1 + KS-2). Nothing is displayed in the frontend.

### Sub-phases

---

#### KU-A � Per-Project Audit Trail Section (Admin Detail Page)

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

In the overview tab, append an "Audit Trail" card at the bottom of the left column after the existing content. The section:
- Fetches `GET /api/construction-projects/../activity-logs` � No. Use the existing general endpoint: `GET /api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/:projectId?pageSize=20`
- Renders a `v-timeline` or compact `v-data-table` of the 20 most recent log entries
- Each row: icon per action, user name, action chip, relative timestamp, metadata preview
- Lazy load: not fetched on mount � triggered by a "Load Audit Trail" button (governance users only) or auto-loaded only for Admin/SuperAdmin/Auditor
- Action color map: CREATE?green, UPDATE?blue, DELETE?red, PUBLISH/SUBMIT?primary, REJECT?orange, UPLOAD?teal, REMOVE_ATTACHMENT?red, WITHDRAW?grey

**Data shape received:**
```
{ data: [{ id, userEmail, userName, action, entityType, entityId, metadata, createdAt }], total, page, pageSize }
```

**Visibility guard:** render the section only when `canManage` (admin/superadmin) is true � reuse the existing `canManage` computed in `detail-[id].vue`.

**Verification:** Admin viewing a project detail page sees "Audit Trail" section; clicking load shows paginated entries; Staff user does not see the section.

---

#### KU-B � Activity Log Viewer Page

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/activity-logs.vue` (new)

A dedicated page showing all COI construction project activity logs:
- Route: `/coi/activity-logs`
- Fetches `GET /api/activity-logs?entityType=CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT&page=N&pageSize=50`
- Optional filter: project ID input (manual UUID or future project picker)
- Columns: Timestamp, User, Action, Entity ID (abbreviated), Metadata preview
- Pagination using `v-pagination` or next/prev buttons
- Back button ? `/coi`
- `definePageMeta({ middleware: 'auth' })` � no role check on page itself; backend enforces 403 for non-authorized users; frontend shows 403 message if API returns error

**Verification:** SuperAdmin can navigate to `/coi/activity-logs`, sees paginated log; page shows "No logs" empty state when table is empty; unauthorized user gets redirected or sees error.

---

#### KU-C � Nav Item

**File:** `pmo-frontend/layouts/default.vue`

In the admin section (the block that pushes to `items` when `isSuperAdmin || isAdmin`), add:
```ts
items.push({ title: 'COI Activity Logs', icon: 'mdi-history', to: '/coi/activity-logs' })
```
Place after "Pending Reviews" item, before "User Management".

**Verification:** Admin/SuperAdmin sees "COI Activity Logs" in the sidebar. Staff user does not.

---

### Execution Order

```
KU-A (audit trail section in detail-[id].vue � safe append, no restructuring)
  ? KU-B (new page � standalone file)
  ? KU-C (nav item in layout � one-liner insert)
```

### Files Affected

| File | Step |
|------|------|
| `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` | KU-A (modify � append to overview tab) |
| `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/activity-logs.vue` | KU-B (new) |
| `pmo-frontend/layouts/default.vue` | KU-C (modify � one line insert) |

---

*Phase KU plan complete.*

## Phase KV � COI Attachment Enterprise Refactor, Document Governance Normalization, Timeline Filter Architecture

**Phase:** KV
**Status:** ? COMPLETE (2026-05-14)
**Research:** Section 2.161 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** Unified Timeline filter, Document Compliance Checklist file access + accordion metadata + group remarks, Attachment Tab enterprise layout refactor, "Others" tab creation, fullscreen modal per section.

---

### Context

Seven continuity gaps identified in Section 2.161:
1. Timeline filter is fragmented (8 separate refs, two unlinked dialogs)
2. Checklist `linkedDocumentId` stored but never rendered as a download link
3. Accordion header shows "approved count" � should show "submitted count" + metadata
4. No per-group remarks field for evaluator feedback
5. Attachment tab has no layout hierarchy (Key Docs, Gallery, Compliance, Others are flat)
6. No "Others" tab for supplementary structured data
7. Gallery and Key Documents upload forms are sequentially stacked (not 2-column)

---

### Governance Directives (Phase KV)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| KV-D1 | `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` must be extracted to `pmo-frontend/utils/coiFormState.ts` and imported by both `detail-[id].vue` and `edit-[id].vue`. No duplication. |
| KV-D2 | `CiDocumentChecklist` receives `documents: DocumentItem[]` as a new prop. The component must NOT fetch documents itself � the parent passes them. No new API calls inside the component. |
| KV-D3 | Per-group remarks stored as JSONB `document_checklist_remarks` on `construction_projects`. Backend sub-route: `PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/document-remarks`. Payload: `{ groupCode: string, remarks: string }`. |
| KV-D4 | "Others" tab in edit page moves `statusUpdateRows`, `readinessDocRows`, `signatoryRows` OUT of `CiBasicInfoForm`. The arrays remain as page-level refs in `edit-[id].vue`. The `CiBasicInfoForm` props for these arrays are removed. |
| KV-D5 | Fullscreen dialogs reuse the same refs/computed as inline sections. No state duplication. |
| KV-D6 | Compliance Checklist is embedded in the edit Attachments tab as `CiDocumentChecklist` with `canEdit` prop. Edit-page `canEditCurrentProject` serves as the `canEdit` value. |
| KV-D7 | `sharedDateFrom` / `sharedDateTo` / `sharedPeriod` are page-level refs in `detail-[id].vue`. Both `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog` computeds derive from them in addition to their existing collection-specific filters. |

---

### Sub-phases

---

#### KV-A � Timeline Unified Filter

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**KV-A1 � Shared filter refs (page level):**
Add three new refs:
```ts
const sharedDateFrom = ref('')
const sharedDateTo = ref('')
const sharedPeriod = ref<'ALL' | 'DAILY' | 'WEEKLY' | 'MONTHLY' | 'QUARTERLY'>('ALL')
```

Add a `periodBounds` computed that returns `{ from: string, to: string }` for non-ALL period values:
- DAILY: today (ISO date)
- WEEKLY: Monday to Sunday of current week
- MONTHLY: 1st to last day of current month
- QUARTERLY: first month of current quarter to last day of last month of quarter (PH fiscal = calendar year; Q1=Jan�Mar, Q2=Apr�Jun, Q3=Jul�Sep, Q4=Oct�Dec)

When `sharedPeriod !== 'ALL'`, the bounds override `sharedDateFrom`/`sharedDateTo` in the filtered computeds.

**KV-A2 � Update `filteredMilestones` computed:**
After existing status/search filters, apply shared date logic:
```ts
const effectiveDateFrom = sharedPeriod.value !== 'ALL' ? periodBounds.value.from : sharedDateFrom.value
const effectiveDateTo   = sharedPeriod.value !== 'ALL' ? periodBounds.value.to   : sharedDateTo.value
if (effectiveDateFrom) items = items.filter(m => (m.targetDate ?? '') >= effectiveDateFrom)
if (effectiveDateTo)   items = items.filter(m => (m.targetDate ?? '') <= effectiveDateTo)
```
Remove the old `milestoneFilterDateFrom`/`milestoneFilterDateTo` refs (replaced by shared state).

**KV-A3 � Update `filteredWorkLog` computed:**
Apply same shared date logic using `e.entryDate` as the comparison field. Remove old `wlogFilterDateFrom`/`wlogFilterDateTo` refs.

**KV-A4 � Timeline tab filter bar (template):**
In the Timeline tab body (before the milestone preview card and work log preview card), add a compact filter bar:
```html
<div class="d-flex align-center flex-wrap ga-2 mb-4 px-1">
  <span class="text-caption font-weight-medium text-grey">Period:</span>
  <v-btn-toggle v-model="sharedPeriod" density="compact" variant="outlined" divided>
    <v-btn value="ALL" size="small">All</v-btn>
    <v-btn value="DAILY" size="small">Daily</v-btn>
    <v-btn value="WEEKLY" size="small">Weekly</v-btn>
    <v-btn value="MONTHLY" size="small">Monthly</v-btn>
    <v-btn value="QUARTERLY" size="small">Quarterly</v-btn>
  </v-btn-toggle>
  <template v-if="sharedPeriod === 'ALL'">
    <v-text-field v-model="sharedDateFrom" type="date" label="From" density="compact" variant="outlined" hide-details style="max-width: 160px" />
    <v-text-field v-model="sharedDateTo" type="date" label="To" density="compact" variant="outlined" hide-details style="max-width: 160px" />
    <v-btn v-if="sharedDateFrom || sharedDateTo" icon="mdi-close-circle" variant="text" size="small" @click="sharedDateFrom=''; sharedDateTo=''" />
  </template>
  <v-chip v-else size="x-small" variant="tonal" color="primary" class="ml-1">
    {{ periodBounds.from }} � {{ periodBounds.to }}
  </v-chip>
</div>
```

**KV-A5 � Remove per-dialog date filter rows:**
Remove `milestoneFilterDateFrom`/`milestoneFilterDateTo` inputs from inside the milestone dialog (KN-C). Remove `wlogFilterDateFrom`/`wlogFilterDateTo` inputs from work log dialog (KN-C). Date filtering now happens at tab level, not inside dialogs.

**Verification:**
- Selecting "Monthly" filters both milestone list and work log list to current month entries
- Custom date range applies to both collections
- Clearing filter restores both lists
- Collection-specific search + status/type filters continue to work inside each dialog

---

#### KV-B � Checklist File Access (Linked Document Download)

**Type:** Frontend only
**Files:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiDocumentChecklist.vue`, `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**KV-B1 � Add `documents` prop to `CiDocumentChecklist`:**
```ts
interface Props {
  projectId: string
  canEdit: boolean
  documents?: DocumentItem[]  // optional; defaults to []
}
// Add DocumentItem interface (same shape as detail page):
interface DocumentItem {
  id: string
  fileName: string
  filePath: string
  documentType?: string
  mimeType?: string
}
```

**KV-B2 � Add `linkedDoc()` helper:**
```ts
function linkedDoc(item: ChecklistItem): DocumentItem | null {
  if (!item.linkedDocumentId || !props.documents?.length) return null
  return (props.documents ?? []).find(d => d.id === item.linkedDocumentId) ?? null
}
```

**KV-B3 � Render download chip in checklist item:**
In the per-item template, after the status chip row, add:
```html
<v-chip
  v-if="linkedDoc(item)"
  size="x-small"
  color="primary"
  variant="tonal"
  prepend-icon="mdi-download"
  :href="linkedDoc(item)!.filePath"
  target="_blank"
  rel="noopener"
  tag="a"
  class="mt-1"
>
  {{ linkedDoc(item)!.fileName }}
</v-chip>
```

**KV-B4 � Replace UUID text field with document picker in edit dialog:**
In the checklist edit dialog, replace the `v-text-field` for `linked_document_id` with a `v-select`:
```html
<v-select
  v-model="form.linked_document_id"
  label="Link Uploaded Document"
  :items="props.documents ?? []"
  :item-title="(d: DocumentItem) => d.fileName"
  item-value="id"
  clearable
  variant="outlined"
  density="comfortable"
  hint="Select a file from the Attachments tab"
  persistent-hint
/>
```

**KV-B5 � Pass `documentFiles` prop in `detail-[id].vue`:**
In the `<CiDocumentChecklist>` component call, add `:documents="documentFiles"`.

**Verification:**
- Checklist item with linked document shows a download chip with file name
- Clicking chip opens file in new tab
- Checklist edit dialog shows document picker dropdown populated with project's documents
- Selecting a file updates `linked_document_id`; saving PATCH reflects on reload

---

#### KV-C � Accordion Metadata (Submission Count + File Count + Latest Date)

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiDocumentChecklist.vue`

**KV-C1 � Update `groupedItems` computed to include per-group metadata:**
```ts
const groupedItems = computed(() => {
  // ... existing grouping logic ...
  return Object.values(groups).sort(...).map(g => ({
    ...g,
    submittedCount: g.items.filter(i => i.submissionStatus !== 'NOT_SUBMITTED').length,
    linkedFileCount: g.items.filter(i => !!i.linkedDocumentId).length,
    missingCount: g.items.filter(i => i.submissionStatus === 'NOT_SUBMITTED').length,
    latestSubmittedAt: g.items
      .filter(i => i.submittedAt)
      .map(i => i.submittedAt!)
      .sort()
      .at(-1) ?? null,
  }))
})
```

**KV-C2 � Update accordion header:**
Replace the single chip with a metadata row:
```html
<v-expansion-panel-title>
  <div>
    <div class="font-weight-medium">{{ g.groupLabel }}</div>
    <div class="d-flex align-center flex-wrap ga-1 mt-1">
      <v-chip size="x-small" variant="tonal" :color="g.submittedCount === g.items.length ? 'success' : 'info'">
        {{ g.submittedCount }} / {{ g.items.length }} submitted
      </v-chip>
      <v-chip v-if="g.linkedFileCount" size="x-small" variant="tonal" color="primary">
        <v-icon start size="x-small">mdi-paperclip</v-icon>{{ g.linkedFileCount }} files
      </v-chip>
      <v-chip v-if="g.missingCount" size="x-small" variant="tonal" color="warning">
        {{ g.missingCount }} missing
      </v-chip>
      <span v-if="g.latestSubmittedAt" class="text-caption text-grey ml-1">
        � Last: {{ new Date(g.latestSubmittedAt).toLocaleDateString('en-PH', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' }) }}
      </span>
    </div>
  </div>
</v-expansion-panel-title>
```

- Accordion header shows submission count, not approval count
- Linked file count chip appears when files are linked
- Missing count chip appears for groups with unsubmitted items
- Latest submission date shows correctly

#### KV-D � Per-Group Remarks (Backend Migration + Sub-route + UI)

**Type:** Backend + Frontend
**Risk:** Medium � requires DB migration and new backend route

**KV-D1 � Database migration:**
File: `database/migrations/049_add_document_checklist_remarks.sql`
```sql
ALTER TABLE construction_projects
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS document_checklist_remarks JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb;
```

**KV-D2 � Backend entity + service + controller:**

Entity (`construction-project.entity.ts`): Add `@Property({ type: 'jsonb', default: '{}' }) documentChecklistRemarks: Record<string, string> = {}`

Service (`construction-projects.service.ts`): Add method:
```ts
async updateDocumentRemarks(projectId: string, groupCode: string, remarks: string, userId: string) {
  const project = await this.projectRepo.findOneOrFail({ id: projectId })
  project.documentChecklistRemarks = { ...project.documentChecklistRemarks, [groupCode]: remarks }
  await this.em.flush()
  this.fireLog(projectId, userId, 'UPDATE', { action: 'update_document_remarks', groupCode })
}
```

Controller: Add `PATCH :id/document-remarks` route (Roles: Admin, Staff):
```ts
@Patch(':id/document-remarks')
@Roles(Role.ADMIN, Role.STAFF)
async updateDocumentRemarks(@Param('id') id: string, @Body() body: { groupCode: string; remarks: string }, @CurrentUser() user: UserEntity) {
  return this.service.updateDocumentRemarks(id, body.groupCode, body.remarks, user.id, user)
}
```

`findOne()` response must include `documentChecklistRemarks` � verify it is serialized in `mapToDetail()` or equivalent service method.

**KV-D3 � CiDocumentChecklist UI (remarks field):**
Add new prop: `groupRemarks?: Record<string, string>` and `canEditRemarks?: boolean`.

In each accordion panel, add a remarks textarea at the bottom of the expanded section:
```html
<div class="mt-3 pa-3 bg-grey-lighten-5 rounded">
  <p class="text-caption text-grey mb-1">Evaluator Remarks / Deficiency Notes</p>
  <v-textarea
    v-if="canEditRemarks"
    :model-value="groupRemarks?.[g.groupCode] ?? ''"
    placeholder="Note any deficiencies, missing documents, or compliance observations�"
    rows="2"
    variant="outlined"
    density="compact"
    hide-details
    @blur="(e: Event) => emitRemarks(g.groupCode, (e.target as HTMLTextAreaElement).value)"
  />
  <p v-else class="text-body-2">{{ groupRemarks?.[g.groupCode] || '�' }}</p>
</div>
```

`emitRemarks()` calls the parent-provided handler (passed via emit or prop function):
```ts
const emit = defineEmits<{ remarksUpdate: [groupCode: string, remarks: string] }>()
function emitRemarks(groupCode: string, value: string) {
  emit('remarksUpdate', groupCode, value)
}
```

In `detail-[id].vue`: handle `@remarks-update` event by calling `PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/document-remarks`.
In `edit-[id].vue`: same handler.

Both pages pass `:group-remarks="project.documentChecklistRemarks"` and `:can-edit-remarks="canEditCurrentProject"`.

**Verification:**
- Migration runs successfully (`ALTER TABLE` idempotent)
- Admin typing in remarks textarea and blurring saves on blur (PATCH call fires)
- Remarks appear on re-load
- Read-only view (detail page, non-admin) shows remarks as text paragraph
- Empty remarks show "�" placeholder
