## Phase KW � COI Timeline Governance, Attachment Architecture, Personnel Authorization, Workflow Continuity Refactor

**Phase:** KW
**Status:** ? PENDING OPERATOR AUTHORIZATION
**Research:** Section 2.162 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** KV bug fixes, Overview Recent Gallery removal, Timeline filter dialog visibility, Manual document naming, Per-tab Save, Others tab rework (monitoring space), MOV evidence aggregation, Personnel tab-level authorization.

---

### Context

VERIFY_STEP KV (Section 2.162) identified 2 HIGH bugs (gallery inner tabs unlinked, docFile collision) and 4 NOT IMPLEMENTED features. KW corrects all regressions and delivers the next governance layer.

---

### Governance Directives (Phase KW)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| KW-D1 | Gallery inner tabs MUST have a shared `v-model`. Add `const galleryActiveTab = ref<'profile' or 'general'>('profile')` and bind to both `v-tabs` and `v-window` in Attachments tab. |
| KW-D2 | Key Documents upload MUST use separate refs (`keyDocFile`, `keyDocType`, `keyDocTitle`, `keyDocUploading`) isolated from Other Attachments refs (`docFile`, `docType`, `docTitle`). |
| KW-D3 | Key Documents upload form primary field is free-text `keyDocTitle`. `keyDocType` is optional classification (defaults to 'other'). |
| KW-D4 | `recentGalleryImages` card in `detail-[id].vue` Overview MUST be removed. Only Profile Images strip (max 3) remains. |
| KW-D5 | Per-tab Save button calls existing `handleSubmit()` � no new backend endpoint required. |
| KW-D6 | Others tab rework: existing admin rows (statusUpdateRows, readinessDocRows, signatoryRows) demoted to collapsed sub-panel. New monitoring sections (Incidents, Risk, Escalations) added as JSONB arrays. |
| KW-D7 | New JSONB fields (`incident_log`, `risk_register`, `escalation_records`) added via MikroORM migration `Migration20260514070000_AddProjectMonitoringFields`. |
| KW-D8 | `useCoiAccess()` extended with per-tab flags: `canEditMilestones`, `canEditWorkLog`, `canEditFinancial`, `canEditPow`, `canEditPersonnel`. |

---

### Sub-phases

---

#### KW-A � Bug Fixes: Gallery Tabs + DocFile Collision

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Risk:** Low � direct defect corrections from KV

**KW-A1 � Gallery inner tabs fix:**

Add to script:
```ts
const galleryActiveTab = ref<'profile' | 'general'>('profile')
```

Bind `v-model="galleryActiveTab"` to the existing `v-tabs` element and the `v-window` element inside the Gallery card of the Attachments tab. Both already have correct `value` attributes on child elements � only the model link is missing.

**KW-A2 � Key Documents separate upload refs:**

Add refs:
```ts
const keyDocFile      = ref<File | null>(null)
const keyDocType      = ref('')
const keyDocTitle     = ref('')
const keyDocUploading = ref(false)
```

Add `uploadKeyDoc()` function (mirrors `uploadEditDoc()` but uses key-doc refs, sends `documentType: keyDocType.value || 'other'`, resets key-doc refs on success, calls `fetchDocs()`):
```ts
async function uploadKeyDoc() {
  if (!keyDocFile.value) return
  if (keyDocFile.value.size > 20 * 1024 * 1024) { toast.error('File exceeds 20 MB'); return }
  keyDocUploading.value = true
  try {
    const fd = new FormData()
    fd.append('file', keyDocFile.value)
    fd.append('documentType', keyDocType.value || 'other')
    if (keyDocTitle.value) fd.append('title', keyDocTitle.value)
    await api.upload(`/api/construction-projects/${projectId}/documents`, fd)
    toast.success('Key document uploaded')
    keyDocFile.value = null
    keyDocType.value = ''
    keyDocTitle.value = ''
    await fetchDocs()
  } catch (err: unknown) {
    toast.error((err as { message?: string })?.message || 'Upload failed')
  } finally {
    keyDocUploading.value = false
  }
}
```

Update Key Documents upload form template to use `keyDocFile`, `keyDocType`, `keyDocTitle`, `keyDocUploading`, `uploadKeyDoc`.

**Verification:**
- KW-A-V1: Clicking "Profile" tab shows only PROFILE images; "General" shows only non-PROFILE
- KW-A-V2: Uploading from Key Docs does not affect Other Attachments type selection

#### KW-F � Others Tab Rework (Project Monitoring Space)

**Type:** Backend + Frontend
**Risk:** Medium � MikroORM migration must run before UI is functional

**KW-F1 � MikroORM migration:**

File: `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260514070000_AddProjectMonitoringFields.ts`

```ts
import { Migration } from '@mikro-orm/migrations';

export class Migration20260514070000_AddProjectMonitoringFields extends Migration {
  override async up(): Promise<void> {
    this.addSql(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_projects
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS incident_log       JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS risk_register      JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS escalation_records JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb;
    `);
  }

  override async down(): Promise<void> {
    this.addSql(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_projects
        DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS incident_log,
        DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS risk_register,
        DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS escalation_records;
    `);
  }
}
```

**KW-F2 � Entity fields:**

Add to `ConstructionProject` entity (`construction-project.entity.ts`):
```ts
@Property({ columnType: 'jsonb', default: '[]' }) incidentLog: any[] = []
@Property({ columnType: 'jsonb', default: '[]' }) riskRegister: any[] = []
@Property({ columnType: 'jsonb', default: '[]' }) escalationRecords: any[] = []
```

**KW-F3 � Adapter + UIProjectDetail interface:**

In `UIProjectDetail`, add:
```ts
incidentLog: Record<string, any>[]
riskRegister: Record<string, any>[]
escalationRecords: Record<string, any>[]
```

In `adaptProjectDetail()`:
```ts
incidentLog: backend.incident_log || [],
riskRegister: backend.risk_register || [],
escalationRecords: backend.escalation_records || [],
```

**KW-F4 � edit-[id].vue Others tab restructure:**

Add page-level refs:
```ts
const incidentLogRows    = ref<Array<{ date: string; severity: string; description: string; status: string }>>([])
const riskRegisterRows   = ref<Array<{ risk: string; likelihood: string; impact: string; mitigation: string; status: string }>>([])
const escalationRows     = ref<Array<{ escalatedTo: string; date: string; issue: string; resolution: string }>>([])
```

Add row helpers: `addIncident/removeIncident`, `addRisk/removeRisk`, `addEscalation/removeEscalation`.

Load in `fetchData()` (after `p` is set):
```ts
incidentLogRows.value  = Array.isArray(p.incident_log)       ? p.incident_log       : []
riskRegisterRows.value = Array.isArray(p.risk_register)      ? p.risk_register      : []
escalationRows.value   = Array.isArray(p.escalation_records) ? p.escalation_records : []
```

Add to `handleSubmit()` payload:
```ts
incident_log:       incidentLogRows.value.length  > 0 ? incidentLogRows.value  : undefined,
risk_register:      riskRegisterRows.value.length > 0 ? riskRegisterRows.value : undefined,
escalation_records: escalationRows.value.length   > 0 ? escalationRows.value   : undefined,
```

**Others window-item restructure (2-column):**

LEFT col (md=7):
- **Incidents card**: add-row form with date + severity select (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL) + description textarea + status select (OPEN/RESOLVED/ESCALATED). Rows listed below.
- **Risk Register card**: risk description + likelihood select (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) + impact select (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) + mitigation + status (OPEN/MITIGATED/CLOSED).

RIGHT col (md=5):
- **Escalation Records card**: escalatedTo + date + issue + resolution. Add/remove rows.
- **Administrative Records** (collapsed `v-expansion-panel` at bottom): contains existing statusUpdateRows/readinessDocRows/signatoryRows (moved here as collapsed sub-section, no content change).

**KW-F5 � detail-[id].vue Others tab:**

Replace Others window-item with 4 read-only cards in a 2-column layout:
- LEFT: Incident Log (severity-colored `v-chip` per row) + Risk Register (impact-color `v-data-table` style)
- RIGHT: Escalation Records (compact list) + Administrative Records (collapsed `v-expansion-panel`)

**Verification:**
- KW-F-V1: Incident entry saves and persists across reload (requires migration applied first)
- KW-F-V2: Admin records (statusUpdateRows etc.) still save and display correctly
- KW-F-V3: Detail page Others tab shows 4 read-only sections

---

#### KW-G � MOV Evidence Aggregated View in Attachments

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

Add refs + fetch:
```ts
const movEntries = ref<any[]>([])
async function fetchMovEntries() {
  try {
    const res = await api.get<any>(`/api/construction-projects/${projectId}/mov-entries`)
    movEntries.value = Array.isArray(res) ? res : (res?.data || [])
  } catch { movEntries.value = [] }
}
```

Call `fetchMovEntries()` in `onMounted()`.

In Other Attachments card template, below the external links section, add:
```html
<v-divider class="my-4" />
<div class="text-overline text-medium-emphasis mb-2">MOV / EVIDENCE ENTRIES</div>
<div v-if="!movEntries.length" class="text-caption text-grey">
  No MOV entries recorded yet. Add via Timeline tab milestone or work log detail.
</div>
<v-list v-else density="compact">
  <v-list-item v-for="mov in movEntries" :key="mov.id">
    <template #prepend><v-icon icon="mdi-link-variant" color="teal" /></template>
    <v-list-item-title>
      <a :href="mov.movLink" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="text-decoration-none">{{ mov.movTitle }}</a>
    </v-list-item-title>
    <v-list-item-subtitle class="d-flex align-center ga-2 mt-1">
      <v-chip size="x-small" variant="tonal"
        :color="mov.verificationStatus === 'VERIFIED' ? 'success' : mov.verificationStatus === 'REJECTED' ? 'error' : 'grey'">
        {{ mov.verificationStatus }}
      </v-chip>
      <span class="text-caption text-grey">{{ mov.evidenceCategory }}</span>
      <span class="text-caption text-grey">{{ mov.relatedEntityType }}</span>
    </v-list-item-subtitle>
  </v-list-item>
</v-list>
```

**Verification:**
- KW-G-V1: MOV entries added via Timeline modals appear in Attachments tab list
- Clicking link opens in new tab

---

#### KW-H � Personnel Tab Authorization Extension

**Type:** Frontend only
**Files:** `pmo-frontend/composables/useCoiAccess.ts`, `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`, `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

After reading the composable, add and return these computed flags:
```ts
const canEditMilestones = computed(() => isAdmin.value)
const canEditWorkLog    = computed(() => canEditCurrentProject.value)
```

- Timeline tab: add `v-if="canEditWorkLog"` to the Add Work Log button and diary edit/delete buttons.

## Phase KX � Gallery Rendering Fix, DTO Normalization, Timeline Filter Stabilization, Image Compaction, MOV in Edit Dialogs

**Phase:** KX
**Status:** ? PENDING OPERATOR AUTHORIZATION
**Research:** Section 2.163 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** 2 critical bugs (gallery imageUrl, DTO forbidNonWhitelisted), timeline filter boundary error + toggle deselection, project image compact layout, checklist title deduplication, MOV section in milestone/diary edit dialogs.

---

### Context

VERIFY_STEP KW (Section 2.163) found 2 CRITICAL bugs introduced in KW:
1. Gallery images always stuck loading (EditGalleryItem uses `filePath` but backend returns `imageUrl`)
2. Others tab PATCH always fails HTTP 400 (monitoring fields missing from DTO, forbidNonWhitelisted rejects them)

Both block core functionality and must be fixed first.

---

### Governance Directives (Phase KX)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| KX-D1 | `EditGalleryItem` interface in `edit-[id].vue` MUST use `imageUrl: string` not `filePath`. All `:src` bindings in gallery template MUST use `img.imageUrl`. |
| KX-D2 | `CreateConstructionProjectDto` MUST include `incident_log`, `risk_register`, `escalation_records` as `@IsOptional() @IsArray()` fields. UpdateConstructionProjectDto inherits via PartialType � no separate change. |
| KX-D3 | The `update()` service method MUST map `dto.incident_log`, `dto.risk_register`, `dto.escalation_records` to entity fields. |
| KX-D4 | Timeline date comparison MUST use `.slice(0, 10)` on ISO datetime strings before string comparison with YYYY-MM-DD bounds. |
| KX-D5 | All three `v-btn-toggle` instances bound to `sharedPeriod` MUST have `:mandatory="true"` to prevent deselection. |
| KX-D6 | Profile images in Overview MUST use fixed `height="160"` instead of `aspect-ratio="16/9"`. All 3 images always in `cols="4"` horizontal strip. |
| KX-D7 | CiDocumentChecklist outer wrapper in edit-[id].vue and detail-[id].vue MUST NOT have a card title � the component renders its own. Remove the outer card title, keep the fullscreen button in the card-title row or move to card-text header. |
| KX-D8 | MOV evidence section in milestone and diary dialogs: show AFTER successful save using `milestoneEditingId`/`diaryEditingId`. |

---

### Sub-phases

---

#### KX-A � Gallery imageUrl Fix (CRITICAL)

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Risk:** Low � field rename, no logic change

**KX-A1 � Interface fix:**
Change `EditGalleryItem` interface:
```ts
interface EditGalleryItem {
  id: string
  imageUrl: string   // was: filePath
  caption?: string
  category?: string
}
```

**KX-A2 � Template fixes:**
Replace ALL occurrences of `img.filePath` with `img.imageUrl` in the gallery sections (both inline and fullscreen dialog), specifically:
- `v-img :src="img.filePath"` ? `:src="img.imageUrl"` (4 occurrences: inline profile, inline general, fullscreen profile, fullscreen general)

Search pattern: `img.filePath` in the Attachments tab gallery section. Do NOT affect document `doc.filePath` references.

**Verification:**
- KX-A-V1: After uploading a gallery image, it renders without staying in loading state
- KX-A-V2: Existing gallery images load correctly on page mount
- KX-A-V3: Profile and general gallery both render in their respective tabs

---

#### KX-B � DTO + Service Fix for Monitoring Fields (CRITICAL)

**Type:** Backend only
**Files:** `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/dto/create-construction-project.dto.ts`, `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`

**KX-B1 � Add fields to CreateConstructionProjectDto:**
At the end of the class (after `signatories`), add:
```ts
@IsOptional()
@IsArray()
incident_log?: Record<string, any>[];

@IsOptional()
@IsArray()
risk_register?: Record<string, any>[];

@IsOptional()
@IsArray()
escalation_records?: Record<string, any>[];
```
No import changes needed � `@IsOptional` and `@IsArray` are already imported.

**KX-B2 � Service update() method � map new DTO fields to entity:**
Find the `update()` service method. Locate where `status_updates`, `readiness_documents`, `signatories` are written to the entity (or where `dto` fields are applied to the entity via `wrap(entity).assign(dto, { em: this.em })`). Add handling:
```ts
if (dto.incident_log !== undefined) entity.incidentLog = dto.incident_log;
if (dto.risk_register !== undefined) entity.riskRegister = dto.risk_register;
if (dto.escalation_records !== undefined) entity.escalationRecords = dto.escalation_records;
```
If the service uses `wrap(entity).assign(dto, { updateByPrimaryKey: false })`, verify MikroORM maps `incident_log` ? `incidentLog` via camelCase conversion. If not, add explicit assignment.

**Verification:**
- KX-B-V1: PATCH with `incident_log: [{ date: '2026-05-14', severity: 'HIGH', description: 'test', status: 'OPEN' }]` returns 200 (not 400)
- KX-B-V2: After save, the incident entry appears in the detail page Others tab on reload
- KX-B-V3: PATCH with no monitoring fields still succeeds (backward compatible)

---

#### KX-C � Timeline Filter Stabilization

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**KX-C1 � Date string normalization in filteredMilestones:**
In the `filteredMilestones` computed, change:
```ts
// Before:
if (df) items = items.filter((m: any) => (m.targetDate ?? '') >= df)
if (dt) items = items.filter((m: any) => (m.targetDate ?? '') <= dt)

// After:
if (df) items = items.filter((m: any) => ((m.targetDate ?? '').slice(0, 10)) >= df)
if (dt) items = items.filter((m: any) => ((m.targetDate ?? '').slice(0, 10)) <= dt)
```

**KX-C2 � Date string normalization in filteredWorkLog:**
Same change for `filteredWorkLog`:
```ts
if (df) items = items.filter(e => (e.entryDate ?? '').slice(0, 10) >= df)
if (dt) items = items.filter(e => (e.entryDate ?? '').slice(0, 10) <= dt)
```

**KX-C3 � Mandatory toggle (prevent deselection):**
Add `:mandatory="true"` to all three `v-btn-toggle` instances:
1. Tab-level filter bar (Timeline window-item body, ~line 1313)
2. Milestone dialog (KW-C2 addition, ~line 2126)
3. Work Log dialog (KW-C2 addition, ~line 2194)

Also add a fallback guard in periodBounds computed:
```ts
const period = sharedPeriod.value || 'ALL'
// Use `period` instead of `sharedPeriod.value` in all if-branches
```

**Verification:**
- KX-C-V1: Selecting "Monthly" filters both milestones and work logs to current month, including entries on the last day of the month
- KX-C-V2: Clicking the active period button does NOT deselect it (mandatory behavior)
- KX-C-V3: Changing period inside milestone dialog immediately updates the filtered list

---

#### KX-D � Project Image Compact Layout

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**KX-D1 � Replace aspect ratio with fixed height:**
In the profile images strip card, change:
```html
<!-- Before -->
<v-col cols="12" :sm="profileImages.length === 1 ? 12 : profileImages.length === 2 ? 6 : 4">
  <v-img :src="img.imageUrl" :aspect-ratio="16/9" cover ...>

<!-- After -->
<v-col cols="4">
  <v-img :src="img.imageUrl" height="160" cover ...>
```

Remove the dynamic `:sm` binding. Use `cols="4"` always � 3 images at equal width. For 1 or 2 images, the columns are still `cols="4"` and images appear left-aligned (the other columns are empty). This is intentional � the strip stays compact regardless of image count.

**KX-D2 � Card variant:**
Change the card to `variant="outlined"` to reduce visual weight:
```html
<v-card v-if="profileImages.length > 0" variant="outlined" class="mb-3">
```

**Verification:**
- KX-D-V1: Profile images strip never exceeds 160px height
- KX-D-V2: 1, 2, or 3 images all display in a horizontal row at the same height
- KX-D-V3: Clicking an image still opens fullscreen preview dialog

---

#### KX-E � Checklist Title Deduplication

**Type:** Frontend only
**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`, `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**KX-E1 � edit-[id].vue: Remove outer card title, keep fullscreen button:**
The outer card currently has:
```html
<v-card class="mb-4">
  <v-card-title class="d-flex align-center ga-2">
    <v-icon icon="mdi-clipboard-check-outline" size="small" />
    Document Compliance Checklist
    <v-spacer />
    <v-btn icon="mdi-fullscreen" ... />
  </v-card-title>
  <v-divider />
  <v-card-text class="pa-0">
    <CiDocumentChecklist ... />
  </v-card-text>
</v-card>
```

Change to: remove the card entirely, replace with a simple wrapper div that contains just the fullscreen button and the component. The component's own summary card provides the visual section header:
```html
<div class="mb-4">
  <div class="d-flex justify-end mb-1">
    <v-btn variant="text" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-fullscreen" @click="checklistFullscreen = true">
      Fullscreen
    </v-btn>
  </div>
  <CiDocumentChecklist ... />
</div>
```

**KX-E2 � detail-[id].vue: Same removal in Documents tab:**
The `<CiDocumentChecklist>` in the Documents tab (left column) currently has no outer card wrapper � the component renders its own cards. Verify no outer title wrapping exists. If it does, remove it.

**Verification:**
- KX-E-V1: Only one "Document Compliance Checklist" header appears in edit Attachments tab
- KX-E-V2: Fullscreen button still accessible

---

#### KX-F � MOV in Milestone/Diary Edit Dialogs

**Type:** Frontend only
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

**KX-F1 � Post-save MOV section in milestone dialog:**

After `saveMilestone()` succeeds, the dialog currently closes (`milestoneDialog.value = false`). Change to: if mode is 'create' and a new entity ID is available, keep dialog open and show a "Add MOV Evidence (optional)" section at the bottom.

Add ref: `const milestoneJustSavedId = ref<string | null>(null)`

Modify `saveMilestone()` to:
1. Keep dialog open after create by NOT setting `milestoneDialog.value = false` immediately
2. Set `milestoneJustSavedId.value = savedId` (capture the returned entity ID from the API)
3. Show MOV section in dialog using `CiMovEvidenceSection` component
4. Add a "Done" button that closes the dialog and resets `milestoneJustSavedId.value = null`

The `CiMovEvidenceSection` is already imported in `detail-[id].vue`. Add import to `edit-[id].vue`:
```ts
import CiMovEvidenceSection from '~/components/coi/CiMovEvidenceSection.vue'
```

In dialog template, after the form fields and save button:
```html
<template v-if="milestoneJustSavedId">
  <v-divider class="my-4" />
  <div class="text-subtitle-2 font-weight-medium mb-2">
    <v-icon size="small" class="mr-1">mdi-link-variant</v-icon>
    Add MOV Evidence (optional)
  </div>
  <CiMovEvidenceSection
    :project-id="projectId"
    related-entity-type="MILESTONE"
    :related-entity-id="milestoneJustSavedId"
    :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject"
    :open-key="`milestone-${milestoneJustSavedId}`"
  />
  <div class="d-flex justify-end mt-3">
    <v-btn color="primary" variant="tonal" @click="milestoneDialog = false; milestoneJustSavedId = null">
      Done
    </v-btn>
  </div>
</template>
```

**KX-F2 � Post-save MOV section in diary dialog:**

Same pattern for `diaryDialog`:
```ts
const diaryJustSavedId = ref<string | null>(null)
```

After `saveDiary()` captures the returned entry ID, keep dialog open if creating. Show `CiMovEvidenceSection` with `related-entity-type="TIMELINE_ENTRY"` and `:related-entity-id="diaryJustSavedId"`.

**Note on API response**: Verify that `saveMilestone()` and `saveDiary()` API calls return the created entity with its ID. If the current POST endpoint returns the created entity, capture `result.id`. If it returns a generic success, a follow-up GET to fetch the latest milestone/diary entry is needed to get the ID.

**Verification:**
- KX-F-V1: After adding a milestone in edit page, dialog stays open showing MOV section
- KX-F-V2: User can add a MOV link in the same dialog workflow
- KX-F-V3: Clicking "Done" closes dialog; MOV entry appears in detail page milestone modal
- KX-F-V4: Same behavior for diary entries

---

## Phase KY � File Serving Architecture, JSONB Storage Fix, Others Tab Sync, External Link Normalization

**Phase:** KY
**Status:** ? PENDING OPERATOR AUTHORIZATION ? ? COMPLETE (2026-05-14)
**Research:** Section 2.164 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** Add file serving (NestJS static assets + Nuxt proxy + /uploads prefix), fix JSONB empty-array double-wrapping, normalize Others tab adapter, clean up external link hints.

---

### Context

VERIFY_STEP KX (Section 2.164) found 2 CRITICAL rendering failures both rooted in the same missing file-serving infrastructure:
1. Document download 404 � no static file server, relative paths break
2. Gallery image loading failure � same relative-path issue, even after KX-A field name fix
Plus JSONB `[[]]` corruption in monitoring fields from empty-array handling in service update().

---

### Governance Directives (Phase KY)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| KY-D1 | StorageService MUST return `/uploads/${relativePath}` as `filePath` so stored paths are absolute-relative and unambiguous. |
| KY-D2 | NestJS `main.ts` MUST serve the uploads directory at `/uploads` prefix via `useStaticAssets`. |
| KY-D3 | Nuxt `nuxt.config.ts` MUST proxy `/uploads/*` ? `http://localhost:3000/uploads/*` so dev server forwards file requests to backend. |
| KY-D4 | Service `update()` MUST filter out empty arrays from `fields` to prevent writing empty JSONB values and breaking the stored state. |
| KY-D5 | Adapter `adaptProjectDetail()` MUST normalize monitoring arrays � filter out non-object array items to prevent `[[]]` corrupt data from rendering phantom rows. |
| KY-D6 | External link hint text MUST say "Any https:// URL" � not "Google Drive". The regex already accepts all https:// URLs. |

---

### Sub-phases

#### KY-A � File Serving (Backend + Frontend Config)

**KY-A1 � NestJS static assets + import:**
File: `pmo-backend/src/main.ts`
Add `NestExpressApplication` type and `join` import. Mount uploads directory as static assets at `/uploads` prefix.

**KY-A2 � StorageService /uploads prefix:**
File: `pmo-backend/src/uploads/storage/storage.service.ts`
Change returned filePath to `/uploads/${relative}` so all downstream consumers receive a server-root-relative URL.

**KY-A3 � Nuxt devProxy for /uploads:**
File: `pmo-frontend/nuxt.config.ts`
Add `/uploads` proxy entry pointing to `http://localhost:3000/uploads`.

**Verification:** KY-A-V1: Uploaded document link opens file in new tab. KY-A-V2: Gallery image renders without loading state.

---

**KY-B1 � Service: exclude empty arrays from fields:**
File: `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`
In `update()`, extend the `fields` filter to also exclude empty arrays.

**KY-B2 � Adapter: normalize monitoring arrays:**
File: `pmo-frontend/utils/adapters.ts`
In `adaptProjectDetail()`, filter monitoring arrays to only include plain objects (not arrays-in-arrays from `[[]]` corruption).

**Verification:** KY-B-V1: PATCH with empty monitoring rows does not overwrite existing DB values. KY-B-V2: Detail page Others tab shows no phantom empty rows.

---

**Verification:** KY-C-V1: Hint text reflects generic URL acceptance.

## Phase LA � Timeline View Governance, Project Image Optimization, Others Tab Sync, Key Document Normalization, MOV Edit Mode, Progress Summary

**Phase:** LA
**Status:** ? COMPLETE (2026-05-18 � all 7 sub-phases implemented, pending operator verification)
**Research:** Section 2.165 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-18
**Scope:** 7 sub-phases addressing verified operational gaps discovered in VERIFY_STEP KZ + KC continuity audit. All changes are frontend-only (adapters.ts + detail-[id].vue + edit-[id].vue). No new backend endpoints. No new migrations.

---

### Context

VERIFY_STEP KZ confirmed as operational (operator confirmed). Migrations 060000�090000 applied. File serving active. KC through KZ all ?. This phase addresses remaining UX and governance gaps surfaced during audit:
1. TypeScript interface gap in `BackendProjectDetail` (causes IDE errors, future regression risk)
2. Key document classification field unusable when taxonomy not seeded
3. MOV evidence not accessible when editing an existing milestone/diary entry
4. Timeline filter toolbar is visually unstructured
5. Project image cards lack metadata and structured layout
6. Timeline has no table view (only list)
7. No Progress Summary card in timeline tab

---

### Governance Directives (Phase LA)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| LA-D1 | `BackendProjectDetail` interface in `adapters.ts` is the ONLY place backend field types are declared. Add fields there � do NOT cast via `(backend as any)` in adapter mapping. |
| LA-D2 | `v-combobox` for key document classification MUST still pass `documentType` to the backend as-is (free-text accepted). The compliance checklist auto-link is best-effort � no behavioral change. |
| LA-D3 | MOV in edit-mode milestone/diary dialogs MUST reuse `CiMovEvidenceSection` with `milestoneEditingId` / `diaryEditingId` as `relatedEntityId`. No new component. |
| LA-D4 | Timeline table view MUST use `v-data-table` (Vuetify 3). Column headers and `item-value` must be defined. No external library. |
| LA-D5 | Progress Summary card is ADDITIVE � placed below existing timeline KPI stat cards. Does NOT replace hero card or existing KPI cards. |
| LA-D6 | No calendar view in this phase � deferred. Complex, requires experimental Vuetify lab component or custom grid. |
| LA-D7 | Filter toolbar restructure is LAYOUT ONLY � no logic change to `sharedPeriod`, `periodBounds`, `filteredMilestones`, `filteredWorkLog` computeds. |

---

```ts
document_checklist_remarks?: Record<string, string>
incident_log?: Record<string, any>[]
risk_register?: Record<string, any>[]
escalation_records?: Record<string, any>[]
```

|----|------|

| LA-A-V2 | Others tab in detail page renders entries when data exists in DB |

```html
/>
```

```html
  <v-card-text>

      <v-icon size="small" color="teal">mdi-link-variant</v-icon>
```

```html
    <div class="text-subtitle-2 font-weight-medium mb-2 d-flex align-center ga-1">

</template>
```

### Sub-Phase LA-D � Timeline Filter Toolbar Restructure

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** Restructure filter bar from flat `d-flex flex-wrap` to a 2-column labeled layout. No logic changes to `sharedPeriod`, `periodBounds`, or filter computeds.

**LA-D-1:** Replace the filter card content in the timeline tab (`<v-card variant="outlined" class="mb-4 pa-3">...`):
```html
<v-card variant="outlined" class="mb-4">
  <v-card-text class="py-2">
    <v-row dense align="center">
      <v-col cols="12" sm="auto">
        <div class="text-caption text-grey font-weight-medium mb-1">Filter Period</div>
        <v-btn-toggle v-model="sharedPeriod" density="compact" variant="outlined" divided :mandatory="true">
          <v-btn value="ALL" size="small">All</v-btn>
          <v-btn value="DAILY" size="small">Daily</v-btn>
          <v-btn value="WEEKLY" size="small">Weekly</v-btn>
          <v-btn value="MONTHLY" size="small">Monthly</v-btn>
          <v-btn value="QUARTERLY" size="small">Quarterly</v-btn>
        </v-btn-toggle>
      </v-col>
      <v-col v-if="sharedPeriod === 'ALL'" cols="12" sm="auto">
        <div class="text-caption text-grey font-weight-medium mb-1">Date Range</div>
        <div class="d-flex align-center ga-2">
          <v-text-field v-model="sharedDateFrom" type="date" label="From" density="compact" variant="outlined" hide-details style="max-width:150px" />
          <v-text-field v-model="sharedDateTo" type="date" label="To" density="compact" variant="outlined" hide-details style="max-width:150px" />
          <v-btn v-if="sharedDateFrom || sharedDateTo" icon="mdi-close-circle" variant="text" size="small" @click="sharedDateFrom = ''; sharedDateTo = ''" />
        </div>
      </v-col>
      <v-col v-else cols="12" sm="auto">
        <div class="text-caption text-grey font-weight-medium mb-1">Active Window</div>
        <v-chip size="small" color="primary" variant="tonal">{{ periodBounds.from }} � {{ periodBounds.to }}</v-chip>
      </v-col>
    </v-row>
  </v-card-text>
</v-card>
```

**Note:** The same restructure should be applied to the mini period toggles inside milestone and work log dialogs (same pattern, smaller scope � just `v-btn-toggle` without date range).

**Verification:**
| ID | Test |
|----|------|
| LA-D-V1 | Filter bar shows Period and Date Range in two visually distinct labeled sections |
| LA-D-V2 | On mobile, sections stack cleanly without overflow |
| LA-D-V3 | Filter logic unchanged � DAILY still shows today's entries |

```html
        <div class="d-flex justify-end pa-1" style="position:absolute;top:0;right:0">

        </div>
      </v-img>
      <v-card-text class="py-2 px-3">
        <div class="text-body-2 font-weight-medium text-truncate">{{ img.caption || 'Project Image' }}</div>
        <v-chip v-if="img.uploadedAt" size="x-small" variant="tonal" color="grey" class="mt-1">
          <v-icon start size="10">mdi-calendar</v-icon>
          {{ formatDate(img.uploadedAt) }}
        </v-chip>
      </v-card-text>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

**LA-E-2:** Add `profilePreviewTitle` and `profilePreviewDate` refs:

```ts
const profilePreviewDate  = ref('')
```

**LA-E-3:** Update the fullscreen dialog to show metadata:
```html

<v-dialog v-model="profilePreviewOpen" max-width="900" scrollable>
  <v-card>
    <v-img :src="profilePreviewImg" max-height="75vh" contain />
    <v-card-text v-if="profilePreviewTitle || profilePreviewDate" class="py-2 d-flex align-center ga-3">
      <div v-if="profilePreviewTitle" class="text-body-1 font-weight-medium">{{ profilePreviewTitle }}</div>
      <v-chip v-if="profilePreviewDate" size="small" variant="tonal" color="grey">

        {{ formatDate(profilePreviewDate) }}
      </v-chip>
    </v-card-text>

    </v-card-actions>
  </v-card>
</v-dialog>
```

```ts
]
```

### Sub-Phase LA-G � Progress Summary Card

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** Add a Progress Summary card in the Timeline tab, placed between the KPI stat cards and the Milestones section. Additive � no existing cards removed.

**LA-G-1:** Add `progressSummary` computed:
```ts
const progressSummary = computed(() => {
  const totalMs = project.value?.milestones?.length ?? 0
  const completedMs = (project.value?.milestones || []).filter((m: any) => m.status === 'COMPLETED').length
  const msRate = totalMs > 0 ? Math.round((completedMs / totalMs) * 100) : 0
  const physical = project.value?.physicalProgress ?? 0
  const targetPhysical = project.value?.targetPhysicalProgress ?? 100
  const financial = project.value?.financialProgress ?? 0
  const targetFinancial = project.value?.targetFinancialProgress ?? 100
  const target = project.value?.targetCompletionDate
  const daysRemaining = target
    ? Math.ceil((new Date(target).getTime() - Date.now()) / 86400000)
    : null
  return { physical, targetPhysical, financial, targetFinancial, msRate, totalMs, completedMs, daysRemaining }
})
```

**LA-G-2:** Add Progress Summary card template below the KPI stat row and above the milestones section divider:
```html
<v-card v-if="project" variant="outlined" class="mb-4">
  <v-card-title class="text-subtitle-2 d-flex align-center ga-2 py-2">
    <v-icon icon="mdi-chart-line-variant" size="small" />
    Progress Summary
  </v-card-title>
  <v-divider />
  <v-card-text class="py-3">
    <v-row dense>
      <v-col cols="12" md="4">
        <div class="text-caption text-grey mb-1">Physical Progress</div>
        <v-progress-linear :model-value="progressSummary.physical" :color="progressSummary.physical >= progressSummary.targetPhysical ? 'success' : 'primary'" height="10" rounded />
        <div class="d-flex justify-space-between text-caption mt-1">
          <span class="font-weight-medium">{{ progressSummary.physical }}%</span>
          <span class="text-grey">Target {{ progressSummary.targetPhysical }}%</span>
        </div>
      </v-col>
      <v-col cols="12" md="4">
        <div class="text-caption text-grey mb-1">Financial Progress</div>
        <v-progress-linear :model-value="progressSummary.financial" :color="progressSummary.financial >= progressSummary.targetFinancial ? 'success' : 'info'" height="10" rounded />
        <div class="d-flex justify-space-between text-caption mt-1">
          <span class="font-weight-medium">{{ progressSummary.financial }}%</span>
          <span class="text-grey">Target {{ progressSummary.targetFinancial }}%</span>
        </div>
      </v-col>
      <v-col cols="12" md="4">
        <div class="text-caption text-grey mb-1">Milestone Completion</div>
        <v-progress-linear :model-value="progressSummary.msRate" :color="progressSummary.msRate >= 100 ? 'success' : 'secondary'" height="10" rounded />
        <div class="d-flex justify-space-between text-caption mt-1">
          <span class="font-weight-medium">{{ progressSummary.completedMs }} / {{ progressSummary.totalMs }}</span>
          <span class="text-grey">{{ progressSummary.msRate }}%</span>
        </div>
      </v-col>
    </v-row>
    <div v-if="progressSummary.daysRemaining !== null" class="mt-3">
      <v-chip
        size="small"
        :color="progressSummary.daysRemaining < 0 ? 'error' : progressSummary.daysRemaining <= 30 ? 'warning' : 'success'"
        variant="tonal"
        prepend-icon="mdi-clock-outline"
      >
        {{ progressSummary.daysRemaining < 0 ? `${Math.abs(progressSummary.daysRemaining)} days overdue` : `${progressSummary.daysRemaining} days remaining` }}
      </v-chip>
    </div>
  </v-card-text>
</v-card>
```

**Verification:**
| ID | Test |
|----|------|
| LA-G-V1 | Progress Summary card appears between KPI stat cards and Milestones section |
| LA-G-V2 | Physical / Financial / Milestone bars show correct values from project data |
| LA-G-V3 | Days remaining chip shows correct value + correct color (red if overdue) |

### Governance Directives Summary

| ID | Rule |
|----|------|
| LA-D1 | BackendProjectDetail is single source of truth for backend field types |

## Phase LB � Others Tab Normalization Fix, Attachment Modal Stabilization, Gallery Metadata, Grouped Timeline Filter, Calendar View

**Phase:** LB
**Status:** ? COMPLETE (2026-05-18 � all 5 sub-phases implemented, pending operator verification + migration run)
**Research:** Section 2.166 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-18
**Scope:** 5 sub-phases. CRITICAL bug fix (LB-A, single-file 3-line change) + attachment label/modal fixes + gallery imageTakenDate + grouped filter mode + calendar view. Frontend-heavy. LB-C requires 1 new migration.

---

### Context

VERIFY_STEP LA confirmed: Others tab `fetchData()` does NOT apply normalization filter on monitoring arrays. Network inspection confirmed `incident_log: [[]]` in PATCH payload � a perpetual corruption cycle. Additionally: attachment section has stale label and modal overflow issue; gallery lacks `imageTakenDate`; grouped filter mode and calendar view remain unimplemented.

---

### Governance Directives (Phase LB)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| LB-D1 | `fetchData()` normalization helper `monNorm()` must apply the SAME filter as `adaptProjectDetail()` � removes any item that is not a plain non-array object. |
| LB-D2 | Attachment modal `v-card-title` must NOT contain user data (fileName) � it must contain only the dialog label ("Document"). FileName moves to body. |
| LB-D3 | `imageTakenDate` is stored as `DATE` (not timestamp) in the DB. Frontend sends ISO date string `YYYY-MM-DD`. |
| LB-D4 | Grouped filter mode (`groupByMode` ref) is ADDITIVE � it does NOT replace the existing `filteredMilestones`/`filteredWorkLog` computeds. Grouping is a rendering-layer transform on top of existing filtered arrays. |
| LB-D5 | Calendar view uses a custom CSS-grid month calendar (NO external library, NO Vuetify labs). Each cell shows colored dots per event. Click a cell to see entries for that day. |
| LB-D6 | Gallery `imageTakenDate` migration uses `IF NOT EXISTS` � safe to run on any schema state. |

---

### Sub-Phase LB-A � Others Tab fetchData Normalization (CRITICAL BUG FIX)

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Lines:** 274�276 (confirmed)
**Scope:** Replace raw assignment of monitoring arrays with normalized assignment using the same filter as `adaptProjectDetail()`.

**LB-A-1:** Replace lines 274�276:
```ts
// REPLACE THIS:
incidentLogRows.value  = Array.isArray((p as any).incident_log)       ? (p as any).incident_log       : []
riskRegisterRows.value = Array.isArray((p as any).risk_register)      ? (p as any).risk_register      : []
escalationRows.value   = Array.isArray((p as any).escalation_records) ? (p as any).escalation_records : []

// WITH THIS (add monNorm helper inline before these 3 lines):
const monNorm = (v: any): any[] => Array.isArray(v) ? v.filter((r: any) => r && typeof r === 'object' && !Array.isArray(r)) : []
incidentLogRows.value  = monNorm((p as any).incident_log)
riskRegisterRows.value = monNorm((p as any).risk_register)
escalationRows.value   = monNorm((p as any).escalation_records)
```

**Effect:**
- `[[]]` from DB ? normalized to `[]` ? user sees empty rows ? adds real data ? saves `[{...}]` ? DB corrected
- `[{...}]` from DB ? passes filter (each item is a non-array object) ? rows load correctly
- No migration needed � DB self-corrects on first valid save after this fix

**Verification:**

| LB-A-V2 | Add one incident row, fill fields, Save ? PATCH payload shows `incident_log: [{...}]` (not `[[]]`) |
| LB-A-V3 | Navigate to detail page ? Others tab shows the saved incident data |
| LB-A-V4 | Reload edit page ? Others tab shows the saved rows correctly populated |

### Sub-Phase LB-D � Grouped Filter Mode (Timeline)

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** Add `groupByPeriod` boolean toggle. When ON, render milestones and work log entries organized into date-unit groups. Does NOT replace existing filter. Additive.

**LB-D-1:** Add `groupByPeriod` ref and grouping computeds:
```ts
const groupByPeriod = ref(false)

const groupedMilestones = computed(() => {
  if (!groupByPeriod.value || sharedPeriod.value === 'ALL') return null
  const map = new Map<string, typeof filteredMilestones.value>()
  for (const m of filteredMilestones.value) {
    const key = getGroupKey(m.targetDate ?? '', sharedPeriod.value)
    if (!map.has(key)) map.set(key, [])
    map.get(key)!.push(m)
  }
  return [...map.entries()].sort((a, b) => a[0].localeCompare(b[0]))
})

const groupedWorkLog = computed(() => {
  if (!groupByPeriod.value || sharedPeriod.value === 'ALL') return null
  const map = new Map<string, typeof filteredWorkLog.value>()
  for (const e of filteredWorkLog.value) {
    const key = getGroupKey(e.entryDate ?? '', sharedPeriod.value)
    if (!map.has(key)) map.set(key, [])
    map.get(key)!.push(e)
  }
  return [...map.entries()].sort((a, b) => a[0].localeCompare(b[0]))
})

function getGroupKey(dateStr: string, period: string): string {
  const d = dateStr.slice(0, 10)
  if (!d || d === '0001-01-01') return 'Unscheduled'
  if (period === 'DAILY') return d
  if (period === 'WEEKLY') {
    const dt = new Date(d)
    const day = dt.getDay()
    const mon = new Date(dt); mon.setDate(dt.getDate() - ((day + 6) % 7))
    return `Week of ${mon.toISOString().slice(0, 10)}`
  }
  if (period === 'MONTHLY') return d.slice(0, 7)
  if (period === 'QUARTERLY') {
    const m = parseInt(d.slice(5, 7), 10)
    return `${d.slice(0, 4)}-Q${Math.ceil(m / 3)}`
  }
  return d.slice(0, 7)
}
```

**LB-D-2:** Add "Group by period" chip-toggle next to the view mode toggle:
```html
<div class="d-flex align-center justify-end ga-2 mb-3">
  <v-chip
    size="small"
    :color="groupByPeriod ? 'primary' : 'default'"
    :variant="groupByPeriod ? 'flat' : 'outlined'"
    prepend-icon="mdi-group"
    style="cursor:pointer"
    @click="groupByPeriod = !groupByPeriod"
  >Group</v-chip>
  <span class="text-caption text-grey">View:</span>
  <v-btn-toggle v-model="timelineViewMode" density="compact" variant="outlined" divided :mandatory="true">
    <v-btn value="list" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-format-list-bulleted">List</v-btn>
    <v-btn value="table" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-table">Table</v-btn>
  </v-btn-toggle>
</div>
```

```html
  <div v-for="[groupKey, items] in groupedMilestones" :key="groupKey">
    <div class="text-overline text-primary px-3 py-1 bg-blue-lighten-5">{{ groupKey }} ({{ items.length }})</div>
    <v-list lines="two" density="comfortable">
      <v-list-item v-for="milestone in items" :key="milestone.id || milestone.name" ... />
    </v-list>
  </div>
</template>
<v-list v-else-if="timelineViewMode === 'list'" ...>
  <!-- existing list view -->
</v-list>
```
Same pattern for work log grouped sections.

### Sub-Phase LB-E � Calendar View (Custom Month Grid)

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** Add `calendar` as a third value for `timelineViewMode`. Implement a custom CSS-grid month calendar. No external library. No Vuetify labs.

**LB-E-1:** Extend `timelineViewMode` type and toggle:
```ts
const timelineViewMode = ref<'list' | 'table' | 'calendar'>('list')
```
Add Calendar button to view toggle:
```html
<v-btn value="calendar" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-calendar-month">Calendar</v-btn>
```

**LB-E-2:** Add calendar state:
```ts
const calendarMonth = ref(new Date().getMonth())
const calendarYear  = ref(new Date().getFullYear())
const calendarLabel = computed(() =>
  new Date(calendarYear.value, calendarMonth.value, 1)
    .toLocaleDateString('en-PH', { year: 'numeric', month: 'long' })
)

function prevMonth() {
  if (calendarMonth.value === 0) { calendarMonth.value = 11; calendarYear.value-- }
  else calendarMonth.value--
}
function nextMonth() {
  if (calendarMonth.value === 11) { calendarMonth.value = 0; calendarYear.value++ }
  else calendarMonth.value++
}

const calendarDays = computed(() => {
  const firstDay = new Date(calendarYear.value, calendarMonth.value, 1).getDay()
  const daysInMonth = new Date(calendarYear.value, calendarMonth.value + 1, 0).getDate()
  const pad = (firstDay + 6) % 7  // ISO week starts Monday
  const cells: Array<{ date: number | null; milestones: any[]; entries: any[] }> = []
  for (let i = 0; i < pad; i++) cells.push({ date: null, milestones: [], entries: [] })
  for (let d = 1; d <= daysInMonth; d++) {
    const dateStr = `${calendarYear.value}-${String(calendarMonth.value + 1).padStart(2,'0')}-${String(d).padStart(2,'0')}`
    cells.push({
      date: d,
      milestones: (project.value?.milestones || []).filter((m: any) => (m.targetDate ?? '').slice(0,10) === dateStr),
      entries: workLogEntries.value.filter(e => (e.entryDate ?? '').slice(0,10) === dateStr),
    })
  }
  return cells
})
```

**LB-E-3:** Calendar template (inside timeline window-item, shown when `timelineViewMode === 'calendar'`):
```html
<v-card v-if="timelineViewMode === 'calendar'" class="mb-4">
  <v-card-title class="d-flex align-center justify-space-between py-2 px-4">
    <v-btn icon="mdi-chevron-left" variant="text" size="small" @click="prevMonth" />
    <span class="text-subtitle-1 font-weight-medium">{{ calendarLabel }}</span>
    <v-btn icon="mdi-chevron-right" variant="text" size="small" @click="nextMonth" />
  </v-card-title>
  <v-divider />
  <v-card-text class="pa-2">
    <!-- Day-of-week headers -->
    <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(7,1fr);gap:2px;margin-bottom:4px">
      <div v-for="d in ['Mon','Tue','Wed','Thu','Fri','Sat','Sun']" :key="d"
           class="text-center text-caption text-grey font-weight-medium py-1">{{ d }}</div>
    </div>
    <!-- Calendar cells -->
    <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(7,1fr);gap:2px">
      <div
        v-for="(cell, idx) in calendarDays"
        :key="idx"
        class="rounded pa-1"
        style="min-height:64px;background:#fafafa;border:1px solid #e0e0e0"
      >
        <div v-if="cell.date" class="text-caption font-weight-medium mb-1">{{ cell.date }}</div>
        <div v-if="cell.milestones.length > 0">
          <v-chip
            v-for="m in cell.milestones.slice(0,2)"
            :key="m.id || m.name"
            size="x-small"
            color="primary"
            variant="flat"
            class="mb-1 d-block text-truncate"
            style="max-width:100%;cursor:pointer"
            @click="openMilestoneDetail(m)"
          >{{ m.name || m.title }}</v-chip>
          <v-chip v-if="cell.milestones.length > 2" size="x-small" variant="tonal">+{{ cell.milestones.length - 2 }}</v-chip>
        </div>
        <div v-if="cell.entries.length > 0">
          <v-chip
            v-for="e in cell.entries.slice(0,1)"
            :key="e.id"
            size="x-small"
            color="info"
            variant="flat"
            class="mb-1 d-block text-truncate"
            style="max-width:100%;cursor:pointer"
            @click="openWorkLogDetail(e)"
          >{{ e.title || e.entryType }}</v-chip>
          <v-chip v-if="cell.entries.length > 1" size="x-small" variant="tonal" color="info">+{{ cell.entries.length - 1 }}</v-chip>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </v-card-text>
</v-card>
```

## Phase LC � Timeline Enterprise Refactor: Advanced Filter, Calendar Navigation, MOV File Upload, Reporter Type

**Phase:** LC
**Status:** ? COMPLETE (2026-05-18 � all 4 sub-phases implemented, pending operator verification + 2 migrations)
**Research:** Section 2.167 (docs/research.md)
**Last Updated:** 2026-05-18
**Scope:** 4 sub-phases. Advanced temporal filter parameterization (LC-A), calendar filtered data + year/month picker (LC-B), MOV file upload backend + frontend (LC-C), reporterType field (LC-D). LC-A and LC-B are frontend-only. LC-C and LC-D require new migrations.

---

### Context

VERIFY_STEP LB confirmed: Calendar uses raw unfiltered data (bug); `periodBounds` is anchored to `new Date()` and cannot navigate to historical periods (critical filter gap); MOV is URL-only (no file upload); `reporterType` missing from timeline entries. Line 2690+ audit confirmed MOV IS implemented post-create and edit-mode � the gap is file upload support.

---

### Governance Directives (Phase LC)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| LC-D1 | `periodBounds` computed MUST use `filterYear`/`filterQuarter`/`filterMonth` refs instead of `new Date()` when those refs are set. When all are null, fall back to today (current behavior). |
| LC-D2 | `filterYear`, `filterQuarter`, `filterMonth` are ORTHOGONAL to `sharedPeriod`. Period = granularity unit (DAILY/WEEKLY/etc). Year/Quarter/Month = the target time window to navigate to. |
| LC-D3 | Calendar `calendarDays` MUST read from `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog` (not raw arrays). The calendar respects the active filter. |
| LC-D4 | MOV file upload endpoint is SEPARATE from MOV link creation. File upload: `POST /:id/mov-entries/:entryId/upload-file`. Link creation: existing `POST /:id/mov-entries`. |
| LC-D5 | `movLink` becomes OPTIONAL in `ConstructionMovEntry` � a MOV entry has either a link OR a file. Service validates that at least one is present. |
| LC-D6 | `reporterType` on `ConstructionTimelineEntry` is optional with default NULL � no migration-time backfill needed. Values: `'CONSTRUCTOR' | 'EVALUATOR' | 'INSPECTOR' | 'ADMIN' | null`. |
| LC-D7 | `filterSortDir` applies to BOTH `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog` � consistent sort across all views. |

---

### Sub-Phase LC-A � Advanced Temporal Filter

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** Add `filterYear`, `filterQuarter`, `filterMonth`, `filterSortDir` refs. Refactor `periodBounds` to use these refs when set. Add filter row UI for year/quarter/month/sort selectors.

**LC-A-1:** Add temporal filter refs after `sharedPeriod`:
```ts
const filterYear    = ref<number | null>(null)
const filterQuarter = ref<1|2|3|4 | null>(null)
const filterMonth   = ref<number | null>(null)  // 1-12
const filterSortDir = ref<'asc' | 'desc'>('asc')

const availableYears = Array.from({ length: 11 }, (_, i) => 2020 + i)
const quarterOptions = [
  { title: 'Q1 (Jan�Mar)', value: 1 },
  { title: 'Q2 (Apr�Jun)', value: 2 },
  { title: 'Q3 (Jul�Sep)', value: 3 },
  { title: 'Q4 (Oct�Dec)', value: 4 },
]
const monthOptions = [
  { title: 'January', value: 1 }, { title: 'February', value: 2 },
  { title: 'March', value: 3 },   { title: 'April', value: 4 },
  { title: 'May', value: 5 },     { title: 'June', value: 6 },
  { title: 'July', value: 7 },    { title: 'August', value: 8 },
  { title: 'September', value: 9 },{ title: 'October', value: 10 },
  { title: 'November', value: 11 },{ title: 'December', value: 12 },
]
```

**LC-A-2:** Refactor `periodBounds` computed:
```ts
const periodBounds = computed((): { from: string; to: string } => {
  const pad = (n: number) => String(n).padStart(2, '0')
  const fmt = (d: Date) => `${d.getFullYear()}-${pad(d.getMonth() + 1)}-${pad(d.getDate())}`

  // Determine reference year (use filterYear or current year)
  const year = filterYear.value ?? new Date().getFullYear()

  if (sharedPeriod.value === 'ALL') return { from: '', to: '' }

  if (sharedPeriod.value === 'QUARTERLY') {
    const q = filterQuarter.value ?? Math.floor(new Date().getMonth() / 3) + 1
    const from = new Date(year, (q - 1) * 3, 1)
    const to   = new Date(year, q * 3, 0)
    return { from: fmt(from), to: fmt(to) }
  }
  if (sharedPeriod.value === 'MONTHLY') {
    // If quarter set: show entire quarter; if month set: show that month; else: current month
    if (filterQuarter.value) {
      const q = filterQuarter.value
      const from = new Date(year, (q - 1) * 3, 1)
      const to   = new Date(year, q * 3, 0)
      return { from: fmt(from), to: fmt(to) }
    }
    const m = (filterMonth.value ?? new Date().getMonth() + 1) - 1
    const from = new Date(year, m, 1)
    const to   = new Date(year, m + 1, 0)
    return { from: fmt(from), to: fmt(to) }
  }
  if (sharedPeriod.value === 'WEEKLY') {
    // Show all weeks within the selected quarter or month range
    if (filterQuarter.value) {
      const q = filterQuarter.value
      const from = new Date(year, (q - 1) * 3, 1)
      const to   = new Date(year, q * 3, 0)
      return { from: fmt(from), to: fmt(to) }
    }
    if (filterMonth.value) {
      const m = filterMonth.value - 1
      const from = new Date(year, m, 1)
      const to   = new Date(year, m + 1, 0)
      return { from: fmt(from), to: fmt(to) }
    }
    // Default: current week
    const now = new Date()
    const day = now.getDay()
    const mon = new Date(now); mon.setDate(now.getDate() - ((day + 6) % 7))
    const sun = new Date(mon); sun.setDate(mon.getDate() + 6)
    return { from: fmt(mon), to: fmt(sun) }
  }
  if (sharedPeriod.value === 'DAILY') {
    // Show all daily entries in: month (if set) OR quarter (if set) OR today
    if (filterMonth.value) {
      const m = filterMonth.value - 1
      const from = new Date(year, m, 1)
      const to   = new Date(year, m + 1, 0)
      return { from: fmt(from), to: fmt(to) }
    }
    if (filterQuarter.value) {
      const q = filterQuarter.value
      const from = new Date(year, (q - 1) * 3, 1)
      const to   = new Date(year, q * 3, 0)
      return { from: fmt(from), to: fmt(to) }
    }
    const s = fmt(new Date())
    return { from: s, to: s }
  }
  return { from: '', to: '' }
})
```

**LC-A-3:** Add sort to `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog`:
At the end of each computed, before `return items`, add:
```ts
if (filterSortDir.value === 'desc') {
  items = [...items].sort((a: any, b: any) => ((b.targetDate ?? b.entryDate ?? '') < (a.targetDate ?? a.entryDate ?? '') ? -1 : 1))
} else {
  items = [...items].sort((a: any, b: any) => ((a.targetDate ?? a.entryDate ?? '') < (b.targetDate ?? b.entryDate ?? '') ? -1 : 1))
}
```

**LC-A-4:** Add secondary filter row to the filter toolbar (after the period toggle row, visible when `sharedPeriod !== 'ALL'`):
```html
<v-col v-if="sharedPeriod !== 'ALL'" cols="12">
  <v-divider class="mb-2" />
  <div class="d-flex align-center ga-2 flex-wrap">
    <div class="text-caption text-grey font-weight-medium">Narrow to:</div>
    <v-select
      v-model="filterYear"
      :items="availableYears"
      label="Year"
      density="compact"
      variant="outlined"
      hide-details
      clearable
      style="max-width:110px"
    />
    <v-select
      v-if="['QUARTERLY','MONTHLY','WEEKLY','DAILY'].includes(sharedPeriod)"
      v-model="filterQuarter"
      :items="quarterOptions"
      label="Quarter"
      density="compact"
      variant="outlined"
      hide-details
      clearable
      style="max-width:150px"
    />
    <v-select
      v-if="['MONTHLY','WEEKLY','DAILY'].includes(sharedPeriod) && !filterQuarter"
      v-model="filterMonth"
      :items="monthOptions"
      label="Month"
      density="compact"
      variant="outlined"
      hide-details
      clearable
      style="max-width:130px"
    />
    <v-btn-toggle
      v-model="filterSortDir"
      density="compact"
      variant="outlined"
      :mandatory="true"
      class="ml-auto"
    >
      <v-btn value="asc" size="small" title="Oldest first">
        <v-icon size="small">mdi-sort-ascending</v-icon>
      </v-btn>
      <v-btn value="desc" size="small" title="Newest first">
        <v-icon size="small">mdi-sort-descending</v-icon>
      </v-btn>
    </v-btn-toggle>
  </div>
</v-col>
```

Also reset `filterQuarter` when `filterMonth` changes and vice versa:
```ts
watch(filterMonth, () => { if (filterMonth.value) filterQuarter.value = null })
watch(filterQuarter, () => { if (filterQuarter.value) filterMonth.value = null })
watch(sharedPeriod, () => { filterYear.value = null; filterQuarter.value = null; filterMonth.value = null })
```

| LC-A-V2 | Select DAILY, set Year=2025, Month=January ? all daily entries from January 2025 shown |
| LC-A-V3 | Sort desc ? entries sorted newest first |
| LC-A-V4 | Switching period resets year/quarter/month to null |
| LC-A-V5 | filterQuarter and filterMonth are mutually exclusive |

**LC-C-1 � Entity update:**
File: `pmo-backend/src/database/entities/construction-mov-entry.entity.ts`

- Add:
```ts

@Property({ nullable: true, type: 'integer' })

@Property({ nullable: true, length: 100 })

```

**LC-C-2 � Migration:**
New file: `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260518020000_MovFileUploadSupport.ts`
```ts
import { Migration } from '@mikro-orm/migrations';
export class Migration20260518020000_MovFileUploadSupport extends Migration {
  override async up(): Promise<void> {
    this.addSql(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_mov_entries
        ALTER COLUMN mov_link DROP NOT NULL,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS file_path   VARCHAR(500),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS file_name   VARCHAR(255),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS file_size   INTEGER,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS mime_type   VARCHAR(100);
    `);
  }
  override async down(): Promise<void> {
    this.addSql(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_mov_entries
        ALTER COLUMN mov_link SET NOT NULL,
        DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS file_path,
        DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS file_name,
        DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS file_size,
        DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS mime_type;
    `);
  }
}
```

**LC-C-3 � Service: new upload method:**
File: `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`
Add `uploadMovFile()` method:
```ts
async uploadMovFile(projectId: string, movEntryId: string, file: Express.Multer.File, userId: string): Promise<ConstructionMovEntry> {
  const entry = await this.movRepo.findOne({ id: movEntryId, projectId });
  if (!entry) throw new NotFoundException('MOV entry not found');
  const uploadResult = await this.uploadsService.uploadFile(file, userId, 'construction_mov', projectId);
  entry.filePath = uploadResult.filePath;
  entry.fileName = file.originalname;
  entry.fileSize = file.size;
  entry.mimeType = file.mimetype;
  await this.em.flush();
  return entry;
}
```

**LC-C-4 � Controller: new upload endpoint:**
File: `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.controller.ts`
```ts
@Post(':id/mov-entries/:entryId/upload-file')
@Roles('Admin', 'Staff')
@HttpCode(HttpStatus.OK)
@UseInterceptors(FileInterceptor('file', { limits: { fileSize: 15 * 1024 * 1024 } }))
uploadMovFile(
  @Param('id', ParseUUIDPipe) id: string,
  @Param('entryId', ParseUUIDPipe) entryId: string,
  @UploadedFile() file: Express.Multer.File,
  @CurrentUser() user: JwtPayload,
) {
  return this.service.uploadMovFile(id, entryId, file, user.sub);
}
```

Also update the create MOV entry DTO to make `mov_link` optional and validate that at least one of `mov_link` or file-upload is provided (service-layer validation).

**LC-C-5 � Frontend component update (`CiMovEvidenceSection.vue`):**

Add `inputMode` ref to toggle between URL and file input:
```ts
const inputMode = ref<'link' | 'file'>('link')
const fileInput = ref<File | null>(null)
const fileUploading = ref(false)
```

Update `submit()` to handle both modes:
```ts
async function submit() {
  if (inputMode.value === 'file') {
    await submitFile()
  } else {
    await submitLink()
  }
}

async function submitFile() {
  if (!fileInput.value) { toast.error('Please select a file'); return }
  if (fileInput.value.size > 15 * 1024 * 1024) { toast.error('File must be = 15 MB'); return }
  if (!formTitle.value.trim()) { toast.error('Evidence title is required'); return }
  submitting.value = true
  try {
    // First create the MOV entry (link-optional)
    const created = await api.post<{ id: string }>(`/api/construction-projects/${props.projectId}/mov-entries`, {
      related_entity_type: props.relatedEntityType,
      related_entity_id: props.relatedEntityId,
      mov_title: formTitle.value,
      mov_description: formDescription.value || undefined,
      evidence_category: formCategory.value || 'other',
      entry_date: formEntryDate.value || undefined,
      remarks: formRemarks.value || undefined,
    })
    // Then upload the file to the created entry
    const fd = new FormData()
    fd.append('file', fileInput.value)
    await api.upload(`/api/construction-projects/${props.projectId}/mov-entries/${(created as any).id}/upload-file`, fd)
    toast.success('File evidence added.')
    resetForm()
    fileInput.value = null
    await fetchEntries()
  } catch (err: unknown) {
    toast.error((err as { message?: string })?.message || 'Failed to upload file evidence.')
  } finally {
    submitting.value = false
  }
}
```

Add mode toggle UI before the add form content:
```html
<v-btn-toggle v-model="inputMode" density="compact" variant="outlined" :mandatory="true" class="mb-3">
  <v-btn value="link" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-link">URL Link</v-btn>
  <v-btn value="file" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-paperclip">File Upload</v-btn>
</v-btn-toggle>
```

Display existing entries: add a chip distinguishing file vs link:
```html
<v-chip v-if="entry.filePath" size="x-small" color="teal" variant="tonal" prepend-icon="mdi-file">
  <a :href="entry.filePath" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="text-decoration-none">{{ entry.fileName || 'File' }}</a>
</v-chip>
<v-chip v-else size="x-small" color="primary" variant="tonal" prepend-icon="mdi-link-variant">
  <a :href="entry.movLink" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="text-decoration-none">{{ entry.movTitle }}</a>
</v-chip>
```

## Phase LD � Others Tab Save Fix, Profile Image Metadata, Gallery Fullscreen Metadata, Attachment Modal Enhancement, Timeline UX Polish

**Phase:** LD
**Status:** ? COMPLETE (2026-05-18 � all 5 sub-phases implemented, pending operator verification)
**Research:** Section 2.168 (docs/research.md)
**Scope:** 5 sub-phases. LD-A (critical bug fix), LD-B, LD-C (low complexity), LD-D (medium), LD-E (low). All frontend-only except LD-A backend verification.

---

### Context

META_DISCUSSION (Section 2.168) confirmed:
1. **LD-A CRITICAL BUG**: Others Tab save sends `undefined` for empty arrays ? backend PATCH ignores them ? clearing incidents/risks/escalations never takes effect.
2. **LD-B GAP**: Profile image upload form missing `imageTakenDate` field; fullscreen preview passes `uploadedAt` where `imageTakenDate` should be.
3. **LD-C GAP**: Gallery fullscreen modal missing date metadata for profile images.
4. **LD-D ENHANCEMENT**: Attachment modal lacks inline file preview; version chip not prominent.
5. **LD-E UX POLISH**: `groupByPeriod` toggle visible/enabled when ALL period selected (confusing); group header labels are ISO format strings (machine-readable, not human-friendly).

All LA/LB/LC phases confirmed WORKING per code audit. LC-C/LC-D (MOV file upload + reporter type) require operator to confirm migration run before testing.

---

### Governance Directives (Phase LD)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| LD-D1 | Others Tab save MUST send explicit `[]` (empty array) for all 6 JSONB monitoring fields, never `undefined`. |
| LD-D2 | Profile image upload form MUST capture `imageTakenDate` (type="date" field, optional). |
| LD-D3 | Fullscreen preview for profile images MUST pass `img.imageTakenDate` (not `uploadedAt`) to the preview dialog. Upload date and taken date are distinct � show both when available. |
| LD-D4 | Gallery fullscreen dialog (galleryFullscreen) MUST display `imageTakenDate` chip in both profile and general sections. |
| LD-D5 | Attachment preview modal MAY show inline image preview for image/* mime types using a `<v-img>` tag when `selectedDoc.filePath` is set and doc type is image. For non-image files, current download-only behavior is retained. |
| LD-D6 | Version chip in document modal MUST be visually distinguished (e.g., `color="success"` or `variant="flat"`) to signal it is the current (latest) version. |
| LD-D7 | `groupByPeriod` chip/toggle MUST be hidden or disabled when `sharedPeriod === 'ALL'`. |
| LD-D8 | Group label keys from `getGroupKey()` MUST be formatted human-readably in the section headers (e.g., `2025-W14` ? `Week 14, 2025`; `2025-04` ? `April 2025`; `2025-Q2` ? `Q2 2025`; daily stays as date). |

---

### Sub-Phase LD-A � Others Tab Save Fix (CRITICAL)

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Scope:** Fix save payload to send explicit `[]` instead of `undefined` for all 6 JSONB monitoring fields.

**LD-A-1:** In `save()` function (around line 448�454), replace the conditional pattern with direct array references:

**Before:**
```typescript
status_updates: statusUpdateRows.value.length > 0 ? statusUpdateRows.value : undefined,
readiness_documents: readinessDocRows.value.length > 0 ? readinessDocRows.value : undefined,
signatories: signatoryRows.value.length > 0 ? signatoryRows.value : undefined,
incident_log: incidentLogRows.value.length > 0 ? incidentLogRows.value : undefined,
risk_register: riskRegisterRows.value.length > 0 ? riskRegisterRows.value : undefined,
escalation_records: escalationRows.value.length > 0 ? escalationRows.value : undefined,
```

**After:**
```typescript
status_updates: statusUpdateRows.value,
readiness_documents: readinessDocRows.value,
signatories: signatoryRows.value,
incident_log: incidentLogRows.value,
risk_register: riskRegisterRows.value,
escalation_records: escalationRows.value,
```

**Verification:**
- LD-A-V1: Add 2 incidents ? save ? detail shows 2 incidents ?
- LD-A-V2: Delete both incidents in edit ? save ? detail shows 0 incidents (empty state) ?

### Sub-Phase LD-E � Timeline UX Polish

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**LD-E-1:** Hide/disable `groupByPeriod` chip toggle when `sharedPeriod === 'ALL'`. Add `v-if="sharedPeriod !== 'ALL'"` to the groupByPeriod chip/toggle element.

**LD-E-2:** Add `formatGroupLabel(key: string, period: string): string` helper:

```typescript
function formatGroupLabel(key: string, period: string): string {
  if (period === 'DAILY') return key  // already YYYY-MM-DD � format as locale date
    ? new Date(key + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-PH', { weekday: 'short', year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })
    : key
  if (period === 'WEEKLY') {
    const [yr, wk] = key.split('-W')
    return `Week ${wk}, ${yr}`
  }
  if (period === 'MONTHLY') {
    const [yr, mo] = key.split('-')
    return new Date(Number(yr), Number(mo) - 1, 1).toLocaleDateString('en-PH', { month: 'long', year: 'numeric' })
  }
  if (period === 'QUARTERLY') {
    const [yr, q] = key.split('-Q')
    return `Q${q} ${yr}`
  }
  return key
}
```

Apply `formatGroupLabel(key, sharedPeriod)` in grouped section headers instead of raw `key`.

**Verification:**
- LD-E-V1: `sharedPeriod === 'ALL'` ? groupByPeriod toggle hidden ?
- LD-E-V2: Weekly grouped view ? headers show "Week 14, 2025" not "2025-W14" ?
- LD-E-V3: Monthly grouped view ? "April 2025" not "2025-04" ?

## Phase LE � Timeline Dialog UX Unification (Add = Edit, Consistent MOV Experience)

**Phase:** LE
**Status:** ? COMPLETE (2026-05-18)
**Scope:** Frontend-only. `edit-[id].vue` only.

### Problem

Milestone and Diary dialogs had **three distinct visual states**:
1. Pre-save Add: form only, no MOV section, "Add" button
2. Post-save Add (KX-F): form hidden, MOV section only, "Done" button
3. Edit: form + MOV section together, "Cancel / Save" buttons

This made Add feel structurally different from Edit � users opening Edit saw the full context (form + MOV), users in Add saw a 2-step flow with a jarring mid-session layout shift.

### Solution

**After Add save succeeds ? transition the dialog to Edit mode** by setting `editingId = newId` and `dialogMode = 'edit'`. The dialog stays open and immediately renders in the Edit state: form populated + MOV section visible + Cancel/Save buttons.

Result: Add and Edit are visually and behaviorally identical after the initial submission. No layout shift, no "Done" button anomaly, no 3-state template complexity.

### Changes

1. Removed `milestoneJustSavedId` and `diaryJustSavedId` refs entirely.
2. In `saveMilestone()` create branch: after POST, set `milestoneEditingId = newId` + `milestoneDialogMode = 'edit'` instead of setting `JustSavedId`.
3. In `saveDiary()` create branch: same pattern with `diaryEditingId` + `diaryDialogMode`.
4. Milestone dialog: collapsed 3 `<template>` blocks into 2 (`v-if edit mode` + `v-else add mode`). Post-save state now reuses the edit-mode template with no extra code.
5. Diary dialog: same template collapse.
6. Cancel in edit mode calls `resetMilestoneForm()` / clears `diaryEditingId` for clean state.
7. `saveDiary()` edit branch: added `diaryEditingId.value = null` on close (previously missing reset).

### Governance Directives

| ID | Rule |
|----|------|
| LE-D1 | Milestone/Diary Add dialog MUST transition to Edit mode after successful POST (set editingId + dialogMode = 'edit'). |
| LE-D2 | MOV section is NEVER shown before entity exists (pre-save). It appears in edit mode only. |
| LE-D3 | `milestoneJustSavedId` and `diaryJustSavedId` are REMOVED � do not reintroduce. |
| LE-D4 | Cancel in Edit mode (including post-Add) MUST reset editingId to null. |

### Verification

| Test ID | Scenario |
|---------|----------|
| LE-V1 | Click "Add Milestone" ? fill form ? click "Add" ? form stays open, MOV section appears, title says "Edit Milestone", Cancel/Save buttons shown |
| LE-V2 | Click pencil on existing milestone ? form + MOV shown immediately (no change) |
| LE-V3 | In post-Add state, edit form fields ? click "Save" ? milestone updated, dialog closes |
| LE-V4 | In post-Add state, click "Cancel" ? dialog closes cleanly, no stale state |
| LE-V5 | Same tests for Diary Entry |

---

*Phase LE complete.*

---

## Phase LF � Timeline Date Governance, Calendar Filter Normalization

**Status:** ✅ LF-A/LF-B/LF-D implemented in `detail-[id].vue`. LF-C: operator migration verification pending. LF-E: deferred.
**Research:** Section 2.169
**Prompt origin:** META_DISCUSSION � CSU CORE: Timeline Date Governance, Calendar Filter Normalization, MOV Dialog Consistency Refactor

### Problem

Two verified governance gaps remain in the Detail page Timeline tab:

1. **`availableYears` hardcoded to 2020�2030** (line 518, `detail-[id].vue`): The Year dropdown in the filter bar and the Calendar year selector both use this array. Infrastructure projects with milestones dated before 2020 or after 2030 cannot be filtered or navigated to.

2. **`periodBounds` conflates time-window with grouping granularity** (lines 540�588): Selecting DAILY with no narrowing shows only today's entries; WEEKLY shows only this week; MONTHLY shows only this month. The period selector is intended as a GROUPING control (group by day/week/month/quarter), not a date-window filter � date windowing should only apply when Year/Quarter/Month narrowing is explicitly set.

Additionally: two Phase LC migrations (MovFileUploadSupport, AddTimelineReporterType) exist in code but their execution status is unconfirmed.

### Sub-phases

---

#### LF-A � Fix `availableYears` (1 line)

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` line 518

**Change:** Replace hardcoded 11-year array with a wide static range 1990 to currentYear+15.

```typescript
// BEFORE
const availableYears = Array.from({ length: 11 }, (_, i) => 2020 + i)

// AFTER
const availableYears = Array.from(
  { length: new Date().getFullYear() + 15 - 1990 + 1 },
  (_, i) => 1990 + i
)
```

**Scope:** `detail-[id].vue` only. No backend changes. No migration.

**Verification:**
- LF-A-V1: Year dropdown shows years from 1990 to currentYear+15
- LF-A-V2: Calendar year selector shows same range
- LF-A-V3: Selecting year 2015 and filtering shows milestones with targetDate in 2015

---

#### LF-B � Fix `periodBounds`: Decouple Period from Time Window

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` lines 540�588 + line 1575 (Active Window chip)

**Problem:** When period = DAILY with no year/quarter/month narrowing ? shows only today's entries. Expected: show ALL entries, grouped by day.

**Root cause:** `periodBounds` falls back to today/this week/this month when no narrowing is set. This makes the period selector act as a date-window filter instead of a grouping control.

**Change 1 � `periodBounds` computed:** Rewrite to return empty bounds (show all) when no narrowing set:

```typescript
const periodBounds = computed((): { from: string; to: string } => {
  if (sharedPeriod.value === 'ALL') return { from: '', to: '' }
  // No narrowing ? show all entries; period controls grouping only
  if (!filterYear.value && !filterQuarter.value && !filterMonth.value) return { from: '', to: '' }

  const year = filterYear.value ?? new Date().getFullYear()
  const pad = (n: number) => String(n).padStart(2, '0')
  const fmt = (d: Date) => `${d.getFullYear()}-${pad(d.getMonth() + 1)}-${pad(d.getDate())}`

  if (filterQuarter.value) {
    const q = filterQuarter.value
    return { from: fmt(new Date(year, (q - 1) * 3, 1)), to: fmt(new Date(year, q * 3, 0)) }
  }
  if (filterMonth.value) {
    const m = filterMonth.value - 1
    return { from: fmt(new Date(year, m, 1)), to: fmt(new Date(year, m + 1, 0)) }
  }
  // Year only ? full year window
  return { from: fmt(new Date(year, 0, 1)), to: fmt(new Date(year, 11, 31)) }
})
```

**Change 2 � Active Window chip (line 1575):** The chip currently shows `{{ periodBounds.from }} � {{ periodBounds.to }}`. After LF-B, when no narrowing is set, `periodBounds` returns `{ from: '', to: '' }`, so the chip should show "All dates" instead of " � ":

```html
<!-- BEFORE -->
<v-chip size="small" color="primary" variant="tonal">{{ periodBounds.from }} � {{ periodBounds.to }}</v-chip>

<!-- AFTER -->
<v-chip size="small" color="primary" variant="tonal">
  {{ periodBounds.from ? `${periodBounds.from} � ${periodBounds.to}` : 'All dates' }}
</v-chip>
```

**Scope:** `detail-[id].vue` only. No backend changes. No migration.

**Verification:**
- LF-B-V1: Select DAILY with no year/quarter/month ? all milestones/entries shown, grouped by day when groupByPeriod is ON
- LF-B-V2: Select WEEKLY with no narrowing ? all entries shown, grouped by week
- LF-B-V3: Select MONTHLY + Year=2025 + Month=March ? only March 2025 entries shown
- LF-B-V4: Select QUARTERLY + Year=2024 + Q2 ? only Q2 2024 entries shown
- LF-B-V5: Active Window chip shows "All dates" when period set but no narrowing
- LF-B-V6: Active Window chip shows date range when narrowing is set

---

#### LF-C � Verify LC-C/LC-D Migrations (Operational)

**Not a code change � operator action required.**

Two MikroORM migrations must be confirmed executed:
- `Migration20260518020000_MovFileUploadSupport` � ALTERs `mov_link` to nullable; ADDs file columns to `construction_mov_entries`
- `Migration20260518030000_AddTimelineReporterType` � ADDs `reporter_type` to `construction_timeline_entries`

**Verification query:**
```sql
SELECT name, executed_at FROM mikro_orm_migrations
WHERE name IN (
  'Migration20260518020000_MovFileUploadSupport',
  'Migration20260518030000_AddTimelineReporterType'
)
ORDER BY executed_at;
```

If NOT present: run `npx mikro-orm migration:up` in `pmo-backend/`.

**Risk if not run:**
- LC-C: Any MOV save without a URL will fail with `null value in column "mov_link" violates not-null constraint`
- LC-D: `reporter_type` filter chip in frontend will silently show no data (no DB error)

---

#### LF-D � Calendar Auto-Navigate on Filter Change

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` � add watcher after `calendarYear`/`calendarMonth` declarations (around line 661)

**Problem:** When user sets filter Year=2024 + Q1, the calendar still shows the current month. The user must manually navigate the calendar to Q1 2024 to see any entries.

**Fix:** Watch `periodBounds` � when it yields a non-empty `from` date, auto-navigate calendar to that month/year:

```typescript
watch(periodBounds, (bounds) => {
  if (bounds.from) {
    calendarYear.value  = parseInt(bounds.from.slice(0, 4))
    calendarMonth.value = parseInt(bounds.from.slice(5, 7)) - 1
  }
}, { immediate: false })
```

**Scope:** `detail-[id].vue` only. Low risk � only triggers when `periodBounds.from` is non-empty (i.e., when Year/Quarter/Month narrowing is set).

**Verification:**
- LF-D-V1: Set filter Year=2023 + Q3 ? calendar auto-navigates to July 2023
- LF-D-V2: Set filter Year=2025 + Month=February ? calendar auto-navigates to February 2025
- LF-D-V3: Select period DAILY with no narrowing ? calendar does NOT navigate (bounds empty)

---

#### LF-E � Edit Page Timeline Filter/Calendar Views (DEFERRED)

**Status:** DEFERRED � pending operator decision

Adding the full filter bar + view mode toggle (List/Table/Calendar) to the Edit Project timeline tab is a significant scope expansion requiring:
- Shared filter state composable (or duplicated state)
- Calendar component instance in `edit-[id].vue`
- Coexistence of CRUD dialogs and read/filter views in same tab

**Operator must explicitly authorize this before implementation proceeds.**

---

### Governance Directives

| ID | Rule |
|----|------|
| LF-D1 | `availableYears` in `detail-[id].vue` MUST generate a wide range (1990 to currentYear+15 minimum). Do not hardcode a fixed 11-year window. |
| LF-D2 | `periodBounds` MUST return `{ from: '', to: '' }` when no Year/Quarter/Month narrowing is set, regardless of period value. Period selector is a GROUPING control only. |
| LF-D3 | Active Window chip MUST show "All dates" when `periodBounds.from` is empty and period is not ALL. |
| LF-D4 | When `periodBounds.from` becomes non-empty, calendar MUST auto-navigate to that month/year. |
| LF-D5 | LC-C/LC-D migrations MUST be confirmed executed before MOV file upload and reporter_type filter can be considered production-ready. |

### Verification Matrix

| Test ID | Scenario |
|---------|----------|
| LF-V1 | Year dropdown in filter bar shows 1990 through currentYear+15 |
| LF-V2 | Calendar year selector shows same range |
| LF-V3 | Select DAILY (no narrowing) ? all milestones/entries visible, grouped by day |
| LF-V4 | Select WEEKLY (no narrowing) ? all entries visible, grouped by week |
| LF-V5 | Select MONTHLY + Year=2025 + Month=February ? only Feb 2025 entries |
| LF-V6 | Active Window chip shows "All dates" when period set with no narrowing |
| LF-V7 | Set Year=2024 + Q2 ? calendar auto-jumps to April 2024 |
| LF-V8 | Confirm both LC-C/LC-D migrations in `mikro_orm_migrations` table |

---

*Phase LF complete (2026-05-20). LF-A/B/D implemented in `detail-[id].vue`. LF-C requires operator verification of Migration20260518020000 and Migration20260518030000 in `mikro_orm_migrations`. LF-E deferred pending operator authorization.*
