## Phase LG � Timeline Dialog MOV Unification + Phase LF Reconciliation

**Status:** ? PENDING Phase 3 authorization
**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` (LG-A, LG-B) | `docs/plan.md` header (LG-C) | Operator verification (LG-D)
**Research:** Sections 2.170�2.177
**Predecessor:** Phase LF (LF-A/B/D code confirmed applied)

---

### Problem Statement

Screenshots confirm: the **Add Milestone** dialog in `edit-[id].vue` shows form fields only � the MOV Evidence section is absent. The **Edit Milestone** dialog shows form + MOV Evidence section. This creates a visually inconsistent UX that violates the unified-dialog principle.

Root cause: MOV section is gated by `v-if="milestoneDialogMode === 'edit' && milestoneEditingId"`. In create mode, `milestoneEditingId` is null ? section hidden. The same pattern exists in the Diary (Work Log) dialog.

The fix must maintain the architectural constraint (MOV requires an entity UUID which only exists after the first save) while ensuring visual parity between Add and Edit modes.

---

### LG-A � Milestone Dialog MOV Section Unification

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Lines affected:** ~2834�2862 (the `v-if/v-else` MOV block at end of Milestone dialog)

**Current `v-else` (create mode):**
```vue
<template v-else>
  <v-card-actions>
    <v-spacer />
    <v-btn variant="text" ... @click="milestoneDialog = false">Cancel</v-btn>
    <v-btn color="primary" ... @click="saveMilestone">Add</v-btn>
  </v-card-actions>
</template>
```

**Required change � add MOV placeholder before the card-actions:**
```vue
<template v-else>
  <v-divider />
  <v-card-text>
    <div class="text-subtitle-2 font-weight-medium mb-2 d-flex align-center ga-1">
      <v-icon size="small" color="teal">mdi-link-variant</v-icon>
      MOV Evidence
    </div>
    <v-alert type="info" variant="tonal" density="compact" class="mt-1">
      MOV evidence can be attached after saving the milestone.
    </v-alert>
  </v-card-text>
  <v-divider />
  <v-card-actions>
    <v-spacer />
    <v-btn variant="text" :disabled="milestoneSubmitting" @click="milestoneDialog = false">Cancel</v-btn>
    <v-btn color="primary" :loading="milestoneSubmitting" @click="saveMilestone">Add</v-btn>
  </v-card-actions>
</template>
```

**Result:** Both Add and Edit modes render the MOV Evidence section header. In create mode, an informational alert replaces the component. After clicking "Add" and saving, the dialog transitions to edit mode with the full `CiMovEvidenceSection` active (existing behavior preserved).

**Verification:**
- LG-A-V1: Open Add Milestone dialog ? MOV Evidence section header visible with info alert
- LG-A-V2: Fill form + click Add ? dialog transitions to Edit mode ? full MOV section active
- LG-A-V3: Open existing Edit Milestone dialog ? full MOV section shown (no regression)
- LG-A-V4: Cancel in Add mode ? dialog closes cleanly

---

### LG-B � Diary (Work Log) Dialog MOV Section Unification

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Lines affected:** ~3158�3190 (the `v-if/v-else` MOV block at end of Diary dialog)

**Same pattern as LG-A.** Apply identical fix:

In the Diary dialog's `v-else` (create mode) branch, add the MOV Evidence placeholder before `v-card-actions`:
```vue
<v-divider />
<v-card-text>
  <div class="text-subtitle-2 font-weight-medium mb-2 d-flex align-center ga-1">
    <v-icon size="small" color="teal">mdi-link-variant</v-icon>
    MOV Evidence
  </div>
  <v-alert type="info" variant="tonal" density="compact" class="mt-1">
    MOV evidence can be attached after saving the work log entry.
  </v-alert>
</v-card-text>
<v-divider />
```

**Verification:**
- LG-B-V1: Open Add Work Log dialog ? MOV Evidence section header visible with info alert
- LG-B-V2: Fill form + click Add ? dialog transitions to Edit mode ? full MOV section active
- LG-B-V3: Open existing Edit Work Log dialog ? full MOV section shown (no regression)

| LG-V1 | Add Milestone dialog opened � shows form + MOV header + info alert |
| LG-V2 | Add Milestone ? click Add ? dialog stays open in edit mode � MOV section active |
| LG-V3 | Edit Milestone opened � shows form + full MOV section (no regression) |
| LG-V4 | Add Work Log dialog opened � shows form + MOV header + info alert |
| LG-V5 | Add Work Log ? click Add ? dialog stays open in edit mode � MOV section active |
| LG-V6 | Edit Work Log opened � shows form + full MOV section (no regression) |
| LG-V7 | Confirm LF-A: year dropdown shows 1990 through currentYear+15 |
| LG-V8 | Confirm LF-B: WEEKLY with no narrowing ? all entries shown, no date restriction |
| LG-V9 | Confirm LF-D: set Year=2024+Q2 ? calendar auto-jumps to April 2024 |
| LG-V10 | Confirm LG-D migrations in `mikro_orm_migrations` table |

## Phase LH — Zero-Save-First MOV: Deferred Buffer Architecture

**Status:** ⬜ PENDING Phase 3
**Scope:** `edit-[id].vue`, new `CiMovPendingSection.vue`
**Prerequisites:** LG-A ✅, LG-B ✅

### Objective

Eliminate the save-first-before-MOV pattern. MOV evidence must be queueable during create mode. Dialog must close fully on successful create (no edit-mode transition).

### Sub-phases

| ID | Step | File | Risk |
|----|------|------|------|
| LH-A | Create `CiMovPendingSection.vue` — lightweight MOV queue component (local state only; title, link, type inputs; add/remove row; no API calls) | `components/coi/CiMovPendingSection.vue` (new) | Low |
| LH-B | Milestone dialog `v-else` branch: replace `v-alert` placeholder with `<CiMovPendingSection v-model="pendingMilestoneMovs" />` | `edit-[id].vue` lines 2856–2870 | Low |
| LH-C | Add `pendingMilestoneMovs: Ref<PendingMovEntry[]>` reactive ref; type `PendingMovEntry = { title: string; link: string; type: 'URL' | 'DRIVE' }` | `edit-[id].vue` script | Low |
| LH-D | `saveMilestone()` create-path: after POST succeeds, drain `pendingMilestoneMovs` via sequential `api.post('/construction-projects/:id/mov-entries', ...)` calls using `created.id`; then close dialog and reset form | `edit-[id].vue` saveMilestone() | Medium |
| LH-E | Remove edit-mode transition on create (`milestoneDialogMode = 'edit'` + `milestoneEditingId = created.id`) — replaced by drain-then-close | `edit-[id].vue` saveMilestone() | Medium |
| LH-F | Diary dialog `v-else` branch: same as LH-B — replace `v-alert` with `<CiMovPendingSection v-model="pendingDiaryMovs" />` | `edit-[id].vue` lines 3193–3211 | Low |
| LH-G | Add `pendingDiaryMovs: Ref<PendingMovEntry[]>` reactive ref | `edit-[id].vue` script | Low |
| LH-H | `saveDiary()` create-path: same drain-then-close as LH-D | `edit-[id].vue` saveDiary() | Medium |
| LH-I | Remove diary edit-mode transition on create | `edit-[id].vue` saveDiary() | Medium |

### `CiMovPendingSection.vue` Specification

- Props: `modelValue: PendingMovEntry[]` (v-model)
- Renders table of queued entries with "Add Entry" button
- Each row: title (text), link (text), type selector (URL / Google Drive)
- No API calls — pure local state proxy
- Shows entry count badge: "0 evidence items queued"
- On save, parent drains entries; component resets

### Governance Directives

| ID | Rule |
|----|------|
| LH-D1 | `CiMovPendingSection` MUST NOT call any API — local state only. |
| LH-D2 | Drain loop in `saveMilestone()` and `saveDiary()` MUST be fire-and-forget non-blocking — MOV failures must NOT rollback the entity creation. Show a non-blocking toast for partial failures. |
| LH-D3 | After successful create + drain, dialog MUST close fully (`milestoneDialog = false`, `diaryDialog = false`). |
| LH-D4 | Edit mode opens the FULL `CiMovEvidenceSection` (existing behavior preserved). |
| LH-D5 | `pendingMilestoneMovs` and `pendingDiaryMovs` MUST be reset when dialog closes (both on save and on cancel). |

### Verification Matrix

| Test ID | Scenario |
|---------|----------|
| LH-V1 | Add Milestone dialog opened → shows form + MOV pending queue section |
| LH-V2 | Add MOV entry in pending queue → entry appears in queue list |
| LH-V3 | Click "Add" (save create) → dialog closes → milestone visible in list → MOV entries attached |
| LH-V4 | Click "Cancel" during create → dialog closes → no entity created → no MOV created |
| LH-V5 | Edit Milestone opened → shows form + full `CiMovEvidenceSection` (no regression) |
| LH-V6 | Same scenarios for Diary dialog (LH-V1 through LH-V5 equivalent) |

---

## Phase LI — Milestone Audit Trail Backend

**Status:** ⬜ PENDING Phase 3
**Scope:** Backend — entity, service, controller, migration
**Prerequisites:** None (independent)

### Objective

Add full audit trail tracking (`createdBy`, `updatedBy`) to milestone CRUD and fire activity log entries for all milestone operations.

### Sub-phases

| ID | Step | File | Risk |
|----|------|------|------|
| LI-A | Add `@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'uuid' }) createdBy?: string` and `@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'uuid' }) updatedBy?: string` and `@Property({ nullable: true, onUpdate: () => new Date(), columnType: 'timestamptz' }) updatedAt?: Date` to `ConstructionMilestone` entity | `construction-milestone.entity.ts` | Low |
| LI-B | Generate MikroORM migration: `ALTER TABLE construction_milestones ADD COLUMN created_by UUID, ADD COLUMN updated_by UUID, ADD COLUMN updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ` | `pmo-backend/src/database/migrations/Migration*.ts` (new) | Low |
| LI-C | Add `userId: string, user?: JwtPayload` params to `createMilestone()` service method; assign `entity.createdBy = userId` | `construction-projects.service.ts:957` | Low |
| LI-D | Add `userId: string, user?: JwtPayload` params to `updateMilestone()` service method; assign `entity.updatedBy = userId` | `construction-projects.service.ts:979` | Low |
| LI-E | Add `userId: string, user?: JwtPayload` params to `removeMilestone()` service method | `construction-projects.service.ts:1015` | Low |
| LI-F | Add `@CurrentUser() user: JwtPayload` to controller `createMilestone` handler; pass `user.userId, user` to service | `construction-projects.controller.ts:218` | Low |
| LI-G | Add `@CurrentUser() user: JwtPayload` to controller `updateMilestone` and `removeMilestone` handlers | `construction-projects.controller.ts:225,236` | Low |
| LI-H | Call `this.fireLog(user, ActivityAction.CREATE, projectId, { milestoneId: entity.id })` in `createMilestone` after flush | `construction-projects.service.ts` | Low |
| LI-I | Call `this.fireLog(user, ActivityAction.UPDATE, projectId, { milestoneId })` in `updateMilestone` after flush | `construction-projects.service.ts` | Low |
| LI-J | Call `this.fireLog(user, ActivityAction.DELETE, projectId, { milestoneId })` in `removeMilestone` | `construction-projects.service.ts` | Low |
| LI-K | Add `@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'uuid' }) updatedBy?: string` to `ConstructionTimelineEntry` entity; MikroORM migration; set `entity.updatedBy = userId` in `updateTimelineEntry` | `construction-timeline-entry.entity.ts` + migration | Low |

### Governance Directives

| ID | Rule |
|----|------|
| LI-D1 | `fireLog()` calls for milestone CUD MUST use `projectId` (not `milestoneId`) as entityId — consistent with existing project-level log pattern. Include `milestoneId` in metadata. |
| LI-D2 | `createdBy` and `updatedBy` MUST be nullable (existing rows have no values). |
| LI-D3 | Migration MUST use MikroORM TypeScript migration format (not raw SQL in `/database/migrations/`). |

## Phase LM — CiMovPendingSection Field Parity

**Objective:** Expand `CiMovPendingSection.vue` to include all MOV fields that `CiMovEvidenceSection` supports, making Add mode MOV input field-complete with Edit mode.

**Research basis:** Section 2.186, 2.192

### LM Pre-Step: Backend DTO Verification
Before implementing, verify `CreateMovEntryDto` in `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/dto/create-mov-entry.dto.ts` exposes:
- `mov_entry_date` (optional ISO date string)
- `mov_category` (optional string)
- `mov_description` (optional string)
- `mov_remarks` (optional string)

If missing, add `@IsOptional() @IsString()` decorated fields to the DTO before frontend expansion.

### LM-A: Expand `PendingMovEntry` Interface
```ts
export interface PendingMovEntry {
  title: string
  link: string
  type: 'URL' | 'DRIVE'
  entryDate: string      // ISO date string, optional
  category: string       // optional
  description: string    // optional
  remarks: string        // optional
}
```

### LM-B: Expand `CiMovPendingSection.vue` Row Card UI
Each pending row card becomes a multi-row form (not just a single inline row):
- Row 1: Type selector + Title (required) + Delete button
- Row 2: URL field + Entry Date picker (`v-menu + v-date-picker`)
- Row 3: Category select + Description
- Row 4: Remarks
- Use `v-expansion-panels` or always-visible fields depending on form density preference
- Default values: `{ title: '', link: '', type: 'URL', entryDate: '', category: '', description: '', remarks: '' }`

### LM-C: Update `addRow()` Default Object
Include new fields with empty string defaults.

### LM-D: Update `updateRow()` Type Signature
Expand `field` keyof to include new fields.

### LM-E: Update Drain Loop in `saveMilestone()` and `saveDiary()`
Map full `PendingMovEntry` to API payload:
```ts
{
  related_entity_type: 'MILESTONE' | 'TIMELINE_ENTRY',
  related_entity_id: createdId,
  mov_title: row.title,
  mov_link: row.link || undefined,
  mov_entry_date: row.entryDate || undefined,
  mov_category: row.category || undefined,
  mov_description: row.description || undefined,
  mov_remarks: row.remarks || undefined,
}
```

### LM Constraints
- File upload NOT included in pending section — file uploads require presigned URL or multipart which needs a persisted entity ID. Users needing file MOV must use Edit mode. This is architecturally sound — URL links cover the primary enterprise use case.
- No backend structural changes to entities — only DTO field additions if missing.

---

## Phase LN — Calendar View Split-Layout Refactor

**Objective:** Replace the current full-width flat calendar grid with a split-layout: left = calendar, right = compact scrollable log list panel.

**Research basis:** Section 2.187

### LN-A: Add `selectedCalendarDate` Ref
```ts
const selectedCalendarDate = ref<string>('')  // YYYY-MM-DD
```

### LN-B: Add `calendarDateEntries` Computed
```ts
const calendarDateEntries = computed(() => {
  if (!selectedCalendarDate.value) return { milestones: [], workLogs: [] }
  const d = selectedCalendarDate.value
  return {
    milestones: filteredMilestones.value.filter((m: any) =>
      (m.targetDate ?? '').slice(0, 10) === d ||
      (m.startDate ?? '').slice(0, 10) === d
    ),
    workLogs: filteredWorkLog.value.filter((e: any) =>
      (e.entryDate ?? '').slice(0, 10) === d
    ),
  }
})
```

### LN-C: Calendar Grid Layout Change
Wrap current calendar card in a `<v-row>`:
```html
<v-row v-if="timelineViewMode === 'calendar'">
  <!-- Left: calendar grid (col md=7) -->
  <v-col cols="12" md="7">
    <!-- existing v-card calendar grid; cells are now clickable to set selectedCalendarDate -->
  </v-col>
  <!-- Right: log list panel (col md=5) -->
  <v-col cols="12" md="5">
    <CiCalendarLogPanel
      :milestones="calendarDateEntries.milestones"
      :work-logs="calendarDateEntries.workLogs"
      :selected-date="selectedCalendarDate"
      @open-milestone="openMilestoneDetail"
      @open-work-log="openWorkLogDetail"
      @fullscreen-milestones="milestonesDialogOpen = true"
      @fullscreen-work-logs="workLogDialogOpen = true"
    />
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

### LN-D: Calendar Cell Click Handler
Add `@click="selectedCalendarDate = cell.dateStr"` to each calendar cell div. Add `cell.dateStr` to `calendarDays` computed output (it already has `date` which is day number — add full ISO string).

### LN-E: Highlight Selected Cell
Add conditional style to calendar cell: `:style="cell.dateStr === selectedCalendarDate ? 'background:#e3f2fd;border-color:#1976d2' : 'background:#fafafa;border:1px solid #e0e0e0'"`.

### LN-F: Right Panel Auto-Month-Navigate
When right-panel item is clicked, if the item's date is not in the currently displayed month, update `calendarYear` and `calendarMonth` to navigate to it before setting `selectedCalendarDate`.

### LN-G: New Component `CiCalendarLogPanel.vue`
Create `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiCalendarLogPanel.vue`:
- Props: `milestones`, `workLogs`, `selectedDate`
- Emits: `openMilestone`, `openWorkLog`, `fullscreenMilestones`, `fullscreenWorkLogs`
- Two sections: Milestones card (with fullscreen icon) + Work Logs card (with fullscreen icon)
- Each section: `<v-list>` with items showing title, type/status chip, progress, date
- Scrollable: `max-height: 320px; overflow-y: auto` per section
- Empty state: "Select a date on the calendar to view entries" when no date selected
- "No milestones/entries for this date" when date selected but list is empty

### LN Constraints
- `calendarDays` computed must add `dateStr` (full ISO) to each cell object
- Existing `prevMonth`/`nextMonth` functions remain unchanged
- Calendar grid min-height can be reduced slightly since right panel shares vertical space

---

## Phase LO — Table View Two-Column Independent Panels

**Objective:** Refactor table mode from a single full-width milestone table (with work logs hidden) to two equal independent panels side-by-side.

**Research basis:** Section 2.188

### LO-A: Remove Full-Width Override in Table Mode
Change milestone column binding:
```html
<!-- Before -->
<v-col cols="12" :md="timelineViewMode === 'table' ? 12 : 6">
<!-- After -->
<v-col cols="12" md="6">
```

### LO-B: Show Work Log Column in Table Mode
Change work log column condition:
```html
<!-- Before -->
<v-col v-if="timelineViewMode === 'list'" cols="12" md="6">
<!-- After -->
<v-col v-if="timelineViewMode !== 'calendar'" cols="12" md="6">
```

### LO-C: Add Table Mode to Work Log Column
Inside the work log column, add a `v-data-table` branch for table mode parallel to the existing list/grouped views:
```html
<v-data-table
  v-else-if="timelineViewMode === 'table'"
  :items="filteredWorkLog"
  :headers="workLogTableHeaders"
  density="compact"
  item-value="id"
  :items-per-page="15"
>
  <!-- custom column templates: entryType chip, date format, actions -->
</v-data-table>
```

### LO-D: Add Fullscreen Buttons to Column Headers (Table Mode)
In table mode, add a fullscreen icon button in each column's `v-card-title`:
```html
<v-btn
  v-if="timelineViewMode === 'table'"
  icon="mdi-fullscreen"
  variant="text"
  size="x-small"
  title="View all milestones"
  @click="milestonesDialogOpen = true"
/>
```
Same for work log column → `workLogDialogOpen = true`.

### LO-E: Work Log Search in Table Mode
Add a search `v-text-field` inside the work log table card (mirrors existing `workLogSearch` in the work log dialog). Bind to existing `workLogSearch` ref or add a separate `tableWorkLogSearch` ref.

### LO Constraints
- List view behavior is UNCHANGED — both columns show in list mode at `md="6"`
- Grouped view inside each column remains in list mode only
- The slice(0, 5) limitation in list mode milestone list is NOT changed here (it already has "View All" button)

---

## Phase LP — Year Range Normalization (1900–2100+)

**Objective:** Extend `availableYears` from 1990-based to 1900-based, and update Vuetify date picker `max` to 2100.

**Research basis:** Section 2.190

### LP-A: Fix `availableYears` in `detail-[id].vue`
```ts
// Before:
const availableYears = computed(() =>
  Array.from({ length: 15 + (new Date().getFullYear() - 1990) }, (_, i) => 1990 + i)
)
// After:
const availableYears = computed(() =>
  Array.from({ length: new Date().getFullYear() + 75 - 1900 + 1 }, (_, i) => 1900 + i)
)
```

### LP-B: Fix `availableYears` in `edit-[id].vue`
Same pattern — locate and update the `availableYears` computed (or constant) in edit page.

### LP-C: Update Date Picker `max` in All Pages
Change `max="2040-12-31"` → `max="2100-12-31"` in all `v-date-picker` components in:
- `edit-[id].vue` (milestone form + diary form date pickers)
- `detail-[id].vue` (filter bar date pickers)

### LP Constraints
- `availableYears` is used for `v-select` year dropdowns — large array (200 items) is acceptable for a select
- No backend impact
- Single-phase, low-risk

---

## Phase LQ — List View Pagination

**Objective:** Replace `slice(0, 5)` hard caps in list mode with proper `v-pagination` controls.

**Research basis:** Section 2.196

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

### LQ-A: Add Page Refs
```ts
const milestonePage = ref(1)
const workLogPage = ref(1)
const itemsPerPage = 5
```
Reset both to `1` whenever filters change (add a `watch` on `filterYear`, `sharedPeriod`, `filterViewType`, `milestoneStatusFilter`).

### LQ-B: Computed Paged Milestones
```ts
const pagedMilestones = computed(() => {
  const start = (milestonePage.value - 1) * itemsPerPage
  return filteredMilestones.value.slice(start, start + itemsPerPage)
})
const milestoneTotalPages = computed(() =>
  Math.ceil(filteredMilestones.value.length / itemsPerPage)
)
```

### LQ-C: Computed Paged Work Logs
```ts
const pagedWorkLog = computed(() => {
  const start = (workLogPage.value - 1) * itemsPerPage
  return filteredWorkLog.value.slice(start, start + itemsPerPage)
})
const workLogTotalPages = computed(() =>
  Math.ceil(filteredWorkLog.value.length / itemsPerPage)
)
```

### LQ-D: Update Template — Milestone List Mode
Replace `v-for="(milestone, idx) in filteredMilestones.slice(0, 5)"` → `v-for="milestone in pagedMilestones"`.
Add below the milestone list:
```html
<v-pagination
  v-if="milestoneTotalPages > 1"
  v-model="milestonePage"
  :length="milestoneTotalPages"
  density="compact"
  rounded
  class="mt-2"
/>
```

### LQ-E: Update Template — Work Log List Mode
Replace `v-for="e in filteredWorkLog.slice(0, 5)"` → `v-for="e in pagedWorkLog"`.
Add same `v-pagination` with `v-model="workLogPage"` and `:length="workLogTotalPages"`.

### LQ Constraints
- Table mode already uses `v-data-table` with its own pagination — unchanged
- Fullscreen dialogs ("View All") show complete unsliced list — no change (acceptable)
- `slice` removal may briefly show all items in other non-list computed branches — verify no regression

## Phase LR — Calendar Panel Dismissibility

**Objective:** Make `CiCalendarLogPanel` dismissible via close button and click-outside.

**Research basis:** Section 2.197

**Files:**
- `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiCalendarLogPanel.vue`
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

### LR-A: Add `@close` Emit to `CiCalendarLogPanel.vue`
In `<script setup>`:
```ts
const emit = defineEmits<{ close: [] }>()
```
In template header row, add close button:
```html
<v-btn icon="mdi-close" size="x-small" variant="text" @click="emit('close')" />
```

### LR-B: Add `v-click-outside` on Panel Card
```html
<v-card v-click-outside="() => emit('close')" ...>
```
Note: `v-click-outside` is a Vuetify built-in directive — no additional package needed.

### LR-C: Handle `@close` in `detail-[id].vue`
```html
<CiCalendarLogPanel
  ...
  @close="selectedCalendarDate = null"
/>
```

### LR-D: Add Placeholder When No Date Selected
Wrap `CiCalendarLogPanel` in a conditional: when `selectedCalendarDate` is null, show a placeholder text block:
```html
<div v-if="!selectedCalendarDate" class="d-flex align-center justify-center h-100 text-grey text-caption pa-4" style="min-height:200px">
  <span><v-icon icon="mdi-calendar-cursor" class="mr-1" />Select a date to view records</span>
</div>
<CiCalendarLogPanel v-else ... @close="selectedCalendarDate = null" />
```

### LR-E: Add Transition
Wrap the conditional panel block in `<v-slide-x-reverse-transition>`:
```html
<v-slide-x-reverse-transition>
  <CiCalendarLogPanel v-if="selectedCalendarDate" ... />
</v-slide-x-reverse-transition>
```

### LR Constraints
- `v-click-outside` fires on any click outside the card — must NOT dismiss when user clicks inside the calendar grid (left side). Verify the directive does not interfere with calendar cell clicks.
- If `v-click-outside` causes issues, fallback: dismiss button only (LR-A + LR-C, skip LR-B).

## Phase LI — Milestone Audit Trail (Backend + Migration)

**Objective:** Add `createdBy`/`updatedBy` tracking and `fireLog()` calls to all milestone CUD operations.

**Research basis:** Section 2.199

**Files:**
- `pmo-backend/src/database/entities/construction-milestone.entity.ts`
- `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.controller.ts`
- `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`
- New migration file: `database/migrations/0XX_add_milestone_audit_fields.ts`

### LI-A: Extend Entity

Add to `ConstructionMilestone` entity:
```ts
@Property({ nullable: true })
createdBy?: string;

@Property({ nullable: true, onUpdate: () => new Date() })
updatedAt?: Date;

@Property({ nullable: true })
updatedBy?: string;
```

### LI-B: Create Migration

```sql
ALTER TABLE construction_milestones
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS created_by VARCHAR(36),
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS updated_by VARCHAR(36),
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ;
```

Migration file: next sequential number in `database/migrations/`.

### LI-C: Update Controller

Add `@CurrentUser() user: JwtPayload` parameter to:
- `createMilestone` (line ~218)
- `updateMilestone` (line ~225)
- `removeMilestone` (line ~236)

Pass `user.sub` to corresponding service methods.

### LI-D: Update Service

**`createMilestone`:** Set `milestone.createdBy = userId`. Call `this.fireLog('CONSTRUCTION_MILESTONE_CREATED', projectId, userId, { title: dto.title })`.

**`updateMilestone`:** Set `milestone.updatedBy = userId`, `milestone.updatedAt = new Date()`. Call `this.fireLog('CONSTRUCTION_MILESTONE_UPDATED', projectId, userId, { milestoneId, ...changes })`.

**`removeMilestone`:** Call `this.fireLog('CONSTRUCTION_MILESTONE_DELETED', projectId, userId, { milestoneId, title: milestone.title })` BEFORE flush.

### LI-E: Frontend Audit Trail Entity Type

`loadAuditTrail()` in `detail-[id].vue` currently queries `CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT`. Add `CONSTRUCTION_MILESTONE` events to the same query OR expand `loadAuditTrail` to also fetch milestone-specific events. Simplest approach: `fireLog` uses entity type `CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT` for milestone events (with metadata indicating the milestone), so the existing frontend query catches them without changes.

### LI Constraints
- Migration columns are nullable — no existing row backfill needed
- `fireLog` entity type must match what the frontend audit trail queries
- Reference implementation: `createTimelineEntry` (controller line 260, service line 1037)

---

## Phase LJ — Edit Page Timeline Search + Filter + Pagination

**Objective:** Add search, status filter, sort, and pagination to the Timeline tab in `edit-[id].vue`.

**Research basis:** Section 2.198

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

**Note:** This phase also implements **LL-F** (Vuetify date pickers in edit page filter bar) which was deferred from Phase LL.

### LJ-A: Add Filter/Search Refs
```ts
const milestoneEditSearch = ref('')
const milestoneEditStatus = ref('')
const milestoneEditSort = ref<'asc' | 'desc'>('asc')
const milestoneEditPage = ref(1)

const workLogEditSearch = ref('')
const workLogEditType = ref('')
const workLogEditSort = ref<'asc' | 'desc'>('desc')
const workLogEditPage = ref(1)
```

### LJ-B: Filtered + Paged Milestone Computed
```ts
const editFilteredMilestones = computed(() => {
  let ms = milestones.value
  if (milestoneEditSearch.value)
    ms = ms.filter(m => m.title.toLowerCase().includes(milestoneEditSearch.value.toLowerCase()))
  if (milestoneEditStatus.value)
    ms = ms.filter(m => m.status === milestoneEditStatus.value)
  ms = [...ms].sort((a, b) => {
    const d = new Date(a.targetDate ?? 0).getTime() - new Date(b.targetDate ?? 0).getTime()
    return milestoneEditSort.value === 'asc' ? d : -d
  })
  return ms
})
const pagedEditMilestones = computed(() => {
  const start = (milestoneEditPage.value - 1) * 5
  return editFilteredMilestones.value.slice(start, start + 5)
})
const milestoneEditTotalPages = computed(() =>
  Math.ceil(editFilteredMilestones.value.length / 5)
)
```

### LJ-C: Filtered + Paged Work Log Computed
Same pattern for `editFilteredWorkLogs` / `pagedEditWorkLogs` / `workLogEditTotalPages`.

### LJ-D: Filter Toolbar (Milestone Column Header)
Add a compact filter row above the milestone list in the Timeline tab:
```html
<v-text-field v-model="milestoneEditSearch" density="compact" placeholder="Search milestones..." clearable ... />
<v-select v-model="milestoneEditStatus" :items="statusOptions" density="compact" clearable placeholder="Status" ... />
<v-btn icon @click="milestoneEditSort = milestoneEditSort === 'asc' ? 'desc' : 'asc'" ... />
```

### LJ-E: Filter Toolbar (Work Log Column Header)
Same pattern for work logs — `workLogEditSearch`, `workLogEditType`, `workLogEditSort`.

### LJ-F: Pagination Controls
```html
<v-pagination v-if="milestoneEditTotalPages > 1" v-model="milestoneEditPage" :length="milestoneEditTotalPages" density="compact" />
```
Same for work log column.

### LJ-G: LL-F — Date Range Pickers (Edit Page Filter Bar)
If the edit page has a date range filter bar (or add one), replace `type="date"` inputs with `v-menu + v-date-picker` pattern (same as `detail-[id].vue` LL-E implementation). Bind to `milestoneEditDateFrom`/`milestoneEditDateTo` refs. Apply to `editFilteredMilestones` computed.

### LJ Constraints
- Do NOT add a Calendar view to the edit page (out of scope)
- Watchers on filter refs must reset page to 1 to avoid out-of-bounds pagination
- Search is client-side (all milestones already loaded from API)

## Phase LM-B — File Upload in Add Mode Drain Loop

**Objective:** After creating a MOV entry in the drain loop, upload the pending file if present.

**Research basis:** Sections 2.195, 2.200

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

**Endpoint confirmed:** `POST /construction-projects/:id/mov-entries/:movEntryId/upload-file`
- Accepts `multipart/form-data`, field name `file`
- Max 15 MB (configured on backend)
- Requires JWT auth

### LM-B-1: Extend PendingMovEntry (already done in LM)
`PendingMovEntry` already has `file?: File` from Phase LM.

### LM-B-2: Extend Drain Loop

In `saveMilestone()` and `saveDiary()` drain loops, after each `await api.post(...)` for MOV text fields:
```ts
for (const entry of pendingMovs) {
  const movRes = await api.post(`/construction-projects/${projectId}/mov-entries`, {
    ...textFields
  })
  const movId = movRes.data?.id ?? movRes.id
  if (entry.file && movId) {
    const fd = new FormData()
    fd.append('file', entry.file)
    await api.post(
      `/construction-projects/${projectId}/mov-entries/${movId}/upload-file`,
      fd,
      { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data' } }
    )
  }
}
```

### LM-B-3: Error Handling
File upload failure is non-blocking (same pattern as MOV text field failure). Show toast warning: "MOV entry created but file upload failed — attach manually."

### LM-B Constraints
- File upload is sequential per entry (not parallel) to avoid race conditions
- Maximum file size: 15 MB — enforced by backend Multer config; frontend should add `size <= 15MB` validation on file selection in `CiMovPendingSection.vue`
- Supported MIME types: images (`image/*`), PDFs, documents — same as existing `CiMovEvidenceSection` upload

```html
</v-card-text>
```
With:

```ts
const recentCalendarMilestones = computed(() =>
  [...(filteredMilestones.value as any[])]
    .sort((a, b) => (b.targetDate ?? '').localeCompare(a.targetDate ?? ''))
    .slice(0, 5)

const recentCalendarWorkLogs = computed(() =>

)
```

## Phase LU — Batch Dialogs MOV Link Field

**Objective:** Add optional `mov_link` URL field to each batch row; update alert text from anti-pattern to instructional note; drain MOV after batch save.

**Research basis:** Section 2.204

**Files:**
- `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiBatchMilestoneDialog.vue`
- `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiBatchDiaryDialog.vue`

### LU-A: Update CiBatchMilestoneDialog — BatchRow interface
```ts
interface BatchRow {
  title: string
  target_date: string
  status: string
  category: string
  mov_link: string   // new
}
```
Default: `mov_link: ''` in `addRow()` and initial row.

### LU-B: Add mov_link field to CiBatchMilestoneDialog row template
After the `category` col, add a new col:
```html
<v-col cols="12" sm="3">
  <v-text-field
    v-model="row.mov_link"
    label="MOV Link (optional)"
    density="compact"
    variant="outlined"
    hide-details
    prepend-inner-icon="mdi-link"
    placeholder="https://..."
  />
</v-col>
```
Adjust adjacent col sizing to fit (reduce `sm` on category or title as needed to stay in 12-col grid).

### LU-C: Update alert text in CiBatchMilestoneDialog
```html
<v-alert type="info" variant="tonal" density="compact" class="mb-3">
  Add up to 50 milestones at once. Title is required per row. For file uploads, categories, and advanced MOV configuration, edit each record individually after saving.
</v-alert>
```

### LU-D: Drain MOV after batch save in CiBatchMilestoneDialog
In `submit()`, after `await api.post(... /batch ...)`:
```ts
const created = await api.post<{ items: Array<{ id: string }> }>(...)
// LU-D: Post MOV for rows with mov_link
const withMov = valid.filter(r => r.mov_link.trim())
for (let i = 0; i < withMov.length; i++) {
  const id = created?.items?.[i]?.id
  if (!id) continue
  try {
    await api.post(`/api/construction-projects/${props.projectId}/mov-entries`, {
      related_entity_type: 'MILESTONE',
      related_entity_id: id,
      mov_title: withMov[i].title.trim(),
      mov_link: withMov[i].mov_link.trim(),
    })
  } catch { /* non-blocking */ }
}
```
> **Caution:** This requires the batch endpoint to return the created items in input order. Verify response shape first. If response shape is unknown, skip LU-D and show a note in the alert instead.

### LU-E: Mirror LU-A/B/C/D for CiBatchDiaryDialog
Apply same pattern: add `mov_link: string` to `BatchDiaryRow`, add field to row template, update alert text, add post-batch MOV drain using `related_entity_type: 'TIMELINE_ENTRY'`.

## Phase MC — New Entity Fields + Migration

**Scope:** Add all new columns to `construction_projects` table via MikroORM migration

### MC-A: Add new properties to `ConstructionProject` entity

```typescript
// Location
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'varchar(500)' })
spatialCoverage?: string;

@Property({ nullable: true, length: 100 })
municipality?: string;

@Property({ nullable: true, length: 100 })
province?: string;

// Implementation Agencies
@Property({ nullable: true, length: 255 })
coImplementingAgency?: string;

@Property({ nullable: true, length: 255 })
attachedAgency?: string;

// Revision Orders / Contract Time
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'date' })
originalStartDate?: Date;

@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'date' })
revisedStartDate?: Date;

@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'date' })
originalCompletionDate?: Date;

@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'date' })
revisedCompletionDate?: Date;

@Property({ nullable: true, length: 100 })
revisedProjectDuration?: string;

// Progress Monitoring
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'date' })
asOfDate?: Date;

@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'decimal(15,2)' })
costIncurredToDate?: string;

// Strategic Alignment
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'jsonb' })
rdpAlignment?: any;

@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'jsonb' })
socioeconomicAgenda?: any;

@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'jsonb' })
csuLikhaGoals?: any;

// Beneficiaries (dynamic list)
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'jsonb' })
beneficiaryList?: any;

// Funding Source
@Property({ nullable: true, length: 20 })
fundingSourceType?: string;

@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'jsonb' })
additionalFundingSources?: any;

// Remarks Log (chronological audit)
@Property({ columnType: 'jsonb', default: '[]' })
remarksLog: any[] = [];

// Personnel Groups
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'jsonb' })
personnelGroups?: any;
```

### MC-B: Create `Migration20260521010000_AddProjectNewFields.ts`
```typescript
export class Migration20260521010000_AddProjectNewFields extends Migration {
  async up(): Promise<void> {
    this.addSql(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_projects
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS spatial_coverage VARCHAR(500),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS municipality VARCHAR(100),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS province VARCHAR(100),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS co_implementing_agency VARCHAR(255),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS attached_agency VARCHAR(255),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS contractor VARCHAR(255),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS original_start_date DATE,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS revised_start_date DATE,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS original_completion_date DATE,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS revised_completion_date DATE,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS revised_project_duration VARCHAR(100),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS as_of_date DATE,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS cost_incurred_to_date DECIMAL(15,2),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rdp_alignment JSONB,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS socioeconomic_agenda JSONB,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS csu_likha_goals JSONB,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS beneficiary_list JSONB,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS funding_source_type VARCHAR(20),
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS additional_funding_sources JSONB,
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS remarks_log JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS personnel_groups JSONB
    `);
  }
}
```

## Phase MG — CiBasicInfoForm Major Refactor

**Scope:** Restructure shared Basic Info form component; update `coiFormState.ts` interface

### MG-A: Update `~/utils/coiFormState.ts` interface `BasicInfoFormState`

Add all new fields: `spatial_coverage`, `municipality`, `province`, `co_implementing_agency`, `attached_agency`, `contractor` (text), `cost_amount`, `original_start_date` (moved here from schedule temporarily — NO, keep in schedule tab), `rdp_alignment: string[]`, `socioeconomic_agenda: string[]`, `csu_likha_goals: string[]`, `beneficiary_list: string[]`, `funding_source_type: string`, `additional_funding_sources: {type:string,name:string,notes?:string}[]`

Remove from interface: `contractor_id` (deprecated), `contract_number` (removed), `summary` (removed), `key_features` (removed), `scope` (removed), `facilities` (removed)

### MG-B: Restructure `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` into sectioned layout

**Card 1 — Core Project Identity (always visible, required):**
- Project Code (text, required, no strict format)
- Project Title (required)
- Campus (select, required)
- Project Status (select: Proposal/Ongoing/Complete/On Hold/Cancelled, required)
- Project Description (textarea, replaces old description field)

**Expansion Panel 0 — Location (open by default):**
- Spatial Coverage (text input)
- Municipality (text input)
- Province (text input)

**Expansion Panel 1 — Implementation Agencies:**
- Implementing Agency (text)
- Co-Implementing Agency (text, optional)
- Attached Agency (text, optional)

**Expansion Panel 2 — Funding:**
- Funding Source Type toggle: Internal / External
- Primary Funding Source (v-select from `fundingSources` filtered by type, with chip display)
- Additional funding sources: inline chip list + "Add custom source" button (opens text input row)
- Cost Amount PHP (replaces Contract Amount label)

**Expansion Panel 3 — Project Objectives (dynamic bullets):**
- List of objective text fields with add/remove buttons
- Each entry: single-line text input + delete button

**Expansion Panel 4 — Beneficiaries (dynamic bullets):**
- List of beneficiary group entries with add/remove
- Each entry: single-line text input + delete button

**Expansion Panel 5 — Strategic Alignment:**
- RDP 2023-2028 multi-select grouped chip selector (see MG-C)
- 0+8 Socioeconomic Agenda multi-select chip selector
- CSU LIKHA Goals multi-select chip selector

**Expansion Panel 6 — Other Attributes:**
- Contractor (text input — replaces dropdown)
- Project Engineer (text)
- Building Type (text)
- Floor Area (number, sqm)

**Remove from form:** Contract Number, Project Summary, Key Features, Project Scope, Facilities

### MG-C: RDP and Socioeconomic Multi-Select Component Pattern

Implement as reusable grouped chip selector using `v-combobox` with grouped items or custom chip group layout. Options defined as static arrays in the component. Selected values stored as string arrays in form state.

RDP chapter options (abbreviated):
```
Ch.1: Macroeconomic Policy
Ch.2: Sustainable Development Foundations
Ch.3: Agriculture, Forestry, Fisheries
Ch.4: Industry and Services
Ch.5: Human Capital Development
Ch.6: Safe and Secure Communities
Ch.7: Good Governance
Ch.8: Peace and Security
Ch.9: Infrastructure Development
Ch.10: Tourism Development
Ch.11: Sound Fiscal Management
```

0+8 Agenda options:
```
0: COVID Recovery
1: Poverty Reduction / High-Growth Economy
2: Political and Economic Transformation
3: Agriculture and Rural Development
4: Energy Security
5: Infrastructure Development
6: Science, Technology, Innovation
7: Healthcare and Education Access
8: Peace and Security
```

CSU LIKHA options: `[OPERATOR TO VERIFY — placeholder options until confirmed]`

## Phase ML — Authorization Refactor (Role-Based Access)

**Scope:** Implement entity-level authorization for contractor, director, and ECO roles

### ML-A: Add role constants to `usePermissions` composable
- `isContractor`: checks for CONTRACTOR role in JWT
- `isDirector`: checks for DIRECTOR role
- `canEditProject(project)`: checks `createdBy === currentUser.id` OR has Admin/SuperAdmin role

### ML-B: Service-layer authorization guards
- `updateProject`: if user is CONTRACTOR, verify `project.createdBy === user.sub` or `project.assignedTo` includes `user.sub`
- `createMilestone`, `createTimelineEntry`: same contractor-level check
- Add `ForbiddenException` if guard fails

### ML-C: Frontend role-based rendering
- Detail page: hide edit buttons for Directors unless explicitly assigned
- Edit page: guard save buttons with `canEditCurrentProject`
- Personnel fields: Contractor users see only their own log entries in timeline (filter by `createdBy`)

### ML-D: Seed CONTRACTOR and DIRECTOR roles if not present

**Verification:** Contractor user cannot edit projects not assigned to them (403). Director user can view but not edit.

## Phase 3 Implementation Status — MA through ME (2026-05-21)

**MA ✅** — Branch `pmo-coi` created and pushed to origin. `refactor/page-structure-feb9` deleted locally and remotely. `CLAUDE.md` updated.

**MB ✅** — `ProjectStatus` enum extended with `PROPOSAL` and `COMPLETE` (PLANNING/COMPLETED kept as legacy until MF migration normalizes data). DTO `@Matches(/^CP-\d{4}-\d{3}$/)` removed. `contractor` text field + `cost_amount` alias added to `CreateConstructionProjectDto`. Entity `contractor VARCHAR(255)` property added.

**MC ✅** — 22 new entity fields added to `ConstructionProject`: spatial_coverage, municipality, province, co_implementing_agency, attached_agency, original_start_date, revised_start_date, original_completion_date, revised_completion_date, revised_project_duration, as_of_date, cost_incurred_to_date, rdp_alignment, socioeconomic_agenda, csu_likha_goals, beneficiary_list, funding_source_type, additional_funding_sources, remarks_log, personnel_groups. DTO updated with all new field validators. `Migration20260521010000_AddProjectNewFields.ts` created. Service `create()` INSERT expanded; `update()` handles all new JSON fields and `cost_amount`→`contract_amount` remap.

**MD ✅** — `FundingSource.type` property added. `Migration20260521020000_AddFundingSourceType.ts` creates column and seeds 5 predefined sources (3 INTERNAL + 2 EXTERNAL). `CreateFundingSourceDto.type` added; service `create()` maps it.

**ME ✅** — POW fully removed:
- Backend: `ConstructionPowItem` entity, `create-pow-item.dto.ts`, `update-pow-item.dto.ts` deleted. `ConstructionPowItem` removed from `entities/index.ts`, `dto/index.ts`, `construction-projects.module.ts`, and service repo injection. 4 POW methods removed from service. 4 POW routes removed from controller. POW public route removed from `public-construction.controller.ts`. `Migration20260521030000_ArchivePowItemsTable.ts` renames table to `_archived_construction_pow_items_20260521`.
- Frontend: `CiPowItemTable.vue` and `useCoiPowItems.ts` deleted. `detail-[id].vue`, `edit-[id].vue`, `public/detail-[id].vue` POW tabs/sections removed. `useCoiAccess.ts` EDITABLE_TABS array updated. `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` POW charts/KPI removed (POW Status, POW Category, Total POW Cost — chart slots replaced; empty-state copy updated).

**MF (status normalization) — split into 2 migrations after operator-reported error 2026-05-21:**

Initial single-migration approach failed: `invalid input value for enum project_status_enum: "PROPOSAL"`. Root cause — the `status` column is a real PostgreSQL ENUM (`project_status_enum`), not VARCHAR as initially analyzed (research §2.209-D corrected). PostgreSQL forbids using a newly-added ENUM value in the same transaction in which it was added.

**Corrected migration plan:**
1. `Migration20260521040000_NormalizeProjectStatus.ts` — REWRITTEN: now overrides `isTransactional()` → `false`, runs only `ALTER TYPE project_status_enum ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'PROPOSAL'` and same for `'COMPLETE'`. Commits new enum values before next migration runs.
2. `Migration20260521050000_NormalizeProjectStatusData.ts` — default transactional, UPDATEs `construction_projects` and `projects` ONLY — normalizes `PLANNING → PROPOSAL` and `COMPLETED → COMPLETE`.

   **Correction (2026-05-21):** First version of 050000 also UPDATEd `repair_projects` based on the MikroORM snapshot, but the live DB has `repair_projects.status` typed as `repair_status_enum` (different enum, no PLANNING value). The literal `'PLANNING'` in the WHERE clause failed to cast → migration failed → transaction rolled back. Fix: removed repair_projects from the UPDATE — only construction_projects and projects use `project_status_enum`.

**Caution:** PostgreSQL does not support removing ENUM values. `down()` for 040000 is a no-op. Legacy values `PLANNING`/`COMPLETED` persist permanently in the enum type (harmless — TS enum can omit them after normalization).

**Verification:**
- ✅ Backend `tsc --noEmit` passes clean (zero errors)
- ⏳ Operator action required: `cd pmo-backend && npm run migration:up` — migrations 010000, 020000, 030000 already applied per operator log. The next run picks up at 040000 (rewritten) and 050000 (new):
  1. `Migration20260521010000_AddProjectNewFields` ✅ already applied
  2. `Migration20260521020000_AddFundingSourceType` ✅ already applied
  3. `Migration20260521030000_ArchivePowItemsTable` ✅ already applied
  4. `Migration20260521040000_NormalizeProjectStatus` — REWRITTEN (ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE only, non-transactional)
  5. `Migration20260521050000_NormalizeProjectStatusData` — NEW (data UPDATE for 3 tables)
- ⏳ After operator confirms 040000 + 050000 applied, MF will be marked ✅ and legacy enum values removed from TS `ProjectStatus` enum (kept as legacy until normalization complete).

UI polish per ECO feedback:
- "Basic Info" tab renamed → **"Project Profile"** in both new.vue and edit-[id].vue
- Every card in `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` now has a `v-card-subtitle` with a 1-line description of what data the section captures
- Card variant changed from `variant="outlined"` → `elevation="2" rounded="lg"` for slight 3D depth without heavy shadow

**MH-B ✅ — Revision Orders MOV upload.**

Both Schedule tabs Revision Orders card now include a "Means of Verification (MOV)" sub-section:
- MOV Link text field (URL — Drive/SharePoint/etc.)
- File upload (`v-file-input`, ≤ 20MB, PDF/DOCX/Image)
- `new.vue`: stages MOV into `pendingDocs` / `pendingLinks` with `documentType: 'revision_order_mov'` — uploaded after project creation
- `edit-[id].vue`: immediate POST via `uploadRevisionMov()` — file → `POST /:id/documents` (multipart), link → `POST /:id/documents` (externalLink); refreshes doc list after upload
- Section subtitle bolds "Documentary MOV is required" — directive enforcement messaging
- All other Schedule cards (Primary Dates, Progress Targets) now have descriptions too

**MI ✅ — Progress and Financial tab + chronological Remarks Log.**

New `progress` tab inserted in tabOrder (between schedule/personnel in new.vue; between timeline/financial in edit-[id].vue — existing `financial` tab kept for fiscal-year financial records, renamed display label to "Financial Records"). Tab contents:

- **Section A — Physical Status:** Percentage Completion (%), As of Date (Vuetify date picker)
- **Section B — Financial Status:** Cost Incurred to Date (PHP), Date Completed (Vuetify date picker)
- **Section C — Remarks Log:** Inline input + Add button; chronological list rendered newest-first; per-entry timestamp + author (when present) + delete button; capped at 320px height with internal scroll

Form state additions: `as_of_date`, `cost_incurred_to_date`, `date_completed`, `remarks_log_entries: Array<{text, author?, createdAt}>`. Hydration in edit-[id].vue parses backend `remarks_log` JSONB tolerating both `created_at` and `createdAt` keys. Payload maps `remarks_log_entries` → `remarks_log` with snake_case `created_at` for backend consistency.

Backend already supports these fields via MC migration — no new migration needed.

**Files touched (this batch):**
- `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiBasicInfoForm.vue` — 7 card subtitles added, elevation+rounded swapped in for outlined
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue` — "Project Profile" tab label, Schedule card subtitles, Revision Orders MOV upload UI + script (stages into pendingDocs/pendingLinks), Progress tab added with full MI content
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` — same renames/subtitles, immediate MOV upload via `uploadRevisionMov()`, Progress tab added with full MI content; existing `financial` tab kept for fiscal-year records (label "Financial Records")
- `pmo-frontend/utils/coiFormState.ts` — no change (interface already had `remarks_log` and date fields from MC)

**Verification:**
- ✅ Backend `tsc --noEmit` — clean
- ✅ Frontend `vue-tsc --noEmit` MG-C + MH-B + MI scope — zero new errors (pre-existing `imageTakenDate` gallery adapter errors persist — out of scope)

**Next phase:** MJ (Personnel tab groups — CSU / Contractor / Others) — awaiting operator authorization.

## Phase NB — Reusable Components: Hierarchical Selector + Bullet List

### NB-A: Create `pmo-frontend/utils/coiHierarchies.ts`
Export three `HierarchyOption[]` const arrays: `LIKHA_OPTIONS`, `RDP_OPTIONS`, `SOCIOECONOMIC_OPTIONS`. Full hierarchy per Section 2.210-C. Single source of truth.

### NB-B: Create `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiHierarchicalSelectorModal.vue`
- Props per Section 2.210-B contract
- Dialog opens via `v-model:open` boolean prop
- Renders nested `v-list-group` with collapsible parents
- Search input filters tree (matches preserved with parent context)
- Per-leaf `v-checkbox`
- Parent shows indeterminate state when partial children selected; clicking parent selects/deselects all visible (filtered) children
- "Clear All" + "Select All Visible" + "Apply" footer buttons
- Emits `update:modelValue` only on Apply (modal acts as transactional editor)

### NB-C: Create `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiBulletListInput.vue`
- Props per Section 2.210-B contract
- If `modelValue.length === 0`, internally normalizes to `['']` (one blank entry visible)
- Each row: bullet icon + `v-text-field` + delete `v-btn`
- "Add" button at bottom
- `update:modelValue` filters out empty strings before emit (so persistence layer never gets `['']`)
- `readOnly` prop: render `<ul><li>` static list instead

### NB-D: Selector trigger component for Project Profile
- Renders chip group of currently-selected items + "Edit Selections" button
- Button opens `CiHierarchicalSelectorModal`
- Shared layout for RDP, 0+8, LIKHA

**Verification:** Unit-mount each component standalone; verify checkbox behavior, search filtering, empty-state rendering.

Update new.vue + edit-[id].vue payload + hydration to send/receive arrays. Backend DTO already accepts `output_indicators?: string[]` and `outcome_indicators?: string[]` (from MC) — no backend change required.

Add `readOnly?: boolean` prop to `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`. When true: all inputs disabled, bullet lists render UL, selector triggers show chips only (no "Edit" button), `v-text-field` becomes plain text display.

## Phase ND — Backend: ConstructionRevisionOrder

### ND-A: Entity
Create `pmo-backend/src/database/entities/construction-revision-order.entity.ts` per Section 2.210-D.

### ND-B: Migration
`Migration20260522010000_CreateConstructionRevisionOrders.ts`:
```sql
CREATE TABLE construction_revision_orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES construction_projects(id),
  revision_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
  revision_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  revision_date DATE NOT NULL,
  new_start_date DATE,
  new_completion_date DATE,
  new_duration VARCHAR(100),
  cost_adjustment DECIMAL(15,2),
  justification TEXT,
  approval_status VARCHAR(50),
  mov_document_id UUID,
  mov_link TEXT,
  created_by UUID,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_by UUID
);
CREATE INDEX idx_cro_project_date ON construction_revision_orders(project_id, revision_date DESC);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_cro_project_revnum ON construction_revision_orders(project_id, revision_number);
```

### ND-C: DTOs
- `create-revision-order.dto.ts` (snake_case fields, class-validator)
- `update-revision-order.dto.ts` (PartialType)
- `query-revision-order.dto.ts` (pagination)

### ND-D: Service methods
- `findRevisionOrders(projectId, query)` — paginated, default sort `revision_date DESC, revision_number DESC`
- `createRevisionOrder(projectId, dto, user)` — auto-increments `revisionNumber` based on max+1 for project; mirrors latest APPROVED dates into project record
- `updateRevisionOrder(projectId, roId, dto, user)` — re-mirrors if dates changed and this is the latest approved
- `removeRevisionOrder(projectId, roId, user)` — re-mirrors from new latest approved or clears project's `revised_*` fields if none remain
- `fireLog()` audit calls on each CUD

### ND-E: Controller routes (RBAC: Admin/Staff write, all read)
- `GET    :id/revision-orders` — paginated list
- `POST   :id/revision-orders` — create (auto-incremented number)
- `PATCH  :id/revision-orders/:roId` — update
- `DELETE :id/revision-orders/:roId` — remove

### ND-F: Module registration of new entity.

**Verification:** Postman gate — create 3 revision orders, verify auto-increment + mirror behavior on project.

---

## Phase NE — Backend: ConstructionProgressReport

### NE-A: Entity per Section 2.210-E.

### NE-B: Migration `Migration20260522020000_CreateConstructionProgressReports.ts`:
```sql
CREATE TABLE construction_progress_reports (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES construction_projects(id),
  report_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  report_date DATE NOT NULL,
  report_number VARCHAR(20),
  percentage_completion DECIMAL(5,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  planned_accomplishment DECIMAL(5,2),
  slippage DECIMAL(5,2),
  cost_incurred_to_date DECIMAL(15,2),
  cost_incurred_this_period DECIMAL(15,2),
  calendar_days_elapsed INTEGER,
  percent_time_elapsed DECIMAL(5,2),
  remarks TEXT,
  issues_encountered TEXT,
  mitigation_actions TEXT,
  mov_document_id UUID,
  mov_link TEXT,
  created_by UUID,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_by UUID
);
CREATE INDEX idx_cpr_project_date ON construction_progress_reports(project_id, report_date DESC);
```

### NE-C: DTOs (create/update/query).

### NE-D: Service methods (parallel to ND-D):
- `findProgressReports(projectId, query)` — paginated, default sort `report_date DESC`
- `createProgressReport(projectId, dto, user)` — mirrors latest values into project record
- `updateProgressReport(projectId, reportId, dto, user)` — re-mirrors if this is latest
- `removeProgressReport(projectId, reportId, user)` — re-mirrors from new latest or clears

### NE-E: Controller routes (parallel to ND-E):
- `GET    :id/progress-reports`
- `POST   :id/progress-reports`
- `PATCH  :id/progress-reports/:reportId`
- `DELETE :id/progress-reports/:reportId`

### NE-F: Module registration.

**Verification:** Postman — create chronological reports, verify project mirror updates correctly when latest changes.

## Phase NB Implementation Status (2026-05-21)

**NB-A ✅** — `pmo-frontend/utils/coiHierarchies.ts` created. Exports `LIKHA_OPTIONS` (5 flat), `RDP_OPTIONS` (5 parts × 18 chapters incl. 9 sub-chapters at 4.1/4.2/4.3/6.1/6.2/12.1/12.2/14.1/14.2), `SOCIOECONOMIC_OPTIONS` (9 parents × 18 sub-items per operator-confirmed structure). Plus `HierarchyOption` interface, `labelForLikha/Rdp/Sea()` lookup helpers, `leafKeysOf()` utility.

**NB-B ✅** — `components/coi/CiHierarchicalSelectorModal.vue` created. Transactional editor (draft set committed only on Apply), nested checkboxes with parent indeterminate state, search filter preserving parent context, "Clear All" / "Select All Visible" / "Apply" / "Cancel" footer. Supports 1, 2, and 3-level depth.

**NB-C ✅** — `components/coi/CiBulletListInput.vue` created. ECO directive enforced: empty modelValue → always renders ONE blank entry visibly (never collapsed). Empty strings filtered on emit so DB never receives `['']`. `readOnly` prop renders static `<ul><li>`. Enter key adds new row. Always keeps at least one row visible after removal.

**NB-D ✅** — `components/coi/CiHierarchicalSelectorTrigger.vue` created. Chip group of selected labels + "Edit Selections" button that opens the NB-B modal. Per-chip closable for quick removal. Resolves key→label via recursive walk of the options tree. Reused across RDP/0+8/LIKHA — only `title`/`options`/`color` change per usage.

**Verification:**
- ✅ `vue-tsc --noEmit` scope of NB files — clean (zero errors)
- Components are self-contained; no consumer pages updated yet (NC's responsibility)

**MJ continuation note:** NB does NOT itself satisfy or resolve MJ scope. MJ remains `⬜ PAUSED` (Personnel tab three-group structure). NB is foundational tooling for NC; MJ resumes only after NK closure per the original NA–NK sequence. If operator wants to interleave MJ now, that is a separate explicit authorization (MJ work would interleave the Personnel tab refactor between NC and ND, but introduces continuity risk if NC's `readOnly` prop work overlaps with personnel groups UI).

**Next phase:** NC — apply NB components inside `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` (replace 3 v-autocomplete blocks with `CiHierarchicalSelectorTrigger`; replace inline bullet lists with `CiBulletListInput`; add `readOnly` prop to the form for detail-page reuse).

## Phase OB — Permanent Nuxt Auto-Import Fix + Render Verification

**Root cause:** Default Nuxt 3 auto-import path-prefixes nested-dir components (`components/coi/Foo.vue` → `<CoiFoo>`). Bare-tag usage of newly-added components silently fails. Already burned us twice in this session.

### OB-A: Configure global `pathPrefix: false`

Edit `pmo-frontend/nuxt.config.ts` — add the components config:

```ts
components: {
  dirs: [
    { path: '~/components', pathPrefix: false },
  ],
},
```

This removes nested-directory path prefixing globally. All components become resolvable by their filename alone. Safe because all `components/coi/*` files already use the `Ci` prefix (no basename collisions).

### OB-B: Remove now-redundant explicit imports (cleanup, not blocking)

After OB-A is verified working, the explicit imports added in NC fix (`CiHierarchicalSelectorTrigger`, `CiBulletListInput`, `CiHierarchicalSelectorModal`) become redundant but harmless. Leave them in place for self-documentation — explicit imports never hurt.

### OB-C: Verify Revision Orders + Progress Report tabs render

After Nuxt config change + dev-server restart:
- Edit page → Schedule tab → confirm `<CiRevisionOrdersTable>` renders the paginated table + "Add Revision Order" button
- Edit page → Progress Report tab → confirm `<CiProgressReportTab>` renders the view-mode toggle + report list

### OB-D: TypeScript verification — `vue-tsc --noEmit` must remain clean

---

## Phase OC — Strategic Alignment UI Hierarchy Refactor

### OC-A: Split single 3-column row into 3 dedicated cards

In `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`, replace the existing 3-column Strategic Alignment cluster with a `v-row` containing 3 `v-col` cards (md=12 on phone/tablet, md=6 on laptop, md=4 on widescreen for side-by-side; auto-stack on small):

```
RDP 2023–2028 card    | 0+8 Agenda card    | LIKHA Goals card
(elevation=1, rounded, padding, subtitle, chip area)
```

Each card includes:
- Title row: icon + label + selection-count chip + Edit Selections button
- Subtitle: 1-line description
- Chip preview area (see OC-B)

### OC-B: Add "show more / fewer" chip pattern

Update `CiHierarchicalSelectorTrigger.vue`:
- Add prop `maxVisibleChips?: number` (default 5)
- Local state `showAll = ref(false)`
- When `selected.length > maxVisibleChips && !showAll`: render only first N chips + a `+M more` ghost chip that toggles `showAll`
- When `showAll` true: render all chips + "Show fewer" link
- Chip container: `d-flex flex-wrap ga-1` with max-height + internal scroll fallback for extreme cases (>30 chips)

### OC-C: Add spacing gap between subtitle, narrative, selection containers

In `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` Strategic Alignment block:
- Move "Strategic Alignment Narrative (optional)" textarea into its OWN card below the 3 selector cards
- Vertical gap `mt-3` between the selectors row and the narrative card

### OC-D: Responsive verification
- Widescreen (xl): 3-column side-by-side
- Laptop (lg): 3-column side-by-side
- Tablet (md): 3-column tighter or 2+1 wrap (acceptable)
- Mobile (sm/xs): stacked single column

---

### OD-A: Extract from Strategic Alignment card

In `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`, move the Output Indicators + Outcome Indicators blocks OUT of the Strategic Alignment card. They become a NEW row with 2 dedicated cards:

```
Output Indicators card    | Outcome Indicators card
(elevation=2, rounded-lg, padding, subtitle, CiBulletListInput)
```

### OD-B: Card structure (each card)

- Title row: icon (mdi-arrow-right-thick for output, mdi-target-account for outcome) + label + count chip
- Subtitle: existing description text
- Card body: `<CiBulletListInput>` instance, capped max-height with internal scroll

### OD-C: Update CiBasicInfoForm layout

The Project Profile tab top-to-bottom order after OC + OD:
```
Row A  — Project Identity (always visible, required)
Row B  — Location | Implementation Agencies
Row C  — Funding & Cost | Other Attributes
Row D  — Project Objectives | Beneficiary Groups
Row E  — Strategic Alignment cluster (3 cards: RDP / 0+8 / LIKHA)
Row F  — Strategic Alignment Narrative (full width)        (NEW from OC-C)
Row G  — Output Indicators | Outcome Indicators            (NEW from OD-A)
```

## Phase OE — Remarks Log — Hybrid Light Audit-Log Refactor

**Architecture decision:** Keep `construction_projects.remarks_log` JSONB (no migration), enhance UX + server-side stamping. See research §2.211-D.

### OE-A: Create reusable `CiRemarksLog.vue` component

`pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiRemarksLog.vue`:
- Props: `modelValue: Array<{ id?, text, author?, createdAt, updatedAt? }>`, `readOnly?: boolean`, `currentUserName?: string`
- Emits: `update:modelValue`
- UI:
  - Top: "Add New Remark" sub-card with `v-textarea` (multi-line) + Add button (clear styling, prominent)
  - Below: chronological card list (newest first) — each entry is its own bordered card with:
    - Author chip + timestamp + per-entry inline edit + delete
    - Inline edit toggles textarea + Save/Cancel
    - Local-only `id` generated if missing (UUID v4) for stable key during edit cycles
  - Pagination: 10 entries per page, `v-pagination` if more
  - Empty state: "No remarks recorded yet"

### OE-B: Integrate into edit-[id].vue Progress tab

Replace the existing inline Remarks Log card body with:
```
<CiRemarksLog v-model="form.remarks_log_entries" :current-user-name="..." />
```

Keep the existing wrapping card title/subtitle for consistency.

### OE-C: Server-side author + timestamp stamping

In `construction-projects.service.ts` `update()` method, when `dto.remarks_log` is present:
- For any entry missing `created_at`: set to NOW
- For any entry missing `author`: resolve from JWT (user.first_name + ' ' + user.last_name if available, else email, else sub UUID)
- Updated entries (with existing `id`): preserve original `created_at`, set `updated_at` + `updated_by`

Approach: pre-process `dto.remarks_log` before it reaches the existing dynamic-fields SQL update path.

### OE-D: Frontend form-state shape

Update `BasicInfoFormState`'s remarks_log_entries to allow id, updatedAt, updatedBy:
```ts
remarks_log_entries: Array<{
  id?: string
  text: string
  author?: string
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt?: string
  updatedBy?: string
}>
```

Hydration: pass-through from server (preserve all fields).

### OE-E: Detail page reuse (read-only)

Defer to NJ continuation: detail-[id].vue will mount `<CiRemarksLog :model-value :read-only="true" />`. Out of OE scope; documented for future.

## Phase OG — Strategic Alignment Chip Text Overflow Fix

*Research backing: §2.212-E*

**Scope:** CSS-only fix. No logic changes.

### OG-A: Add word-break to individual chips in CiHierarchicalSelectorTrigger.vue

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiHierarchicalSelectorTrigger.vue`

Locate the `v-chip` elements in the chip preview area. Add inline style:

```html
<v-chip
  ...existing props...
  style="white-space: normal; word-break: break-word; max-width: 260px;"
>
```

This prevents long RDP 2023–2028 attribute names from overflowing the chip boundary. The `d-flex flex-wrap ga-1` container already handles row-wrapping.

### OG-B: Verify no visual regression in short chip names

Quick visual spot-check: short names (e.g., "SDG 1") must still display inline, not stretched to 260px.

---

## Phase OH — Revision Orders Dual View (Table + List)

*Research backing: §2.212-C*

**Scope:** Add List view mode to `CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue`. No backend changes.

### OH-A: Add view-mode toggle to CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue

Extend `viewMode` ref from `'table'` (current only) to `'table' | 'list'`.

Add `v-btn-toggle` (icon buttons: `mdi-table` / `mdi-format-list-bulleted`) at the top of the component, aligned right alongside the "Add Revision Order" button.

### OH-B: Implement List view

List view requirements:
- Show 5 records per page (local `listPage` ref + computed `pagedListItems`)
- Each record as a `v-card` with `elevation=1`, `rounded`, padding
- Card layout:
  - Header row: revision number badge + type chip (color-coded: VOR=blue, CTE=orange, WSO=green, WRO=purple, OTHER=grey) + approval status chip + date
  - Body: justification text (truncated at 3 lines with expand on click)
  - Footer row: new dates (if any) | cost adjustment (if any) | MOV link icon
- Chronological order (latest first)
- `v-pagination` at bottom (5 per page)

### OH-C: Preserve table view unchanged

The existing `v-data-table` with 8-per-page client pagination remains the default. View toggle defaults to `'table'`.

### OH-D: readOnly mode propagation

When `readOnly=true`, both views must hide Add/Edit/Delete actions. Table view already handles this; list view must check `!readOnly`.

---

## Phase OI — Progress Report Tab Refactor

*Research backing: §2.212-B*

**Scope:** `CiProgressReportTab.vue` — add Project Date header, fix pagination, add List view.

### OI-A: Add Project Date synchronized header props

Add optional props to `CiProgressReportTab`:

```ts
projectDuration?: string
projectStartDate?: string
projectTargetCompletionDate?: string
```

Render at top of component as a read-only info banner (3 chips or a summary row):
```
Duration: [value or —]  |  Start: [value or —]  |  Target Completion: [value or —]
```
Use `v-chip` or simple `<span>` with label styling. These are display-only; no mutation.

### OI-B: Fix card view pagination

Card view currently iterates all `items` with no limit.

Add:
```ts
const cardPage = ref(1)
const cardItemsPerPage = 5
const pagedCardItems = computed(() =>
  items.value.slice((cardPage.value - 1) * cardItemsPerPage, cardPage.value * cardItemsPerPage)
)
```

Replace `v-for="r in items"` with `v-for="r in pagedCardItems"`.
Add `<v-pagination v-model="cardPage" :length="Math.ceil(items.value.length / cardItemsPerPage)" />` below card grid.

### OI-C: Fix table view pagination

Remove `hide-default-footer` and `items-per-page=50`. Replace with proper `v-data-table` pagination:
- `items-per-page="10"` (default)
- Vuetify data-table built-in footer pagination

### OI-D: Add List view mode

Add `'list'` to `viewMode` type. Add list icon button to the view toggle.

List view:
- 5 per page (client-side)
- Cards similar to OH-B style but for progress reports
- Card layout: report type chip + report number + date header; progress %, planned %, slippage row; cost incurred; remarks (truncated); issues/mitigation collapsible; edit/delete actions (if not readOnly)
- `v-pagination` at bottom

### OI-E: View toggle order

`v-btn-toggle` order: List (default) | Table | Comparison

Update `viewMode` default to `'list'` (list view as entry point is more readable for periodic reports).

*Research backing: §2.212-D, §2.212-G*

```html
<v-tab value="progress">
  <v-icon start>mdi-chart-line</v-icon>Progress Reports
</v-tab>
```

New tab order: `overview`, `progress`, `timeline`, `analytics`, `gallery`, `documents`, `team`, `others`

### OJ-B: Add Progress window-item

In the `<v-window>` block, add:

```html
<v-window-item value="progress">
  <CiProgressReportTab
    :project-id="projectId"
    :read-only="!canEditCurrentProject"
    :project-duration="project?.projectDuration || project?.duration"
    :project-start-date="project?.startDate"
    :project-target-completion-date="project?.targetCompletionDate"
  />
</v-window-item>
```

Guard: only render when `projectId` is truthy (already guaranteed by page lifecycle).

### OJ-C: Revision Orders in detail-[id].vue

The detail page has no `schedule` tab (schedule info is inside `overview`). Place `CiRevisionOrdersTable` inside the `progress` window-item, below `CiProgressReportTab`, wrapped in a section header:

```html
<v-divider class="my-4" />
<div class="text-subtitle-1 font-weight-medium mb-3">Revision Orders</div>
<CiRevisionOrdersTable
  :project-id="projectId"
  :read-only="!canEditCurrentProject"
/>
```

### OJ-D: Verify projectId availability

`projectId` in detail-[id].vue is derived from `route.params.id` (confirmed in existing code). Pass directly.

## Phase OK — new.vue: Progress Tab Alignment

*Research backing: §2.212-D, §2.212-F*

**Scope:** Align new.vue progress tab with system-wide standards. No backend or contract changes.

### OK-A: Replace inline remarkText/addRemark with CiRemarksLog

In `new.vue`, locate the inline remarks log block (lines ~946–1100 progress tab section). Replace the manual `newRemarkText` / `addRemark` / `v-for over remarks_log` pattern with:

```html
<CiRemarksLog
  v-model="form.remarks_log"
  :read-only="false"
/>
```

Ensure `form.remarks_log` is initialized as `[]` in the form state. The `CiRemarksLog` component manages the array directly via `v-model`.

### OK-B: Add post-creation instructional banner

In the new.vue progress tab, above the Physical/Financial status cards, add an info alert:

```html
<v-alert type="info" variant="tonal" class="mb-4" density="compact">
  <strong>Initial snapshot only.</strong> Full progress report history (monthly / quarterly entries, 
  slippage tracking, cost incurred per period) is available after the project is saved.
</v-alert>
```

### OK-C: No CiProgressReportTab in new.vue

`CiProgressReportTab` requires a persisted `projectId` for API calls. new.vue cannot provide this before save. The flat-field Physical/Financial cards remain. This is the intentional architectural boundary.

Replace with:
```vue
<v-col cols="12" md="7" class="pa-3">
  <div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 10px 16px;">
    <div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey-darken-1">New Start</div>
      <div class="text-body-2">{{ r.newStartDate || '—' }}</div>
    </div>
    <div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey-darken-1">New Completion</div>
      <div class="text-body-2">{{ r.newCompletionDate || '—' }}</div>
    </div>
    <div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey-darken-1">Duration</div>
      <div class="text-body-2">{{ r.newDuration || '—' }}</div>
    </div>
    <div>
      <div class="text-caption text-grey-darken-1">Cost Adjustment</div>
      <div class="text-body-2">{{ r.costAdjustment != null ? `₱${Number(r.costAdjustment).toLocaleString('en-PH')}` : '—' }}</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</v-col>
```

**Right column** (change `flex-md-column` → `flex-row flex-wrap`):
```vue
<v-col cols="12" md="2" class="d-flex flex-row flex-wrap align-center justify-center ga-1 pa-2 bg-grey-lighten-5">
```

**Left and center cols**: add `style="border-right: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08)"` for crisp section separation.

### OR-B: CiProgressReportTab.vue — same refactor

Apply identical CSS grid pattern to center stat block (% Completion / Planned% / Slippage / Cost to Date). Apply same `flex-row flex-wrap` to right action column. Add hairline border-right to left and center cols.

### OR-C: Verification
- On desktop (md+): action buttons appear in a horizontal row, not a vertical stack
- Stat values left-aligned with even gap spacing; no U-shape perception
- Hairline dividers visually separate the 3 zones
- Cards still expand/collapse correctly
