## Phase XA — SC-A Watcher: Handle Empty visibleTabOrder

**Research reference:** §2.241 Root Cause A
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Risk:** Minimal. Change is a 2-line patch to the SC-A watcher. Authorized users are unaffected (they always have tabs).

### XA-1: Fix SC-A watcher — remove empty-guard, add empty-string fallback

**Location:** `edit-[id].vue` around lines 295–299 (SC-A watcher)

Current:
```typescript
watch(visibleTabOrder, (tabs) => {
  if (tabs.length > 0 && !tabs.includes(activeTab.value)) {
    activeTab.value = tabs[0]
  }
}, { immediate: false })
```

Replace with:
```typescript
watch(visibleTabOrder, (tabs) => {
  if (!tabs.includes(activeTab.value)) {
    activeTab.value = tabs[0] ?? ''
  }
}, { immediate: false })
```

**Why:** When `tabs = []`, `tabs.length > 0` blocked the redirect, leaving `activeTab = 'basic'`. The `v-window` then rendered the 'basic' window-item (full form content). With `tabs[0] ?? ''`, `activeTab` becomes `''` when no tabs exist — no `<v-window-item>` has `value=""`, so nothing renders.

---

## Phase XB — v-window and v-window-item: Add v-if Guards

**Research reference:** §2.241 Root Cause B
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Risk:** Minimal. Guards are additive and match existing `<v-tab>` guards exactly. Authorized users see no change.

### XB-1: Gate `<v-window>` on visibleTabOrder

**Location:** The `<v-window v-model="activeTab">` element (currently around line 1982).

Add `v-if="visibleTabOrder.length > 0"`:
```html
<v-window v-if="visibleTabOrder.length > 0" v-model="activeTab">
```

**Why:** When no tabs are visible, the entire window component is unmounted. Even if `activeTab` contains a stale value, no content can render inside an unmounted v-window.

### XB-2: Add v-if to each v-window-item matching the v-tab guards

**Location:** Inside `<v-window>`, each of the 7 `<v-window-item>` elements.

Add `v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('X')"` to each item to mirror its paired `<v-tab>`:

```html
<v-window-item v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('basic')" value="basic">
<v-window-item v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('schedule')" value="schedule">
<v-window-item v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('timeline')" value="timeline">
<v-window-item v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('progress')" value="progress">
<v-window-item v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('personnel')" value="personnel">
<v-window-item v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('documents')" value="documents">
<v-window-item v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('others')" value="others">
```

**Why:** Defense-in-depth. Even if XA-1 watcher lags (e.g., SSR edge, reactive flush delay), a window-item with `v-if=false` is fully unmounted — its content cannot render.

---

## Phase XC — Middleware: Redirect Contractors from Edit Routes to Detail

**Research reference:** §2.241 Root Cause C
**File:** `pmo-frontend/middleware/permission.ts`
**Risk:** Low. Contractors navigating to `/coi/edit-[id]` are redirected to `/coi/detail-[id]` (same content, read-only view). No data is lost. No other user type is affected.

### XC-1: Add edit-route redirect inside the contractor branch

**Location:** `permission.ts`, inside the `if (isContractor.value)` block, before the allowlist check (between the `contractorCreationBlocked` check and the `allowedPrefixes` check).

```typescript
// XC-A: Redirect contractors from edit routes to detail (read-only view)
const editPathMatch = to.path.match(/^\/coi\/edit-(.+)/)
if (editPathMatch) {
  const id = editPathMatch[1]
  return navigateTo(`/coi/detail-${id}`)
}
```

Full context:
```typescript
if (isContractor.value) {
  const contractorCreationBlocked = ['/coi/new']
  if (contractorCreationBlocked.some(p => to.path === p)) {
    return navigateTo('/dashboard')
  }
  // XC-A: Redirect contractors from edit routes to detail (read-only view)
  const editPathMatch = to.path.match(/^\/coi\/edit-(.+)/)
  if (editPathMatch) {
    const id = editPathMatch[1]
    return navigateTo(`/coi/detail-${id}`)
  }
  const allowedPrefixes = ['/dashboard', '/coi', '/login', '/contractor']
  ...
}
```

**Why:** Architectural correctness — contractors are read-only users. The edit page is for authoring. Redirecting to the detail page gives contractors the correct read-only view (which already correctly filters tabs via `isTabVisible`). This eliminates the entire class of "contractor on edit page" issues.

---

## Phase XD — Edit Page: Defense-in-Depth Contractor Redirect

**Research reference:** §2.241 Root Cause D
**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`
**Risk:** Minimal. A `watchEffect` runs once on mount. If not a contractor, it exits immediately. No performance impact.

### XD-1: Add watchEffect contractor redirect at page setup

**Location:** `edit-[id].vue` setup block, after the `useCoiAccess(rawProject)` destructure (around line 49).

```typescript
// XD-A: Defense-in-depth — if a contractor somehow reaches this page, redirect to detail.
// Primary guard is middleware (XC). This is the fallback.
if (import.meta.client) {
  watchEffect(() => {
    if (isContractorUser.value && authStore.user) {
      router.replace(`/coi/detail-${projectId}`)
    }
  })
}
```

**Why:** Handles direct URL navigation, browser back, or any future middleware bypass. `router.replace` (not `push`) prevents the edit page from polluting browser history.

---

**Research reference:** §2.242
**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue`
**Risk:** Medium. Large deletion + restructure within one file. Left panel (institutional) is UNTOUCHED. Right panel state is replaced, not modified.

### YA-1: Add EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES set and EXTERNAL_CATEGORY_OPTIONS

After the `ACCESS_PRESETS` block, insert:

```typescript
// YA-A: External personnel category set — single source for isExternalUser + add panel options
const EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES = new Set([
  'CONTRACTOR', 'CONSTRUCTOR', 'SUPPLIER',
  'CONSULTANT', 'EXTERNAL_AUDITOR', 'EXTERNAL_PARTNER',
])

const EXTERNAL_CATEGORY_OPTIONS = [
  { title: 'Contractor',                    value: 'CONTRACTOR' },
  { title: 'Constructor',                   value: 'CONSTRUCTOR' },
  { title: 'Supplier',                      value: 'SUPPLIER' },
  { title: 'Consultant',                    value: 'CONSULTANT' },
  { title: 'External Auditor',              value: 'EXTERNAL_AUDITOR' },
  { title: 'External Implementing Partner', value: 'EXTERNAL_PARTNER' },
]
```

### YA-2: Add DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS function

After the existing `DEFAULT_PERMS` function:

```typescript
// YA-B: External personnel default to deny-all (VA-A enforcement for new assignments)
const DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS = (): FullPermissions => ({
  canCreate: false, canEdit: false, canDelete: false,
  canUpload: false, canReview: false, canApprove: false,
  tabProjectProfile: false, tabDatesDuration: false, tabTimeline: false,
  tabProgressReport: false, tabPersonnel: false,
  tabAttachments: false, tabOthers: false,
  accessLevel: 'Viewer',
})
```

### YA-3: Update isExternalUser to use EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES set

Replace:
```typescript
function isExternalUser(u: DraftUser): boolean {
  return u.personnelCategory === 'CONTRACTOR'
    || (u as any).userRole === 'Contractor'
}
```
With:
```typescript
function isExternalUser(u: DraftUser): boolean {
  return EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES.has(u.personnelCategory ?? '')
    || (u as any).userRole === 'Contractor'
}
```

### YA-4: Update draft watch VA-A isExternal check to use EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES

In `watch(() => props.assignedUsers, ...)`, replace:
```typescript
const isExternal = (u as any).personnel_category === 'CONTRACTOR'
  || (u as any).personnelCategory === 'CONTRACTOR'
  || (u as any).user_role === 'Contractor'
```
With:
```typescript
const isExternal = EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES.has((u as any).personnel_category ?? '')
  || EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES.has((u as any).personnelCategory ?? '')
  || (u as any).user_role === 'Contractor'
```

### YA-5: Add external single-add state and commitAddExternal

After the existing `commitAdd` / `removeUser` block:

```typescript
// YA-C: External single-add (mirrors institutional flow, deny-all defaults)
const addExternalUserId = ref<string | null>(null)
const addExternalCategory = ref('CONTRACTOR')
const addExternalProjectRole = ref('')

function commitAddExternal() {
  if (!addExternalUserId.value) return
  if (draft.value.some(d => d.id === addExternalUserId.value)) { toast.error('User already assigned'); return }
  const staff = staffUsers.value.find(s => s.id === addExternalUserId.value)
  if (!staff) return
  draft.value.push({
    id: staff.id, name: `${staff.first_name} ${staff.last_name}`, email: staff.email,
    role: null, department: staff.campus ?? null, phone: null,
    personnelCategory: addExternalCategory.value, projectRole: addExternalProjectRole.value || null,
    permissions: DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS(), _permOpen: false,
  })
  addExternalUserId.value = null; addExternalProjectRole.value = ''; addExternalCategory.value = 'CONTRACTOR'
}
```

### YA-6: Parameterize batch dialog for institutional vs external mode

Add `batchMode` ref and update `openBatchDialog`:

```typescript
const batchMode = ref<'institutional' | 'external'>('institutional')

function openBatchDialog(mode: 'institutional' | 'external' = 'institutional') {
  batchMode.value = mode
  batchSelected.value = []
  batchCategory.value = mode === 'external' ? 'CONTRACTOR' : 'IMPLEMENTING'
  batchProjectRole.value = ''; batchSearch.value = ''
  loadStaff(); batchDialog.value = true
}
```

Update `commitBatch` to use appropriate default perms:
```typescript
function commitBatch() {
  for (const uid of batchSelected.value) {
    if (draft.value.some(d => d.id === uid)) continue
    const staff = staffUsers.value.find(s => s.id === uid)
    if (!staff) continue
    draft.value.push({
      id: staff.id, name: `${staff.first_name} ${staff.last_name}`, email: staff.email,
      role: null, department: staff.campus ?? null, phone: null,
      personnelCategory: batchCategory.value, projectRole: batchProjectRole.value || null,
      permissions: batchMode.value === 'external' ? DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS() : DEFAULT_PERMS(),
      _permOpen: false,
    })
  }
  batchDialog.value = false
}
```

### YA-7: Remove all invite infrastructure

Delete the following blocks in their entirety:

**Contractor-specific search (replaced by YA-5):**
- `contractorUsers`, `contractorLoading`, `addContractorId`, `addContractorRole` refs
- `loadContractors()` function
- `commitAddContractor()` function

**Invite state:**
- `invites`, `contractors`, `inviteLoading`, `inviteGenerating`, `inviteError`, `copiedToken` refs
- `showInviteHistory`, `pendingInvites`, `historicInvites` computed

**Invite confirm dialogs:**
- `revokeConfirmDialog`, `revokeTargetId`, `deleteConfirmDialog`, `deleteTargetId` refs

**Invite functions:**
- `loadContractorData()`, `generateInvite()`, `revokeInvite()`, `deleteInvite()`
- `removeContractor()`, `copyInviteLink()`, `inviteStatusColor()`
- `promptRevoke()`, `confirmRevoke()`, `promptDelete()`, `confirmDelete()`

**Invite watcher:**
- `watch(() => props.projectId, (id) => { if (id && isAdmin.value) loadContractorData() })`

## Phase ZA — Backend: Distinguish ACCOUNT_INACTIVE from INVALID_CREDENTIALS

**Status:** ⬜ PENDING Phase 3 authorization
**Scope:** `pmo-backend/src/auth/auth.service.ts` — login() method only
**Research:** §2.243-A
**Risk:** LOW — additive pre-check; no existing validation path altered

### ZA-1: Add ACCOUNT_INACTIVE pre-check in `login()`

File: `pmo-backend/src/auth/auth.service.ts`

In `login()`, before the `validateUser()` call, add a lightweight pre-query to detect inactive accounts and throw a distinct error code:

```typescript
async login(dto: LoginDto) {
  // ZA-1: Pre-check account existence and activation status.
  // Returns a distinct error code so the frontend can show a
  // context-specific message instead of generic "Invalid credentials."
  const account = await this.em.findOne(
    User,
    { $or: [{ email: { $ilike: dto.identifier } }, { username: { $ilike: dto.identifier } }] },
    { filters: false },
  )
  if (account && !account.isActive) {
    this.logger.warn(`LOGIN_FAILURE: user_id=${account.id}, reason=ACCOUNT_INACTIVE`)
    throw new UnauthorizedException('ACCOUNT_INACTIVE')
  }

  const user = await this.validateUser(dto.identifier, dto.password)
  if (!user) {
    throw new UnauthorizedException('INVALID_CREDENTIALS')
  }
  // ... rest of login unchanged
```

**Constraint:** The pre-check must use `{ filters: false }` (bypass `notDeleted` soft-delete filter) so it finds the user even if soft-deleted. The existing `validateUser()` already uses the MikroORM ORM layer with its own filter behavior.

---

*Phase ZA implementation complete when: `POST /api/auth/login` with inactive account credentials returns HTTP 401 with body `{ message: 'ACCOUNT_INACTIVE' }` instead of `'INVALID_CREDENTIALS'`.*

---

## Phase ZB — Backend: Expand `findEligibleForAssignment()` to All Active Users

**Status:** ⬜ PENDING Phase 3 authorization
**Scope:** `pmo-backend/src/users/users.service.ts` — `findEligibleForAssignment()` only
**Research:** §2.243-B
**Risk:** LOW — additive query change; removes a filter, adds a field

### ZB-1: Remove role filter, add `email` field

File: `pmo-backend/src/users/users.service.ts`

Replace the `findEligibleForAssignment()` body:

```typescript
async findEligibleForAssignment(): Promise<any[]> {
  const conn = this.em.getConnection()
  const result = await conn.execute(
    `SELECT u.id, u.first_name, u.last_name, u.email, u.campus
     FROM users u
     WHERE u.deleted_at IS NULL
       AND u.is_active = true
     ORDER BY u.last_name, u.first_name`,
    [],
  )
  return result
}
```

**What changed:**
- Removed `AND EXISTS (... r.name IN ('Staff', 'Admin', 'SuperAdmin'))` filter
- Added `u.email` to SELECT — needed by `CiPersonnelAccessCard` autocomplete subtitle

**No controller/route changes.** `GET /api/users/eligible-for-assignment` endpoint is unchanged.

---

*Phase ZB implementation complete when: `GET /api/users/eligible-for-assignment` returns users with all roles (including Contractor) and includes `email` field in each row.*

---

## Phase ZC — Backend: Google OAuth Strategy Auto-Create New Users

**Status:** ⬜ PENDING Phase 3 authorization
**Scope:** `pmo-backend/src/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts`
**Research:** §2.243-C
**Risk:** LOW-MEDIUM — changes account creation behavior on OAuth flow; new User entity required in `GoogleStrategy` constructor

### ZC-1: Import User entity in GoogleStrategy

File: `pmo-backend/src/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts`

The strategy currently uses only raw SQL (`em.getConnection().execute`). To create users via ORM, import is needed. However, since `EntityManager` is already injected, we can use `em.create()` and `em.persistAndFlush()` directly.

No new imports needed beyond what exists.

### ZC-2: Replace "user not found" rejection with auto-create

File: `pmo-backend/src/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts`

Replace the `if (result.length === 0)` block (lines 63–70):

**Remove:**
```typescript
if (result.length === 0) {
  return done(
    new UnauthorizedException('No account found. Contact your administrator.'),
    false,
  )
}
```

**Replace with:**
```typescript
if (result.length === 0) {
  // ZC-A: Auto-create account for first Google OAuth login.
  // OAuth-verified accounts are activated immediately — no admin approval needed.
  const nameParts = (profile.displayName || email.split('@')[0]).split(' ')
  const firstName = nameParts[0] || ''
  const lastName = nameParts.length > 1 ? nameParts.slice(1).join(' ') : ''
  const { User } = await import('../../database/entities')
  const newUser = this.em.create(User, {
    email,
    username: email,
    googleId: profile.id,
    firstName,
    lastName,
    avatarUrl: profile.photos?.[0]?.value ?? undefined,
    isActive: true,
    status: 'ACTIVE',
    metadata: { registrationSource: 'google-oauth', registeredAt: new Date().toISOString() },
  })
  try {
    await this.em.persistAndFlush(newUser)
  } catch (err: any) {
    if (err?.code === '23505') {
      // Race condition: email registered between lookup and insert — fetch and continue
      const existing = await this.em.getConnection().execute(
        `SELECT id, email, is_active, google_id FROM users WHERE LOWER(email) = LOWER(?) AND deleted_at IS NULL LIMIT 1`,
        [email],
      )
      if (existing.length > 0) return done(null, existing[0])
    }
    return done(err, false)
  }
  this.logger.log(`GOOGLE_AUTO_CREATED: user_id=${newUser.id}, email=${email}`)
  return done(null, { id: newUser.id, email: newUser.email, is_active: true, google_id: newUser.googleId })
}
```

**Add logger** to `GoogleStrategy` constructor:
```typescript
private readonly logger = new Logger(GoogleStrategy.name)
```

Add `Logger` to the NestJS imports at the top.

**Directive 203 (link google_id if email already exists):** PRESERVED — remains at lines 84–88 unchanged. This covers the case where an account was pre-created by admin but had no `google_id` yet.

---

*Phase ZC implementation complete when: A Google account with an email NOT in the users table successfully authenticates and creates a new user in the `users` table with `is_active=true` and `google_id` populated.*

---

## Phase ZE — Backend: Assign Default Role on Account Activation

**Status:** ⬜ PENDING Phase 3 authorization
**Scope:** `pmo-backend/src/users/users.service.ts` — `activateUser()` method
**Research:** §2.243-F
**Risk:** LOW — additive SQL after existing activation UPDATE; 'Staff' role must exist in `roles` table (confirmed by seed migrations)

### ZE-1: Assign 'Staff' role after `activateUser()` if user has no roles

File: `pmo-backend/src/users/users.service.ts`

After the existing activation UPDATE and `rowCount` check, add:

```typescript
// ZE-A: Assign default 'Staff' role if user has no roles after activation.
// Prevents the "login works but sidebar is empty" confusion.
const existingRoles = await conn.execute(
  `SELECT id FROM user_roles WHERE user_id = ? LIMIT 1`,
  [id],
)
if (existingRoles.length === 0) {
  const staffRole = await conn.execute(
    `SELECT id FROM roles WHERE name = 'Staff' AND deleted_at IS NULL LIMIT 1`,
    [],
  )
  if (staffRole.length > 0) {
    await conn.execute(
      `INSERT INTO user_roles (id, user_id, role_id, is_superadmin, assigned_by, created_at, updated_at)
       VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), ?, ?, false, ?, NOW(), NOW())
       ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`,
      [id, staffRole[0].id, adminId],
    )
    this.logger.log(`USER_DEFAULT_ROLE_ASSIGNED: user=${id}, role=Staff, by=${adminId}`)
  }
}
```

**Invariant:** If user already has a role assigned by admin (pre-activation role grant), `existingRoles.length > 0` → skip. Role assignment is only a fallback.

---

*Phase ZE implementation complete when: Activating a PENDING user with no roles results in the user being assigned the 'Staff' role; user JWT after login contains `roles: ['Staff']`.*

---

## Phase ZF — Frontend: Login Page Error Message for Inactive Accounts

**Status:** ⬜ PENDING Phase 3 authorization
**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/pages/login.vue` — `handleLogin()` function only
**Research:** §2.243-A
**Risk:** NONE — conditional string replacement; no auth logic touched

### ZF-1: Map ACCOUNT_INACTIVE error code to descriptive message

File: `pmo-frontend/pages/login.vue`

Replace the `catch` block in `handleLogin()`:

```typescript
// ZF-A: Map backend error codes to user-readable messages
} catch (error: unknown) {
  const err = error as { message?: string; statusCode?: number }
  if (err.statusCode === 429) {
    errorMessage.value = 'Too many login attempts. Please wait a minute and try again.'
  } else if (err.statusCode === 401) {
    if (err.message === 'ACCOUNT_INACTIVE') {
      errorMessage.value = 'Your account is pending activation by an administrator. You will be contacted when your account is approved.'
    } else {
      errorMessage.value = 'Invalid credentials. Please check your email/username and password.'
    }
  } else if (err.statusCode === 503) {
    errorMessage.value = 'Backend server not running. Please start the backend first.'
  } else {
    errorMessage.value = err.message || 'Login failed. Please try again.'
  }
}
```

---

*Phase ZF implementation complete when: Login attempt with PENDING account credentials shows "Your account is pending activation by an administrator" instead of "Invalid credentials".*

---

  - Document delete buttons: `v-if="canDeleteResources"` (currently shows if `canEditCurrentProject`)

  - Checklist edit button in `CiDocumentChecklist`: pass `:can-edit="canUploadDocuments"` instead of `canEditCurrentProject`
  - Checklist remarks: pass `:can-edit-remarks="canEditCurrentProject"` (retain admin-only for remarks)
- [x] ZP-2: **Global filter bar** — add a search `<v-text-field>` above the 2-column top row:
  - `const attachmentFilter = ref('')`
  - Computed `filteredKeyDocs`: `keyDocs.filter(d => matches(d.fileName, attachmentFilter))`
  - Computed `filteredGallery`: `existingGallery.filter(g => matches(g.caption || g.category, attachmentFilter))`
  - Computed `filteredOtherDocs`: `otherDocs.filter(d => matches(d.fileName, attachmentFilter))`
  - `matches(text, q)`: `text.toLowerCase().includes(q.toLowerCase())`
  - Apply to list renders in all 4 sections
- [x] ZP-3: **Batch upload / drag-and-drop** for Key Documents and Other Attachments:
  - Add `multiple` attribute to file inputs: `<v-file-input multiple ...>`
  - Wrap upload logic to iterate over all selected files: `for (const file of files) { fd.append('file', file); await api.upload(...) }`
  - Add `@dragover.prevent` and `@drop.prevent="handleFileDrop"` on the upload card body for drag-and-drop
  - `handleFileDrop(event: DragEvent)` — extract `event.dataTransfer.files`, filter by MIME/extension, call upload loop
- [x] ZP-4: **Preview limit (5 items)** — Key Documents and Other Attachments sections show max 5 items inline; remaining items visible via fullscreen dialog (dialogs already exist):
  - `keyDocsPreview = computed(() => filteredKeyDocs.value.slice(0, 5))`
  - Add `v-if` guard: render "... and X more" chip that triggers `keyDocsFullscreen = true` when count > 5
  - Same pattern for `otherDocs` → `otherDocsPreview`

*Phase ZP complete when: Upload buttons respect `canUploadDocuments`; delete buttons respect `canDeleteResources`; filter bar narrows all 4 sections simultaneously; multi-file drag-and-drop works on upload cards; >5 files show "X more" overflow trigger.*

  - New tab order: `['overview', 'progress', 'timeline', 'analytics', 'documents', 'team', 'others']`

- [x] ZQ-6: **Audit log section** — add `CiAuditLogPanel` (from ZR) at the bottom of the documents window-item, below Other Attachments

*Phase ZQ complete when: detail-[id].vue has 7 tabs (gallery removed); the `documents` tab shows the enterprise 4-section layout; gallery images render in Profile/General sub-tabs; global filter works; audit log panel is visible at bottom.*

## Phase ZS — DB Migration: `template_url` on `construction_document_types` + New `construction_document_submissions` Table

**Research reference:** §2.247-B, §2.247-C
**File:** New MikroORM migration file
**Authorized:** ⬜ (awaiting Phase 3 RUN_ACE)

### Steps

- [x] ZS-1: Create `Migration20260527010000_TemplateUrlAndSubmissionsTable.ts` in `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/`
- [x] ZS-2: `up()`:
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE construction_document_types ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS template_url TEXT NULL;

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS construction_document_submissions (
    id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    checklist_item_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES construction_document_checklist(id),
    project_id        UUID NOT NULL,
    document_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id),
    version           INTEGER NOT NULL,
    submitted_by      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    submitted_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    submission_notes  TEXT NULL,
    created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
  );
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_doc_submissions_checklist ON construction_document_submissions(checklist_item_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_doc_submissions_project   ON construction_document_submissions(project_id);
  ```
- [x] ZS-3: `down()`:
  ```sql
  DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_doc_submissions_project;
  DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_doc_submissions_checklist;
  DROP TABLE IF EXISTS construction_document_submissions;
  ALTER TABLE construction_document_types DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS template_url;
  ```
- [ ] ZS-4: **OPERATOR ACTION REQUIRED** — run `npx mikro-orm migration:up` from `pmo-backend/`

*Phase ZS complete when: `construction_document_types` has `template_url TEXT NULL`; `construction_document_submissions` table exists with both indexes.*

---

## Phase ZT — Backend: Entity, Endpoints, Soft-Delete Activation, Deletion Guard

**Research reference:** §2.247-A, §2.247-D, §2.247-E, §2.247-F
**Files:** New `construction-document-submission.entity.ts`; `construction-projects.service.ts`; `construction-projects.controller.ts`; new DTOs
**Authorized:** ⬜ (awaiting Phase 3 RUN_ACE)

### Steps

- [x] ZT-1: **Create `ConstructionDocumentSubmission` entity** at `pmo-backend/src/database/entities/construction-document-submission.entity.ts`:
  - `@Entity({ tableName: 'construction_document_submissions' })`
  - Fields: `id` (UUID PK), `checklistItemId` (UUID, not a relation — raw FK), `projectId` (UUID), `documentId` (UUID, not a relation — raw FK), `version` (integer), `submittedBy` (UUID), `submittedAt` (timestamptz), `submissionNotes` (text nullable), `createdAt` (timestamptz auto)
  - Register in `entities/index.ts` + `ConstructionProjectsModule` entity list

- [x] ZT-2: **Add `templateUrl` to `ConstructionDocumentType` entity** — `@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'text' }) templateUrl?: string`

- [x] ZT-3: **Activate soft-delete in `removeDocument()`** — replace the current `em.removeAndFlush(doc)` + `uploadsService.deleteFile()` block:
  ```typescript
  // Check if any submission history references this document
  const subCount = await em.getConnection().execute(
    'SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS n FROM construction_document_submissions WHERE document_id = ?',
    [docId]
  )
  const hasHistory = (subCount[0]?.n ?? 0) > 0

  // Soft-delete the document
  doc.deletedAt = new Date()
  doc.deletedBy = userId
  await em.flush()

  // Only clear checklist link if NO submission history
  if (!hasHistory) {
    // existing: clear linked_document_id on checklist items
  }
  // Never call uploadsService.deleteFile() — physical file preserved
  ```

- [x] ZT-4: **Add submission POST endpoint** — service method `submitDocumentVersion(projectId, checklistItemId, file, notes, user)`:
  - Upload file via `uploadsService.uploadFile()`
  - Create new `Document` row (same pattern as `addDocumentToProject()`)
  - Determine next `version` = current `MAX(version)` for this `checklist_item_id` + 1 (or 1 if none)
  - Insert into `construction_document_submissions`
  - UPDATE checklist item: `linked_document_id = newDocId`, `current_version = version`, `submission_status = 'SUBMITTED'`, `submitted_by = user.sub`, `submitted_at = NOW()`
  - Fire audit log: `action=SUBMIT`, `section=CHECKLIST`, `metadata: { version, fileName, checklistItemId }`

- [x] ZT-5: **Add submission GET endpoint** — service method `getDocumentSubmissions(projectId, checklistItemId)`:
  - Raw SQL: `SELECT s.*, d.original_name, d.file_path, d.file_size, u.name AS submitter_name FROM construction_document_submissions s JOIN documents d ON d.id = s.document_id JOIN users u ON u.id = s.submitted_by WHERE s.checklist_item_id = ? AND s.project_id = ? ORDER BY s.version DESC`
  - Return array of submission history rows

- [x] ZT-6: **Add template upload endpoint** — service method `uploadDocumentTypeTemplate(typeId, file, userId)`:
  - Upload via `uploadsService.uploadFile(file, userId, 'coi-templates')`
  - Find `ConstructionDocumentType` by id, set `templateUrl = result.filePath`, flush
  - Controller: `POST /document-types/:typeId/template` with `@FileInterceptor('file')`, `@Roles('Admin', 'SuperAdmin')`

- [x] ZT-7: **Wire controller routes** for all new endpoints:
  - `GET  /:id/document-checklist/:itemId/submissions` → `getDocumentSubmissions()`
  - `POST /:id/document-checklist/:itemId/submissions` → `submitDocumentVersion()` with `@FileInterceptor`
  - `POST /document-types/:typeId/template` → `uploadDocumentTypeTemplate()` with `@FileInterceptor`

*Phase ZT complete when: All three new routes respond correctly; `removeDocument()` soft-deletes instead of hard-deleting; submission history is recorded in `construction_document_submissions`.*

---

## Phase ZU — Frontend: `CiDocumentChecklist.vue` — Template Download, Submit/Resubmit Dialog, History Drawer

**Research reference:** §2.247-G
**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiDocumentChecklist.vue`
**Authorized:** ⬜ (awaiting Phase 3 RUN_ACE)

### Steps

- [x] ZU-1: **Extend `ChecklistItem` interface** — add `documentType?: { templateUrl?: string }` (or ensure the fetched checklist item includes `templateUrl` from the join)
  - Verify: `findDocumentChecklist()` backend query must JOIN `construction_document_types` and include `template_url` in the result shape; adapt frontend adapter if needed

- [x] ZU-2: **Template download chip** — for each checklist item, if `item.documentType?.templateUrl` is truthy, render:
  ```html
  <v-chip
    size="x-small"
    color="info"
    prepend-icon="mdi-file-download"
    :href="item.documentType.templateUrl"
    target="_blank"
    rel="noopener"
  >
    Download Blank Form
  </v-chip>
  ```
  Position: in the action chip row, before the "Submit" button

- [x] ZU-3: **Submit/Resubmit dialog** — replace or augment the current single-button upload action:
  - Dialog ref: `submitDialog = ref(false)`, `submitDialogItem = ref<ChecklistItem | null>(null)`
  - Dialog fields: file input (`v-file-input`, required), notes textarea (optional)
  - On confirm: call `POST /:id/document-checklist/:itemId/submissions` via `api.upload()` with FormData `{ file, notes }`
  - After success: refresh checklist data, show success snackbar
  - Button label: `linked_document_id` is null → "Submit"; else → "Resubmit" (with version count badge)

  - Fetches `GET /:id/document-checklist/:itemId/submissions` on open
  - Renders each submission as a `v-timeline-item`: version number, submitter name, submitted_at date, file name with download link, notes (if any)
  - Close button in drawer header

- [x] ZU-5: **RBAC gate** — Submit/Resubmit button: `v-if="canEdit"` (existing prop); History button: visible to all authenticated roles (no gate needed — read-only)

*Phase ZU complete when: Each checklist item with a `templateUrl` shows a "Download Blank Form" chip; "Submit/Resubmit" dialog creates a new submission row and updates the checklist item; "History" drawer lists all past submissions with download links.*

## Phase ZW â€” DB Migration: Expand Document Type Taxonomy (ECO Forms + Supporting Documents)

**Research reference:** Â§2.248-A, Â§2.248-B
**File:** New MikroORM migration `Migration20260527020000_ExpandDocumentTypeTaxonomy.ts`
**Authorized:** â¬œ (awaiting Phase 3 RUN_ACE)

### Governance Directives (ZW)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| ZW-D1 | All 25 new type codes use `is_required = FALSE`. These are evidence/submission types, not mandatory compliance items. |
| ZW-D2 | `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` in `coiFormState.ts` is UNCHANGED. ECO Forms and SD types are separate repositories. |
| ZW-D3 | The existing GROUP_1â€“GROUP_6 rows must not be modified. Only the check constraint is altered. |
| ZW-D4 | Contractor-specific certificates (GYM/IEB x 4 contractors) are ad-hoc examples â€” NOT seeded as system type codes. |
| ZW-D5 | SD-ECO-ECO-019 assigned to "Mandatory Certification - LUDIP" (no official SD code in the image). |

### Steps

- [x] ZW-1: Create `Migration20260527020000_ExpandDocumentTypeTaxonomy.ts` in `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/`
- [x] ZW-2: `up()` â€” ALTER check constraint:
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE construction_document_types
    DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS construction_document_types_group_code_check;
  ALTER TABLE construction_document_types
    ADD CONSTRAINT construction_document_types_group_code_check
    CHECK (group_code IN (
      'GROUP_1','GROUP_2','GROUP_3','GROUP_4','GROUP_5','GROUP_6',
      'ECO_FORMS','SD_ORDERS','SD_REPORTS','SD_CERTS'
    ));
  ```
- [x] ZW-3: `up()` â€” INSERT ECO Forms (6 types):
  ```sql
  INSERT INTO construction_document_types
    (group_code, group_label, type_code, type_label, is_required, sort_order)
  VALUES
    ('ECO_FORMS','ECO Forms','F_ECO_001','Data Request Form',FALSE,1),
    ('ECO_FORMS','ECO Forms','F_ECO_002','Service Request Form',FALSE,2),
    ('ECO_FORMS','ECO Forms','F_ECO_003','Initial Project Proposal Form',FALSE,3),
    ('ECO_FORMS','ECO Forms','F_ECO_004','Concrete Pouring Record (CPR)',FALSE,4),
    ('ECO_FORMS','ECO Forms','F_ECO_005','Pre-Inspection Form',FALSE,5),
    ('ECO_FORMS','ECO Forms','F_ECO_006','Contract Time Extension Request',FALSE,6)
  ON CONFLICT (type_code) DO NOTHING;
  ```
- [x] ZW-4: `up()` â€” INSERT SD Orders (7 types):
  ```sql
  INSERT INTO construction_document_types
    (group_code, group_label, type_code, type_label, is_required, sort_order)
  VALUES
    ('SD_ORDERS','Supporting Docs â€” Orders','SD_ECO_001','Variation Order',FALSE,1),
    ('SD_ORDERS','Supporting Docs â€” Orders','SD_ECO_002','Work Suspension Order',FALSE,2),
    ('SD_ORDERS','Supporting Docs â€” Orders','SD_ECO_003','Work Resumption Order',FALSE,3),
    ('SD_ORDERS','Supporting Docs â€” Orders','SD_ECO_004','Contract Time Extension Order',FALSE,4),
    ('SD_ORDERS','Supporting Docs â€” Orders','SD_ECO_005','Notice of Non-Compliance',FALSE,5),
    ('SD_ORDERS','Supporting Docs â€” Orders','SD_ECO_006','Show Cause Order',FALSE,6),
    ('SD_ORDERS','Supporting Docs â€” Orders','SD_ECO_007','Notice of Termination',FALSE,7)
  ON CONFLICT (type_code) DO NOTHING;
  ```
- [x] ZW-5: `up()` â€” INSERT SD Reports & Monitoring (8 types):
  ```sql
  INSERT INTO construction_document_types
    (group_code, group_label, type_code, type_label, is_required, sort_order)
  VALUES
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_008','Construction Logbook',FALSE,1),
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_009','Weekly Accomplishment Report',FALSE,2),
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_010','Monthly Progress Report',FALSE,3),
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_011','Site Instruction',FALSE,4),
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_012','Site Inspection Report',FALSE,5),
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_013','Project Inspection Report',FALSE,6),
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_014','Quality Assessment Report',FALSE,7),
    ('SD_REPORTS','Supporting Docs â€” Reports & Monitoring','SD_ECO_015','Safety Compliance Report',FALSE,8)
  ON CONFLICT (type_code) DO NOTHING;
  ```

- [x] ZW-6: `up()` â€” INSERT SD Certifications (4 types):

  ```sql
    (group_code, group_label, type_code, type_label, is_required, sort_order)
  ```

  ```sql

  ALTER TABLE construction_document_types DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS construction_document_types_group_code_check;
  ```

## Phase ZX â€” Backend: Grouped Document Types Endpoint + Bulk Template Status

**Research reference:** Â§2.248-H, Â§2.248-B
**Files:** `construction-projects.service.ts`, `construction-projects.controller.ts`
**Authorized:** â¬œ (awaiting Phase 3 RUN_ACE)

### Steps

- [x] ZX-1: **Extend `findDocumentTypes()`** to return grouped structure accepted by the frontend hub:
  ```typescript
  async findDocumentTypesGrouped(): Promise<{ groupCode: string; groupLabel: string; types: ConstructionDocumentType[] }[]> {
    const all = await this.docTypeRepo.find({ isActive: true }, { orderBy: { groupCode: 'asc', sortOrder: 'asc' } })
    const groups = new Map<string, { groupCode: string; groupLabel: string; types: ConstructionDocumentType[] }>()
    for (const t of all) {
      if (!groups.has(t.groupCode)) groups.set(t.groupCode, { groupCode: t.groupCode, groupLabel: t.groupLabel, types: [] })
      groups.get(t.groupCode)!.types.push(t)
    }
    return Array.from(groups.values())
  }
  ```
  - Add new route `GET /document-types/grouped` alongside existing `GET /document-types`
  - Existing flat endpoint unchanged (backward compat)

- [x] ZX-2: **Template bulk status endpoint** â€” `GET /document-types/template-status` returns a flat map `{ typeCode: string, templateUrl: string | null }[]` for all types. Used by the hub to show "template available" indicators without fetching the full type list again.
  - Implementation: SELECT id, type_code, template_url FROM construction_document_types WHERE is_active = true

- [x] ZX-3: **Wire new controller routes** (must be ABOVE the `:id` route to avoid UUID collision):
  ```typescript
  @Get('document-types/grouped')
  @Roles('Admin', 'Staff', 'Viewer', 'Auditor')
  findDocumentTypesGrouped() { return this.service.findDocumentTypesGrouped() }

  @Get('document-types/template-status')
  @Roles('Admin', 'Staff', 'Viewer', 'Auditor')
  getDocumentTypeTemplateStatus() { return this.service.getDocumentTypeTemplateStatus() }
  ```

*Phase ZX complete when: `GET /document-types/grouped` returns ECO_FORMS, SD_ORDERS, SD_REPORTS, SD_CERTS groups alongside GROUP_1â€“6; `GET /document-types/template-status` returns templateUrl per typeCode.*

## Phase ZY â€” Frontend: Core Hub Infrastructure (Filter Composable + Repository Cards + Hub Sections 1, 2, 5, 6, 7)

### Governance Directives (ZY)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|

| ZY-D2 | The hub must be backward-compatible: existing `canUpload`, `canDelete`, `canEditRemarks` prop API unchanged. |
| ZY-D3 | Gallery data fetched via `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/gallery` within the hub (same as current detail-[id].vue). |
| ZY-D4 | `api.del()` not `api.delete()` â€” JB-D3 directive preserved. |
| ZY-D5 | Upload forms hidden by default; shown only when user clicks "Upload" button (progressive disclosure). |

### Steps

**ZY-1: `useAttachmentFilter` composable** (`pmo-frontend/composables/useAttachmentFilter.ts`)
- [x] ZY-1a: Implement composable with `searchText`, `filterType`, `filterSection`, `filterDateFrom`, `filterDateTo`, `filterStatus` refs
- [x] ZY-1b: `matchesDoc(doc)` â€” AND-evaluates all active filters against a document row
- [x] ZY-1c: `matchesGallery(img)` â€” evaluates text + date filters against a gallery row
- [x] ZY-1d: `activeCount` computed + `reset()` function
- [x] ZY-1e: Filter UI sub-component: always-visible search bar + collapsible advanced panel showing active filter chips with `activeCount` badge

**ZY-2: `CiRepositoryCard.vue`** (`pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiRepositoryCard.vue`)
- [x] ZY-2a: Props: `title`, `icon`, `color`, `docCount`, `completedCount`, `totalTypes`, `latestUpload`, `canUpload`, `loading`
- [x] ZY-2b: Emits: `open` (full modal), `upload` (quick upload trigger)
- [x] ZY-2c: Template: card with header (icon + title + count chip), completion progress bar, latest upload line, two action buttons ("Open Repository" + optional "â†‘ Upload")
- [x] ZY-2d: Completion bar formula: `completedCount / totalTypes` (types with at least one submitted document)

**ZY-3: `CiRepositoryModal.vue`** (`pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiRepositoryModal.vue`)
- [x] ZY-3a: Props: `title`, `icon`, `color`, `projectId`, `typeCodes: string[]`, `canUpload`, `canDelete`, `mode: 'view'|'edit'|'staging'`
- [x] ZY-3b: Fetches documents filtered by `typeCodes` from parent's already-fetched `documents` prop (no extra API call)
- [x] ZY-3c: Shows: grouped file list per typeCode, downloadable blank template chip per type (if `templateUrl`), upload form (if canUpload, hidden by default), submission history button per file
- [x] ZY-3d: Filter bar inside modal using `useAttachmentFilter`
- [x] ZY-3e: Staging mode: renders staged files list, "Add File" action queues to parent

**ZY-4: `CiAttachmentHub.vue` â€” core structure + Sections 1, 2, 5, 6, 7**
- [x] ZY-4a: Props + emits interface (see Â§2.248-F)
- [x] ZY-4b: `fetchDocuments()` â€” GET `/api/construction-projects/:id/documents`
- [x] ZY-4c: `fetchGallery()` â€” GET `/api/construction-projects/:id/gallery`
- [x] ZY-4d: `fetchDocTypes()` â€” GET `/document-types/grouped` (lazy, on mount)
- [x] ZY-4e: Internal section navigation â€” horizontal `v-tabs` within the hub, collapsible to a select dropdown on mobile
- [x] ZY-4f: **Section 1 â€” Key Documents**: preview card (5 items, overflow chip), fullscreen modal with `<CiRepositoryModal>` using `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES`, upload form gated by `canUpload`, drag-and-drop zone
- [x] ZY-4g: **Section 2 â€” Gallery**: Profile/General inner tabs, 5-item grid preview, upload form (profile + general) gated by `canUpload`, fullscreen modal
- [x] ZY-4h: **Section 5 â€” Compliance Checklist**: delegates to `<CiDocumentChecklist :can-edit="canUpload" :can-edit-remarks="canEditRemarks" :documents="documents" />`
- [x] ZY-4i: **Section 6 â€” Other Attachments**: documents not in any known typeCode group + external links + MOV evidence read-only; upload form gated by `canUpload`
- [x] ZY-4j: **Section 7 â€” Audit Log**: delegates to `<CiAuditLogPanel :project-id="projectId" />`

*Phase ZY complete when: `CiAttachmentHub` renders all five sections correctly in isolation; filter composable narrows all section lists simultaneously; repository cards show correct counts.*

## Phase ZZ-B â€” Frontend: Sync detail-[id].vue, edit-[id].vue, new.vue to Use CiAttachmentHub

**Research reference:** Â§2.248-E, Â§2.248-I
**Files:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`, `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`, `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue`
**Authorized:** â¬œ (awaiting Phase 3 RUN_ACE)

### Governance Directives (ZZ-B)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| ZZ-B-D1 | The attachment window-item in all three pages is replaced with a single `<CiAttachmentHub>` call. All attachment state (documents, gallery, docTypes) moves into the hub. |
| ZZ-B-D2 | `detail-[id].vue`: `fetchDocuments()` and `fetchGallery()` calls stay on the page only if other parts of the page need them. If only the attachment tab uses them, move entirely into hub. |
| ZZ-B-D3 | `new.vue`: `pendingDocs`, `pendingImages`, `pendingLinks` are replaced by the hub's staging queue exposed via `@update:pending-queue`. The `handleCreate()` upload loop reads from `pendingQueue.value`. |
| ZZ-B-D4 | The fullscreen dialog refs (`keyDocsFullscreen`, `galleryFullscreen`, etc.) are removed from the page scope â€” they move into the hub. |
| ZZ-B-D5 | No regression: all existing functionality (upload, delete, filter, checklist, audit log) must work identically after this replacement. |

### Steps

**detail-[id].vue:**
- [x] ZZ-B-1: Remove the entire documents window-item content (lines ~2971â€“3120 currently)
- [x] ZZ-B-2: Replace with: `<CiAttachmentHub :project-id="projectId" mode="view" :can-upload="false" :can-delete="false" :can-edit-remarks="canEditCurrentProject" />`
- [x] ZZ-B-3: Remove now-orphaned local refs: `attachmentFilter`, `attachmentMatches`, `documentFiles`, `documentLinks`, `documentsByType`, `docsWindowOpen`, `docsWindowSearch`, `docsWindowSort`, `filteredAllDocs`, `keyDocuments`, `generalGalleryImages`, `docsWindowCategories`
- [x] ZZ-B-4: Retain `fetchDocuments()` and `fetchGallery()` calls in `onMounted` ONLY if used outside the attachment tab (e.g., overview tab shows profile gallery). Otherwise remove.

**edit-[id].vue:**
- [x] ZZ-B-5: Remove the attachment window-item content (lines ~2539â€“2872 currently)
- [x] ZZ-B-6: Replace with:
  ```html
  <CiAttachmentHub
    :project-id="projectId"
    mode="edit"
    :can-upload="canUploadDocuments"
    :can-delete="canDeleteResources"
    :can-edit-remarks="canEditCurrentProject"
  />
  ```
- [x] ZZ-B-7: Remove now-orphaned refs: `attachmentFilter`, `filteredKeyDocs`, `filteredOtherDocs`, `filteredProfileGallery`, `filteredGeneralGallery`, `keyDocFile`, `keyDocType`, `keyDocTitle`, `keyDocUploading`, `keyDocsFullscreen`, `galleryFullscreen`, `checklistFullscreen`, `otherDocsFullscreen`, `handleKeyDocDrop`, `handleOtherDocDrop`, `uploadKeyDoc`
- [x] ZZ-B-8: Retain `existingDocs` ref only if used elsewhere in the edit page (e.g., MOV section, document picker). If not, remove entirely.

**new.vue:**
- [x] ZZ-B-9: Add `pendingQueue` ref: `const pendingQueue = ref<StagedQueue>({ docs: [], images: [], links: [] })`
- [x] ZZ-B-10: Replace documents window-item content with:
  ```html
  <CiAttachmentHub
    mode="staging"
    :can-upload="true"
    :can-delete="true"
    :model-value="pendingQueue"
    @update:model-value="pendingQueue = $event"
  />
  ```
- [x] ZZ-B-11: Update `handleCreate()` to read from `pendingQueue.value.docs`, `pendingQueue.value.images`, `pendingQueue.value.links` instead of `pendingDocs.value`, `pendingImages.value`, `pendingLinks.value`
- [x] ZZ-B-12: Remove old `pendingDocs`, `pendingImages`, `pendingLinks` refs and all associated `addPending*` functions

*Phase ZZ-B complete when: All three pages render the attachment section exclusively via `<CiAttachmentHub>`; no triplication of attachment logic remains; functional regression test confirms upload, delete, filter, checklist, gallery, Supporting Documents, ECO Forms, and audit log all work correctly from each page.*

## Phase AAA — Enterprise Attachment Hub v2 (Major Refactor)

> **Status:** ⬜ PLAN READY — Awaiting Phase 3 Authorization (`RUN_ACE, PROCEED WITH PHASE 3`)
> **Research Reference:** `research.md` §2.249 (all subsections A–G)
> **Baseline:** ZW–ZZ implemented. `CiAttachmentHub`, `CiRepositoryCard`, `CiRepositoryModal`, `CiDocumentChecklist`, `CiAuditLogPanel` all exist.
> **Goal:** Transform the Attachment Tab into a centralized enterprise document governance system — CPES-ready, compliance-tracked, audit-driven.

---

### Governance Directives (Phase AAA)

| ID | Directive |
|----|-----------|
| AAA-D1 | `CiAttachmentHub.vue` remains the ONLY attachment renderer for all three COI pages (detail/edit/new). No attachment logic in page files. |
| AAA-D2 | The Compliance Checklist (Section 5, now Section 1) MUST be the default active section when `hasProject = true`. When staging (new.vue), default to `key`. |
| AAA-D3 | CPES documents are stored in `construction_document_types` with group_code = `CPES_DOCS`. The existing `CiDocumentChecklist` will automatically include them (handles all groups). |
| AAA-D4 | Audit log fix (AAA-A) is a prerequisite for the "recent activity" feature in the enterprise modal (AAA-I). Implement AAA-A before AAA-I. |
| AAA-D5 | Custom key document sections are per-project (stored as JSONB on `construction_projects`), NOT global document types. Do NOT insert per-project sections into `construction_document_types`. |
| AAA-D6 | Batch upload = frontend loop over multiple selected files calling single-file endpoint. No new backend endpoint. |
| AAA-D7 | `CiRepositoryModal` handles documents (file-type). `CiGalleryModal` handles images (gallery-type). They are separate components — do NOT merge. |
| AAA-D8 | All filter/search/sort/pagination in modal windows is CLIENT-SIDE on props data. No new paginated backend endpoints. |
| AAA-D9 | `api.del()` not `api.delete()` — JB-D3. |
| AAA-D10 | New migration filename: `Migration20260527030000_AddCpesDocumentTypes.ts` (next in sequence after ZW migration). |

---

### Sub-Phase AAA-A — Audit Log 404 Fix

**Scope:** `pmo-backend/src/activity-logs/activity-log.controller.ts`
**Risk:** ZERO — 1-line change, no logic change, zero side effects
**Dependencies:** None

#### AAA-A-1: Fix controller prefix

Change line 8:
```typescript
// BEFORE
@Controller('api/activity-logs')
// AFTER
@Controller('activity-logs')
```

**Why:** `main.ts` sets global prefix `api` via `app.setGlobalPrefix`. The controller was double-prefixing → route registered at `/api/api/activity-logs`. Fix restores it to `/api/activity-logs`. See research §2.249-C.

**Restart required:** Yes — NestJS route registration happens at startup.

---

### Sub-Phase AAA-B — CPES Document Type Migration

**Scope:** New MikroORM migration
**Risk:** LOW — additive INSERT only; ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING prevents duplicates
**Dependencies:** AAA-A (can run in parallel — no dependency)

#### AAA-B-1: Create migration file

File: `pmo-backend/src/database/mikro-migrations/Migration20260527030000_AddCpesDocumentTypes.ts`

```typescript
import { Migration } from '@mikro-orm/migrations';

export class Migration20260527030000_AddCpesDocumentTypes extends Migration {
  async up(): Promise<void> {
    // 1. Drop old check constraint
    await this.execute(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_document_types
        DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS construction_document_types_group_code_check
    `);
    // 2. Re-add with CPES_DOCS added
    await this.execute(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_document_types
        ADD CONSTRAINT construction_document_types_group_code_check
        CHECK (group_code IN (
          'GROUP_1','GROUP_2','GROUP_3','GROUP_4','GROUP_5','GROUP_6',
          'ECO_FORMS','SD_ORDERS','SD_REPORTS','SD_CERTS',
          'CPES_DOCS'
        ))
    `);
    // 3. INSERT 22 CPES document types
    await this.execute(`
      INSERT INTO construction_document_types
        (group_code, group_label, type_code, type_label, is_required, sort_order, is_active)
      VALUES
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_STE','Approved Statement of Time Elapsed (STE)',true,10,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_SWA','Approved Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA)',true,20,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_VAR_ORDERS','All Approved Variation Orders',false,30,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_TIME_EXT','All Approved Time Extensions',false,40,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_SUSPENSION','Time Suspension/Resume Orders and Monthly Suspension Reports',false,50,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_CONTRACTOR_LIC','Contractor Valid License (or JV Special License and Agreement)',true,60,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_BOQ','Contract of Agreement and Bill of Quantities (BOQ)',true,70,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_NTP','Notice to Proceed (NTP)',true,80,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_MATERIALS_ENG','Materials Engineers Accreditation (for projects above Php 150M)',false,90,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_COMPLETION_INSP','Final Completion Inspection Report',true,100,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_CERT_COMPLETION','Certificate of Completion',true,110,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_STRAIGHT_LINE','Straight Line Diagram (Roads and Flood Control)',false,120,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_AS_BUILT','As-Built Plan',true,130,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_POW','Program of Works (POW)',true,140,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_MATERIALS_TEST','Materials Test Reports/Results',true,150,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_QCP','Original and Revised Quality Control Program (QCP)',true,160,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_APPROVED_PLANS','Approved Plans',true,170,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_CPM_SCHEDULE','CPM Work Schedule or Bar Chart / Manpower and Equipment Schedule',true,180,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_ORG_CHART','Organizational Chart and Financial Chart (S-curve or cash flow)',true,190,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_LAYOUT','General Layout of Project Facilities',false,200,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_SUBCONTRACTORS','List of Subcontractors or Suppliers',false,210,true),
        ('CPES_DOCS','CPES Documentary Requirements','CPES_COVER_SHEET','Cover Sheet (Annex-8)',true,220,true)
      ON CONFLICT (type_code) DO NOTHING
    `);
  }

  async down(): Promise<void> {
    await this.execute(`
      DELETE FROM construction_document_types WHERE group_code = 'CPES_DOCS'
    `);
    await this.execute(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_document_types
        DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS construction_document_types_group_code_check
    `);
    await this.execute(`
      ALTER TABLE construction_document_types
        ADD CONSTRAINT construction_document_types_group_code_check
        CHECK (group_code IN (
          'GROUP_1','GROUP_2','GROUP_3','GROUP_4','GROUP_5','GROUP_6',
          'ECO_FORMS','SD_ORDERS','SD_REPORTS','SD_CERTS'
        ))
    `);
  }
}
```

**Operator action required (AAA-B-2):** Run `npx mikro-orm migration:up` from `pmo-backend/` after implementing.

---

### Sub-Phase AAA-C — Document Service: Uploader Name

**Scope:** `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts`
**Risk:** LOW — adds an optional field to existing response; consumers that don't use it are unaffected
**Dependencies:** None

#### AAA-C-1: Locate `getDocumentsForProject` raw SQL

Find the method that lists documents for a project. It currently returns a flat array of documents joined from the `documents` table where `documentable_id = projectId` and `documentable_type = 'CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT'`.

#### AAA-C-2: Add LEFT JOIN to users table

Modify the raw SQL SELECT to include:
```sql
SELECT d.*, u.full_name AS uploaded_by_name, u.email AS uploaded_by_email
FROM documents d
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = d.uploaded_by
WHERE d.documentable_id = ? AND d.documentable_type = 'CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT'
  AND d.deleted_at IS NULL
ORDER BY d.created_at DESC
```

Return `uploaded_by_name` (prefer `full_name`, fallback to `email`) as an additional field on each document row.

#### AAA-C-3: Update `HubDoc` interface in `CiAttachmentHub.vue`

Add `uploadedByName?: string` to the `HubDoc` interface (line ~16).

In document list items in the hub template, render:
```html
<v-list-item-subtitle v-if="doc.uploadedByName" class="text-caption text-grey">
  Uploaded by {{ doc.uploadedByName }}
</v-list-item-subtitle>
```

---

### Sub-Phase AAA-D — Hub Section Reorder + CPES Section + Default Active

**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiAttachmentHub.vue`
**Risk:** LOW — section reorder only; no logic changes to existing sections
**Dependencies:** AAA-B (CPES types must exist in taxonomy for `cpesTypes` computed to return data)

#### AAA-D-1: Add CPES section refs

After `sdCertsCodes` computed (line ~116), add:
```typescript
const cpesTypes = computed(() => docTypeGroups.value.find((g) => g.groupCode === 'CPES_DOCS')?.types ?? [])
const cpesCodes = computed(() => cpesTypes.value.map((t) => t.typeCode))
const cpesStats = computed(() => repoStats(cpesCodes.value))
```

Also add `cpesCodes` to `managedCodes` set so CPES docs don't appear in "Other Attachments":
```typescript
const managedCodes = computed(() => new Set<string>([
  ...keyCodes,
  ...codesForGroup('ECO_FORMS'),
  ...sdOrdersCodes.value,
  ...sdReportsCodes.value,
  ...sdCertsCodes.value,
  ...cpesCodes.value,  // ADD THIS
]))
```

#### AAA-D-2: Reorder `sections` computed + add CPES

Replace the current `sections` computed (line ~434) with:
```typescript
const sections = computed(() => {
  const base = []
  if (hasProject.value) base.push({ value: 'checklist', label: 'Compliance Checklist', icon: 'mdi-clipboard-check-outline' })
  base.push(
    { value: 'key',        label: 'Key Documents',       icon: 'mdi-file-star-outline' },
    { value: 'gallery',    label: 'Gallery',              icon: 'mdi-image-multiple' },
    { value: 'eco',        label: 'ECO Forms',            icon: 'mdi-form-select' },
    { value: 'supporting', label: 'Supporting Documents', icon: 'mdi-folder-multiple-outline' },
  )
  if (cpesTypes.value.length) base.push({ value: 'cpes', label: 'CPES Documents', icon: 'mdi-certificate-outline' })
  base.push({ value: 'other', label: 'Other Attachments', icon: 'mdi-paperclip' })
  if (hasProject.value) base.push({ value: 'audit', label: 'Audit Log', icon: 'mdi-history' })
  return base
})
```

#### AAA-D-3: Default `activeSection` to checklist when hasProject

Change line `const activeSection = ref('key')` to:
```typescript
const activeSection = ref('')
onMounted(() => {
  activeSection.value = hasProject.value ? 'checklist' : 'key'
})
```

After the Supporting Documents `</v-window-item>` (section 4), add:
```html
<!-- ===== Section 5: CPES Documents ===== -->
<v-window-item v-if="cpesTypes.length" value="cpes">
  <v-row dense>
    <v-col cols="12">
      <CiRepositoryCard
        title="CPES Documentary Requirements"
        icon="mdi-certificate-outline"
        color="deep-purple"
        :doc-count="cpesStats.docCount"
        :completed-count="cpesStats.completedCount"
        :total-types="cpesStats.totalTypes"
        :latest-upload="cpesStats.latestUpload"

        @open="openRepo('CPES Documentary Requirements', 'mdi-certificate-outline', 'deep-purple', cpesCodes)"
        @upload="openRepo('CPES Documentary Requirements', 'mdi-certificate-outline', 'deep-purple', cpesCodes, true)"
      />

    <v-col cols="12">
      <v-alert type="info" variant="tonal" density="compact" class="mt-2">
        CPES documentary requirements for CSUCC construction projects. Upload the required files via the repository, then track submission status in the Compliance Checklist.
```

### Sub-Phase AAA-E — Compliance Checklist Grid Dashboard

**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiDocumentChecklist.vue`
**Risk:** MEDIUM — replaces collapsible accordion with grid; functional behavior (PATCH/submit/history) is preserved unchanged
**Dependencies:** AAA-B (CPES types seeded so they appear in checklist)

#### AAA-E-1: Replace `v-expansion-panels` with responsive grid

Replace the `<v-expansion-panels v-else multiple class="mb-4">` block and all its children with a `v-row dense` grid layout:

Each group becomes a `v-col cols="12" md="6" xl="4"` containing a `v-card`:

```html
<v-row v-else dense class="mb-4">
  <v-col v-for="g in groupedItems" :key="g.groupCode" cols="12" md="6" xl="4">
    <v-card variant="outlined" class="h-100">
      <v-card-title class="d-flex align-center ga-2 pb-1 text-body-1">
        <v-icon icon="mdi-folder-outline" size="small" />
        {{ g.groupLabel }}
        <v-spacer />
        <v-chip
          size="x-small"
          variant="tonal"
          :color="g.submittedCount === g.items.length ? 'success' : (g.missingCount > 0 ? 'warning' : 'info')"
        >
          {{ g.submittedCount }}/{{ g.items.length }}
        </v-chip>
      </v-card-title>
      <v-progress-linear
        :model-value="g.items.length ? (g.submittedCount / g.items.length) * 100 : 0"
        :color="g.submittedCount === g.items.length ? 'success' : 'primary'"
        height="4"
        class="mx-4"
      />
      <v-card-text class="pt-2">
        <!-- Item list — compact, always visible (no expand needed) -->
        <v-list density="compact" class="pa-0">
          <v-list-item v-for="item in g.items" :key="item.id" lines="one" class="px-0 py-0">
            <template #prepend>
              <v-icon
                :icon="item.submissionStatus === 'NOT_SUBMITTED' ? 'mdi-circle-outline' : 'mdi-check-circle'"
                :color="statusColor(item.submissionStatus)"
                size="small"
              />
            </template>
            <v-list-item-title class="text-caption">
              {{ item.documentType?.typeLabel || 'Unknown' }}
            </v-list-item-title>
            <template #append>
              <div class="d-flex align-center ga-1">
                <v-chip :color="statusColor(item.submissionStatus)" size="x-small" variant="tonal">
                  {{ statusLabel(item.submissionStatus) }}
                </v-chip>
                <v-btn v-if="canEdit" icon="mdi-upload" size="x-small" variant="text" color="primary" @click="openSubmitDialog(item)" />
                <v-btn icon="mdi-history" size="x-small" variant="text" color="grey" @click="openHistory(item)" />
              </div>
            </template>
          </v-list-item>
        </v-list>
        <!-- Per-group remarks (preserved) -->
        <div v-if="canEditRemarks || groupRemarks?.[g.groupCode]" class="mt-2 pt-2 border-t">
          <v-textarea
            v-if="canEditRemarks"
            :model-value="groupRemarks?.[g.groupCode] ?? ''"
            placeholder="Evaluator remarks…"
            rows="1"
            auto-grow
            variant="outlined"
            density="compact"
            hide-details
            class="text-caption"
            @blur="onRemarksBlur(g.groupCode, $event)"
          />
          <p v-else class="text-caption text-grey">{{ groupRemarks?.[g.groupCode] || '—' }}</p>
        </div>
      </v-card-text>
    </v-card>
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

All existing dialogs (`submitDialog`, `historyDrawer`, `dialog` edit) are PRESERVED unchanged — just the container grid changes.

### Sub-Phase AAA-F — Key Documents Enterprise Repository Cards

**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiAttachmentHub.vue` — Section 1 template block
**Risk:** MEDIUM — replaces flat list + single upload form with card-based layout; upload logic unchanged
**Dependencies:** None (works without taxonomy changes)

#### AAA-F-1: Replace flat Key Documents section with repository cards

Replace the `<v-window-item value="key">` content block.

**3 premade cards** (one per primary key doc type):

```html
<v-window-item value="key">
  <v-alert type="info" variant="tonal" density="compact" icon="mdi-information-outline" class="mb-3">
    Governance documents required for project evaluation and compliance. Upload once — reference everywhere.
  </v-alert>

  <v-row dense>
    <!-- Card per primary key doc type -->
    <v-col v-for="kd in keyDocCardDefs" :key="kd.typeCode" cols="12" sm="6" md="4">
      <CiRepositoryCard
        :title="kd.label"
        :icon="kd.icon"
        :color="kd.color"
        :doc-count="keyDocsByType[kd.typeCode]?.length ?? 0"
        :completed-count="keyDocsByType[kd.typeCode]?.length ? 1 : 0"
        :total-types="1"
        :latest-upload="keyDocsByType[kd.typeCode]?.[0]?.createdAt ?? null"
        :can-upload="canUpload"
        @open="openRepo(kd.label, kd.icon, kd.color, [kd.typeCode])"
        @upload="openRepo(kd.label, kd.icon, kd.color, [kd.typeCode], true)"
      />
    </v-col>
    <!-- Other Key Docs (remaining key types + custom sections) -->
    <v-col cols="12" sm="6" md="4">
      <CiRepositoryCard
        title="Other Key Documents"
        icon="mdi-file-multiple-outline"
        color="grey"
        :doc-count="otherKeyDocCount"
        :completed-count="otherKeyDocCompletedCount"
        :total-types="otherKeyCodes.length"
        :latest-upload="otherKeyLatest"
        :can-upload="canUpload"
        @open="openRepo('Other Key Documents', 'mdi-file-multiple-outline', 'grey', otherKeyCodes)"
        @upload="openRepo('Other Key Documents', 'mdi-file-multiple-outline', 'grey', otherKeyCodes, true)"
      />
    </v-col>
  </v-row>
</v-window-item>
```

#### AAA-F-2: Add `keyDocCardDefs` and supporting computed refs

After `keyDocFallback` (line ~277), add:

```typescript
const keyDocCardDefs = [
  { typeCode: 'PROJECT_PROFILE',   label: 'Project Profile',          icon: 'mdi-file-account',          color: 'warning' },
  { typeCode: 'FEASIBILITY_STUDY', label: 'Feasibility Study',        icon: 'mdi-file-search-outline',   color: 'indigo' },
  { typeCode: 'HGDG_FORM',         label: 'HGDG Form',                icon: 'mdi-file-certificate',      color: 'teal' },
]
const primaryKeyTypeCodes = keyDocCardDefs.map((k) => k.typeCode)
const otherKeyCodes = computed(() => keyCodes.filter((c) => !primaryKeyTypeCodes.includes(c)))

const keyDocsByType = computed<Record<string, HubDoc[]>>(() => {
  const m: Record<string, HubDoc[]> = {}
  allDocs.value.filter((d) => keyCodes.includes(d.documentType)).forEach((d) => {
    ;(m[d.documentType] ??= []).push(d)
  })
  return m
})
const otherKeyDocCount = computed(() => allDocs.value.filter((d) => otherKeyCodes.value.includes(d.documentType)).length)
const otherKeyDocCompletedCount = computed(() => new Set(allDocs.value.filter((d) => otherKeyCodes.value.includes(d.documentType)).map((d) => d.documentType)).size)
const otherKeyLatest = computed(() => {
  const dates = allDocs.value.filter((d) => otherKeyCodes.value.includes(d.documentType)).map((d) => d.createdAt).filter(Boolean) as string[]
  return dates.sort().at(-1) ?? null
})
```

#### AAA-F-3: Custom key document containers (per-project)

**Backend:** Add `customKeySections` JSONB field to `construction_projects` entity:
```typescript
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'jsonb' })
customKeySections?: Array<{ id: string; label: string; typeCode: string }>;
```

Add migration `Migration20260527040000_AddCustomKeySections.ts`:
```sql
ALTER TABLE construction_projects ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS custom_key_sections JSONB DEFAULT '[]';
```

Add service method `updateCustomKeySections(projectId, sections, user)` and controller route:
```
PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/custom-key-sections
```

**Frontend:** `CiAttachmentHub` receives a `customKeySections` prop (or fetches from project). Add an "Add Section" button at the end of the Key Documents row that opens a dialog to enter a label. On confirm, calls the PATCH endpoint and adds a new card dynamically.

### Sub-Phase AAA-G — Gallery Repository Modal (Lightbox + Grid/List Toggle)

**Scope:** New `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiGalleryModal.vue` + hub Section 2 upgrade
**Risk:** LOW — additive; existing inline gallery remains unchanged as fallback
**Dependencies:** None

#### AAA-G-1: Create `CiGalleryModal.vue`

Full-screen dialog for gallery browsing.

**Props:**
```typescript
interface Props {
  modelValue: boolean      // v-model open state
  projectId?: string
  title?: string
  images: HubGallery[]    // passed from hub
  canUpload?: boolean
  canDelete?: boolean
  mode?: 'view' | 'edit' | 'staging'
}
```

**Emits:** `update:modelValue`, `upload`, `delete`

**Features:**
- Grid view (default): `v-row dense` with `v-col cols="6" sm="4" md="3"` image cards
- List view: `v-list` with thumbnail + caption + metadata per row
- Grid/list toggle: `v-btn-toggle` in toolbar
- Category filter: `v-select` (ALL, PROFILE, BEFORE, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, DEFECT)
- Search by caption: `v-text-field` in toolbar
- Lightbox: clicking any image opens a `v-dialog` max-width="900" with `v-img` and metadata panel
- Image metadata: category, caption, imageTakenDate, uploadedByName, createdAt
- Download button per image (`<a :href="img.imageUrl" download>`)
- Delete button per image (gated by canDelete)
- Drag-and-drop upload zone when canUpload
- Total count chip in dialog title

In the Gallery `v-card-title` line, add:
```html
<v-btn variant="text" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-folder-open-outline" @click="galleryModalOpen = true">
  Open Gallery ({{ gallery.length }})
</v-btn>
```

Add `CiGalleryModal` below the CiRepositoryModal in the hub template:
```html
<CiGalleryModal
  v-model="galleryModalOpen"
  title="Project Gallery"
  :images="gallery"
  :can-upload="canUpload"
  :can-delete="canDelete"
  :mode="mode"

/>
```

### Sub-Phase AAA-H — CiRepositoryModal Enterprise Upgrade

**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiRepositoryModal.vue`
**Risk:** MEDIUM — significant template changes; upload/delete logic unchanged
**Dependencies:** AAA-A (for recent activity section to work)

#### AAA-H-1: Add search, filter, sort to modal

Add inside the modal dialog toolbar:
```html
<div class="d-flex align-center ga-2 flex-wrap px-4 pb-2">
  <v-text-field
    v-model="modalSearch"
    prepend-inner-icon="mdi-magnify"
    placeholder="Search files…"
    variant="outlined"
    density="compact"
    hide-details
    clearable
    style="min-width:200px;flex:1 1 200px"
  />
  <v-select
    v-model="modalFilterType"
    :items="typeFilterItems"
    label="Type"
    variant="outlined"
    density="compact"
    hide-details
    clearable
    style="max-width:200px"
  />
  <v-btn-toggle v-model="modalSort" density="compact" variant="outlined">
    <v-btn value="date-desc" icon="mdi-sort-calendar-descending" size="small" />
    <v-btn value="date-asc" icon="mdi-sort-calendar-ascending" size="small" />
    <v-btn value="name-asc" icon="mdi-sort-alphabetical-ascending" size="small" />
  </v-btn-toggle>
</div>
```

Computed `filteredModalDocs`:
```typescript
const filteredModalDocs = computed(() => {
  let docs = [...props.documents]
  if (modalSearch.value) {
    const q = modalSearch.value.toLowerCase()
    docs = docs.filter(d => d.fileName.toLowerCase().includes(q) || (d.description ?? '').toLowerCase().includes(q))
  }
  if (modalFilterType.value) docs = docs.filter(d => d.documentType === modalFilterType.value)
  if (modalSort.value === 'date-asc') docs.sort((a, b) => (a.createdAt ?? '') < (b.createdAt ?? '') ? -1 : 1)
  else if (modalSort.value === 'name-asc') docs.sort((a, b) => a.fileName.localeCompare(b.fileName))
  else docs.sort((a, b) => (a.createdAt ?? '') > (b.createdAt ?? '') ? -1 : 1) // date-desc default
  return docs
})
```

#### AAA-H-2: Add drag-and-drop upload zone

In upload section of modal, add drag-and-drop zone:
```html
<div
  class="upload-zone pa-6 rounded border-dashed text-center"
  :class="{ 'upload-zone--active': isDragOver }"
  @dragover.prevent="isDragOver = true"
  @dragleave="isDragOver = false"
  @drop.prevent="handleModalDrop"
>
  <v-icon icon="mdi-cloud-upload-outline" size="32" color="grey" />
  <div class="text-body-2 text-grey mt-1">Drag files here or click to browse</div>
  <v-btn variant="tonal" size="small" class="mt-2" @click="triggerModalFileInput">Browse</v-btn>
  <input ref="modalFileInputRef" type="file" multiple style="display:none" @change="handleModalFileChange" />
</div>
```

`handleModalDrop` and `handleModalFileChange` loop over files and emit `upload` for each:
```typescript
async function handleModalFileBatch(files: File[]) {
  for (const file of files) {
    emit('upload', { file, documentType: modalUploadType.value || props.typeCodes[0] || 'other', title: file.name, description: '' })
  }
}
```

#### AAA-H-3: Add pagination (client-side "load more")

```typescript
const MODAL_PAGE_SIZE = 20
const modalDisplayLimit = ref(MODAL_PAGE_SIZE)
const pagedModalDocs = computed(() => filteredModalDocs.value.slice(0, modalDisplayLimit.value))
const hasMoreModalDocs = computed(() => filteredModalDocs.value.length > modalDisplayLimit.value)
```

Replace `v-for="doc in modalDocs"` with `v-for="doc in pagedModalDocs"`.
Add load-more button: `<v-btn v-if="hasMoreModalDocs" @click="modalDisplayLimit += MODAL_PAGE_SIZE">Load more ({{ filteredModalDocs.length - modalDisplayLimit }} remaining)</v-btn>`

Reset `modalDisplayLimit` to `MODAL_PAGE_SIZE` whenever `modalSearch` or `modalFilterType` changes.

#### AAA-H-4: Show uploader info on document rows

If `doc.uploadedByName` is present, show it in the list item subtitle:
```html
<v-list-item-subtitle class="text-caption">
  {{ typeLabel(doc.documentType) }}
  <span v-if="doc.uploadedByName"> · {{ doc.uploadedByName }}</span>
  <span v-if="doc.createdAt"> · {{ formatDate(doc.createdAt) }}</span>
</v-list-item-subtitle>
```

#### AAA-H-5: Add recent activity section (bottom of modal)

After the document list, add a collapsible "Recent Activity" section:
```html
<v-divider class="mt-4" />
<v-expansion-panels variant="accordion" class="mt-2">
  <v-expansion-panel>
    <v-expansion-panel-title class="text-caption text-medium-emphasis">
      <v-icon icon="mdi-history" size="small" class="mr-1" />Recent Activity
    </v-expansion-panel-title>
    <v-expansion-panel-text>
      <div v-if="recentActivity.length === 0" class="text-caption text-grey pa-2">No recent events.</div>
      <v-list v-else density="compact" class="pa-0">
        <v-list-item v-for="ev in recentActivity" :key="ev.id" class="px-0">
          <v-list-item-title class="text-caption">{{ ev.userName }} {{ ev.action.toLowerCase() }}</v-list-item-title>
          <v-list-item-subtitle class="text-caption text-grey">{{ formatDate(ev.createdAt) }}</v-list-item-subtitle>
        </v-list-item>
      </v-list>
    </v-expansion-panel-text>
  </v-expansion-panel>
</v-expansion-panels>
```

`recentActivity` is fetched on modal open (if `projectId` prop provided):
```typescript
async function fetchRecentActivity() {
  if (!props.projectId) return
  try {
    const res = await api.get(`/api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/${props.projectId}?pageSize=5`)
    recentActivity.value = (res?.data ?? [])
  } catch { recentActivity.value = [] }
}
watch(() => props.modelValue, (open) => { if (open) fetchRecentActivity() })
```

**Note:** CiRepositoryModal needs new props: `projectId?: string`. Hub passes `:project-id="projectId"` to the modal.

### Sub-Phase AAA-I — Audit Log Panel Upgrade

**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiAuditLogPanel.vue` + `pmo-backend/src/activity-logs/activity-log.entity.ts`
**Risk:** LOW — additive enum values; panel upgrade is additive UI change
**Dependencies:** AAA-A (audit log fix must be applied first)

#### AAA-I-1: Expand ActivityAction enum

File: `pmo-backend/src/activity-logs/activity-log.entity.ts`

Add new actions to the `ActivityAction` enum:
```typescript
DOWNLOAD = 'DOWNLOAD',
BATCH_UPLOAD = 'BATCH_UPLOAD',
REMARKS_UPDATE = 'REMARKS_UPDATE',
TEMPLATE_UPLOAD = 'TEMPLATE_UPLOAD',
```

**Note:** Enum expansion is backward-compatible — existing rows in `activity_logs` table are unaffected. TypeScript enum changes are compile-time only.

#### AAA-I-2: Upgrade `CiAuditLogPanel` from expansion panel to table view

Replace `<v-expansion-panels>` wrapper with a plain `v-card`:
```html
<v-card variant="outlined">
  <v-card-title class="d-flex align-center ga-2 text-body-1">
    <v-icon icon="mdi-history" size="small" color="grey" />
    <span>Attachment Audit Log</span>
    <v-chip v-if="total" size="x-small" variant="tonal" color="grey" class="ml-1">{{ total }} events</v-chip>
    <v-spacer />
    <!-- Filter controls -->
    <v-select
      v-model="filterAction"
      :items="actionFilterItems"
      label="Action"
      variant="outlined"
      density="compact"
      hide-details
      clearable
      style="max-width:160px"
    />
  </v-card-title>
  <v-divider />
  <v-card-text class="pa-0">
    <v-data-table
      :headers="auditHeaders"
      :items="filteredEntries"
      :loading="loading"
      density="compact"
      class="elevation-0"
      :items-per-page="10"
    >
      <template #item.action="{ item }">
        <v-chip :color="actionColor(item.action)" size="x-small" variant="tonal">
          {{ ACTION_LABELS[item.action] ?? item.action }}
        </v-chip>
      </template>
      <template #item.createdAt="{ item }">
        <span class="text-caption">{{ formatDate(item.createdAt) }}</span>
      </template>
      <template #item.metadata="{ item }">
        <span v-if="item.metadata?.fileName" class="text-caption">{{ item.metadata.fileName }}</span>
        <span v-else class="text-caption text-grey">—</span>
      </template>
    </v-data-table>
  </v-card-text>
</v-card>
```

`auditHeaders`:
```typescript
const auditHeaders = [
  { title: 'User', key: 'userName', width: 160 },
  { title: 'Action', key: 'action', width: 120 },
  { title: 'File', key: 'metadata', width: 200 },
  { title: 'Section', key: 'metadata.section', width: 120 },
  { title: 'Date', key: 'createdAt', width: 160 },
]
```

`filteredEntries = computed(() => filterAction.value ? entries.value.filter(e => e.action === filterAction.value) : entries.value)`

---

### Phase AAA Execution Order

1. **AAA-A** — Audit Log 404 fix (prerequisite for activity panel + modal recent activity)
2. **AAA-B** — CPES migration (prerequisite for AAA-D CPES section to show data)
3. **AAA-C** — Uploader name JOIN (backend + HubDoc interface update)
4. **AAA-D** — Hub reorder + CPES section (requires AAA-B)
5. **AAA-E** — Checklist grid dashboard refactor
6. **AAA-F** — Key Documents enterprise cards + custom containers
7. **AAA-G** — Gallery repository modal
8. **AAA-H** — CiRepositoryModal enterprise upgrade (requires AAA-A for recent activity)
9. **AAA-I** — Audit Log panel upgrade (requires AAA-A)

AAA-C, AAA-E, AAA-F, AAA-G can run in parallel after AAA-A and AAA-B are complete.

---

### Phase AAA Verification Checklist

| ID | Test | Status |
|----|------|--------|
| AAA-A-V1 | `GET /api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/{id}?pageSize=20` returns 200 with `{ data: [], total: 0, page: 1, pageSize: 20 }` | ✅ code |
| AAA-B-V1 | `GET /api/construction-projects/document-types/grouped` returns a group with `groupCode: "CPES_DOCS"` containing 22 types | ✅ code |
| AAA-B-V2 | `CiDocumentChecklist` shows a "CPES Documentary Requirements" group card after migration | ✅ code |
| AAA-C-V1 | Document list response includes `uploadedByName` field; hub renders "Uploaded by [name]" subtitle on document list items | ✅ code |
| AAA-D-V1 | Checklist is the first tab in the hub when viewing an existing project | ✅ code |
| AAA-D-V2 | "CPES Documents" section tab appears in hub with correct doc count | ✅ code |
| AAA-D-V3 | In staging mode (new.vue), first tab is Key Documents (not Checklist) | ✅ code |
| AAA-E-V1 | Checklist renders as responsive grid cards — no expansion panels | ✅ code |
| AAA-E-V2 | Each group card shows progress bar, submitted/missing chips, item list | ✅ code |
| AAA-E-V3 | Submit/History/Review dialogs all function identically to previous implementation | ✅ code |
| AAA-F-V1 | Key Documents section shows 3 premade cards + "Other Key Documents" card | ✅ code |
| AAA-F-V2 | Each premade card opens modal showing only that type's documents | ✅ code |
| AAA-F-V3 | "Add Section" button opens dialog; entering a label creates a new card visible immediately | ✅ code |
| AAA-G-V1 | "Open Gallery" button in hub Section 2 opens CiGalleryModal fullscreen | ✅ code |
| AAA-G-V2 | Grid/list toggle works; all images visible in both views | ✅ code |
